description: Runs one crawl with cache, robots, retries, and parsing when you need one URL.
heuristics: Defaults cache_mode to ENABLED when config.cache_mode is None.
Checks robots.txt before any fetch when check_robots_txt is enabled.
Retries blocked fetches across proxy candidates and max_retries before optional fallback_fetch_function.

description: Crawls many URLs with a dispatcher and can stream results for concurrent batches.
heuristics: Falls back to MemoryAdaptiveDispatcher with RateLimiter(base_delay=(mean_delay, mean_delay+max_range)).
Bypasses the dispatcher entirely when deep_crawl_strategy is set on the primary config.
Auto-releases proxy_session_id after batch or stream when proxy_session_auto_release is true.

description: Fetches pages over aiohttp when you want low-memory HTTP crawling without a browser.
heuristics: Uses aiohttp TCPConnector(limit=min(32, cpu_count*4), ttl_dns_cache=300).
Treats any non-text/html response or attachment Content-Disposition as a file download.
Decodes text content with response.charset or chardet, and saves downloads under downloads_path.

description: Loads file:// or raw: HTML directly, or uses a browser when interactions are needed.
heuristics: Fast path returns HTML directly for file:// and raw: when no browser-only features are requested.
Browser processing turns on for local/raw sources when js_code, wait_for, screenshot, pdf, or capture flags are set.
Sets redirected_url to config.base_url for raw:/file:// inputs instead of raw content or about:blank.

description: Downloads or opens a PDF and turns pages into HTML, markdown, images, and links.
heuristics: Downloads remote PDFs with requests.get(stream=True, timeout=(20, 600)).
Processes pages in parallel batches with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=batch_size).
Wraps each page as <div class="pdf-page" data-page="N"> and tags extracted links/images with page numbers.

description: Creates or reuses a browser context with UA, proxy, geo, and blocking settings.
heuristics: Context signatures hash proxy_config, locale, timezone_id, geolocation, navigator flags, and _browser_version.
Injects proxy credentials at context level via ProxySettings instead of CLI proxy auth.
Blocks CSS, static assets, or ad/tracker routes when avoid_css, text_mode, or avoid_ads is enabled.

description: Reuses a named page session across crawls when state must persist on one tab.
heuristics: Reuses sessions[session_id] until the session TTL expires at 1800 seconds.
Calls window.stop() before reusing a session page to abort pending loads from prior navigations.
Stores session_id back onto CrawlResult for both live and cached results.

description: Uses persistent browser profiles when login or identity state must survive runs.
heuristics: launch_persistent_context uses user_data_dir and supports full proxy auth via proxy={server,username,password}.
BrowserProfiler stores profiles under ~/.crawl4ai/profiles.
AGGRESSIVE profile shrinking keeps Network, Cookies, Local Storage, IndexedDB, Preferences, and storage_state.json.

description: Waits for CSS or JS readiness conditions before capture on dynamic pages.
heuristics: smart_wait accepts explicit js: and css: prefixes, then falls back from CSS to JS auto-detection.
csp_compliant_wait polls the user function every 100ms until timeout.
wait_for uses wait_for_timeout when set, otherwise page_timeout.

description: Runs pre-wait or post-wait JavaScript when custom page actions are required.
heuristics: js_code_before_wait runs before wait_for so scripts can trigger the state being awaited.
robust_execute_user_script catches 'Execution context was destroyed' and waits for the next load.
Runs string scripts as a one-item list and keeps going even when one chunk returns an error object.

description: Modifies the live DOM before capture when iframes, shadow roots, or popups block data.
heuristics: flatten_shadow_dom injects attachShadow(...mode:'open') and serializes shadow content before capture.
process_iframes replaces each iframe with a div containing the frame body HTML.
remove_consent_popups runs before remove_overlay_elements.

description: Captures screenshots, PDFs, or MHTML when a crawl needs visual or archive artifacts.
heuristics: Exports PDF with page.pdf(print_background=True).
capture_mhtml waits for domcontentloaded, networkidle, a 1s delay, and two requestAnimationFrame ticks.
take_screenshot uses full-page stitching only when scrolling is needed and viewport-only is not forced.

description: Records network and console activity during a crawl for runtime diagnostics.
heuristics: capture_network_requests logs request, response, and requestfailed events with timestamps.
capture_console_messages uses adapter-specific console and page error capture handlers.
Event listeners are always removed in finally to avoid accumulating handlers across page reuse.

description: Selects rotating or sticky proxies before fetch attempts when blocks are likely.
heuristics: Sticky sessions call get_proxy_for_session(proxy_session_id, ttl=proxy_session_ttl).
Non-sticky rotation calls get_next_proxy() before the crawl.
Per-attempt anti-bot retries still iterate through config._get_proxy_list(), including direct access when configured.

description: Flags anti-bot responses from status, patterns, and broken pages when fetches look blocked.
heuristics: HTTP 429 is always treated as blocked.
HTTP 403/503 with non-data HTML is treated as blocked even on large SPA shells.
Structural fallback marks pages blocked when they lack <body> or have minimal text plus no content elements.

description: Uses a last-resort fetcher after retries fail when the browser path stays blocked.
heuristics: fallback_fetch_function runs only after all proxy attempts fail and never for raw: URLs.
Fallback HTML is reprocessed through aprocess_html with is_raw_html=True when possible.
If processing fallback HTML fails, it returns a minimal CrawlResult with raw HTML and status_code 200.

description: Cleans extracted DOM content by dropping junk nodes and noisy attributes.
heuristics: remove_empty_elements_fast drops leaf nodes below the word threshold except tags like a, img, td, and th.
remove_unwanted_attributes_fast keeps only IMPORTANT_ATTRS and optional data-* attributes.
The scraper can remove forms, comments, excluded_tags, and excluded_selector matches before returning cleaned_html.

description: Extracts scored links, media variants, and page metadata from cleaned DOM output.
heuristics: normalize_url preserves HTTPS for internal links when preserve_https_for_internal_links is enabled.
Link scoring uses title, rel, path keywords, depth, HTTPS, and page-context term overlap.
Images are kept only when their heuristic score beats image_score_threshold and may include srcset/picture variants.

description: Converts cleaned or raw HTML into markdown with citations for text-first output.
heuristics: Default html2text options set body_width=0, single_line_break=True, and mark_code=True.
Relative links are resolved against base_url or a <base href> found in the raw HTML.
Unique markdown links become numbered citations with a generated References section.

description: Filters page sections by BM25 or pruning when only the most relevant blocks matter.
heuristics: BM25ContentFilter stems tokens, weights tags like h1=5.0 and h2=4.0, then drops chunks below bm25_threshold.
PruningContentFilter removes comments and excluded tags before scoring nodes.
Dynamic pruning adjusts thresholds using tag importance, text ratio, and link ratio.

description: Uses an LLM to turn page chunks into structured blocks when rules are not enough.
heuristics: Merges sections by chunk_token_threshold with overlap=int(chunk_token_threshold*overlap_rate).
Groq providers run sequentially with a 0.5s delay; others use up to 4 worker threads.
When force_json_response is on, dict results become one-item lists unless the lone value is already a list.

description: Extracts structured items with CSS, XPath, or lxml selectors when layout is stable.
heuristics: JsonCssExtractionStrategy and JsonXPathExtractionStrategy force input_format='html'.
Field pipelines can apply text, attribute, html, and regex steps after selector lookup.
Computed fields are evaluated through AST-checked expressions that block imports and private attributes.

description: Pulls entities with regex patterns when the target lives in text, not stable DOM nodes.
heuristics: Built-in IntFlag patterns cover email, phone, URL, dates, money, hashtags, cards, and more.
Compiles only the requested built-ins plus any custom overrides into self._compiled.
Each match returns url, label, value, and exact [start, end] span offsets.

description: Clusters related text chunks into topical sections before downstream extraction.
heuristics: Falls back from DEL splitting to double-newline splitting when no delimiter is present.
Pre-filters chunks by cosine similarity to semantic_filter and keeps extra top matches when too few pass.
Forms flat clusters with hierarchical linkage and drops clusters below word_count_threshold.

description: Turns data tables into headers and aligned rows when pages contain tabular content.
heuristics: Only extracts tables whose data-table score meets table_score_threshold, default 7.
Adds points for thead/th/caption/summary and subtracts 3 for nested tables or role=presentation/none.
Expands colspan values and pads rows to the detected max column count.

description: Fetches and merges link head previews when discovered URLs need quick metadata.
heuristics: Filters internal and/or external links with fnmatch include_patterns and exclude_patterns.
Deduplicates URLs while preserving order and caps the list at max_links.
Uses AsyncUrlSeeder head extraction with BM25 scoring only when link_preview_config.query is set.

description: Stores crawl results in SQLite plus hashed files for fast result reuse.
heuristics: Writes html, cleaned_html, markdown, extracted_content, and screenshot to filesystem by xxhash hash.
Keeps row metadata, media, links, response_headers, and downloaded_files in SQLite.
Upserts cache rows on url conflict instead of inserting duplicates.

description: Validates cached pages with HTTP validators and head fingerprints before reuse.
heuristics: Sends HEAD with If-None-Match and If-Modified-Since when stored validators exist.
Marks cache fresh immediately on HTTP 304.
Falls back to hashing up to 64KB of streamed <head> content when validators are missing or changed.

description: Schedules batch crawls around memory pressure for safer high-volume concurrency.
heuristics: Enters memory_pressure_mode above memory_threshold_percent and exits only below recovery_threshold_percent.
Requeues tasks when current_memory_percent reaches critical_threshold_percent.
Uses a priority queue that boosts long-waiting URLs after fairness_timeout.

description: Applies per-domain delays and backoff when sites start rate limiting batches.
heuristics: Initial delay is random within base_delay when a domain has no current delay.
429 and 503 double the domain delay with jitter until max_delay and max_retries are hit.
Successful responses shrink current_delay to 75% or the base range and reset fail_count.

description: Tracks task status, memory, waits, and dispatch stats during batch crawling.
heuristics: Each task gets a UUID task_id and a DispatchResult with memory_usage, peak_memory, start_time, and end_time.
Monitor updates status through QUEUED, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, or FAILED.
Streaming mode yields finished tasks and excludes placeholder requeued results from completed_count.

description: Explores links level by level when shallow coverage matters before deeper hops.
heuristics: Stores current_level as (url, parent_url) tuples and annotates result metadata with depth and parent_url.
Skips discovery once next_depth exceeds max_depth or max_pages capacity is exhausted.
Only discovers links from successful crawls and can resume from visited/pending state.

description: Explores one branch at a time when deeper paths matter before sibling pages.
heuristics: Maintains a stack of (url, parent_url, depth) tuples instead of levels.
Uses a separate _dfs_seen set to dedupe discovered URLs before they are crawled.
Pushes new links in reverse order so the first discovered child is processed next.

description: Crawls highest-scoring URLs first when priority-driven exploration is needed.
heuristics: Uses an asyncio.PriorityQueue of (-score, depth, url, parent_url) items.
Seeds the queue with the start URL score from url_scorer when present.
Processes queued URLs in batches of up to BATCH_SIZE=10.

description: Rejects invalid or unwanted deep-crawl URLs before they reach the frontier.
heuristics: Deep crawl only accepts http/https URLs with a netloc containing a dot.
FilterChain short-circuits on the first synchronous rejection and gathers async filter checks together.
URLPatternFilter fast-paths simple suffix, prefix, and domain matches before regex fallback.

description: Selects, orders, and caches backend engines for a search category.
heuristics: backend strings are split on commas; list backends are joined for deprecated compatibility.
"auto"/"all" uses all engines, but text prepends wikipedia and grokipedia ahead of shuffled others.
Engine instances are cached per DDGS instance and sorted by (priority, random) descending.

description: Fetches remote content with browser impersonation and DDGS-specific error handling.
heuristics: HttpClient wraps primp.Client with impersonate="random" and impersonate_os="random".
BaseSearchEngine.request returns response text only for HTTP 200 and otherwise returns None.
primp.TimeoutError becomes TimeoutException; other exceptions become DDGSException with type and repr.

description: Normalizes result fields on assignment so engines can write raw values.
heuristics: BaseResult.__setattr__ normalizes title, body, href, url, thumbnail, image, date, author, publisher, and info.
_normalize_text strips HTML tags, unescapes entities, NFC-normalizes, removes Unicode C chars, and collapses spaces.
_normalize_url unquotes URLs and replaces spaces with '+', while _normalize_date ISO-serializes int timestamps.

description: Deduplicates cross-engine hits and keeps the richest copy before ranking.
heuristics: DDGS._search constructs ResultsAggregator with cache fields {"href", "image", "url", "embed_url"}.
ResultsAggregator.append replaces a cached item when the new item's body is longer.
extract_dicts returns cached items ordered by descending hit frequency from Counter.most_common().

description: Ranks results by Wikipedia priority and query-token matches in titles and bodies.
heuristics: Query tokens come from splitting on non-word chars and dropping tokens shorter than 3 characters.
Docs are bucketed into wikipedia hits, both title+body, title-only, body-only, and neither.
Items titled with both "Category:" and "Wikimedia" are skipped entirely.

description: Resolves proxy aliases and TLS verify options before HTTP requests.
heuristics: _expand_proxy_tb_alias maps "tb" to "socks5h://127.0.0.1:9150".
HttpClient passes verify=True when verify is bool, but uses a string verify value as ca_cert_file.
CLI api propagates the resolved proxy into child-process env and the foreground process env.

description: Runs text metasearch and returns ranked top results from available text engines.
heuristics: DDGS.text is a thin wrapper over _search("text", query, **kwargs).
In _search, the deprecated keywords argument overrides query before validation.
Empty queries raise DDGSException("query is mandatory.").

description: Queries Wikipedia OpenSearch, then hydrates the best hit with article extract text.
heuristics: Wikipedia.build_payload splits region like "us-en" and uses the lang part in the API hostname.
The first request uses action=opensearch with profile=fuzzy and limit=1.
extract_results makes a second query for extracts and drops pages whose body contains "may refer to:".

description: Queries Grokipedia typeahead and converts its first match into a text result.
heuristics: build_payload sends {"query": query, "limit": "1"} to /api/typeahead.
Titles have leading/trailing underscores stripped with .strip("_").
If the snippet contains a blank line, only the text after the first double newline is kept as body.

description: Bootstraps DuckDuckGo's vqd token before image, news, or video API calls.
heuristics: DuckDuckGo image/news/video engines GET https://duckduckgo.com with q=query to obtain vqd.
_extract_vqd scans for byte patterns vqd= with double quotes, ampersand, or single quotes.
If no marker matches, _extract_vqd raises DDGSException mentioning the query.

description: Searches DuckDuckGo images with vqd, safesearch, and optional media filters.
heuristics: Payload always includes o=json, q, l=region, vqd, p=safesearch, and ct=AT.
timelimit, size, color, type_image, layout, and license_image are packed into one comma-joined f field.
Page offsets use s=(page-1)*100 for page > 1.

description: Searches DuckDuckGo videos with vqd and optional publish, duration, and license filters.
heuristics: Payload f combines publishedAfter timelimit, videoDefinition, videoDuration, and videoLicense.
Safesearch maps on->1, moderate->-1, off->-2 in the p field.
Page offsets use s=(page-1)*60 for page > 1.

description: Runs news metasearch across Bing, DuckDuckGo, and Yahoo-style backends.
heuristics: DDGS.news delegates to _search("news", query, **kwargs).
CLI and REST expose news backends auto/all/bing/duckduckgo/yahoo, matching the engine registry.
Backend payloads honor timelimit with provider-specific params such as df, qft, or btf.

description: Normalizes provider news dates into UTC ISO strings when formats are recognizable.
heuristics: bing_news.extract_date tries %d.%m.%Y, %m/%d/%Y, and %d/%m/%Y before checking relative-day regexes.
yahoo_news.extract_date converts relative minute/hour/day/week/month/year phrases into UTC ISO timestamps.
Both helpers return the original date string unchanged when parsing fails.

description: Searches Anna's Archive books and fixes its commented HTML and relative links.
heuristics: AnnasArchive picks a random TLD from gd, gl, or pk when building search_url.
pre_process_html removes <!-- and --> before XPath extraction.
post_extract_results prefixes each relative result.url with the search_url base domain.

description: Fetches a URL once and returns markdown, plain text, rich text, HTML, or bytes.
heuristics: DDGS.extract GETs the target URL through HttpClient with the DDGS proxy, timeout, and verify settings.
Non-200 responses raise DDGSException("Failed to fetch ...: HTTP ...").
Unknown fmt values fall back to resp.text_markdown via content_map.get(fmt, resp.text_markdown).

description: Prints results as colorized terminal records and pauses between items on TTYs.
heuristics: _print_data forces no_color=True when stdout is not a TTY.
Fields content, href, image, source, thumbnail, and url wrap at width 300; others wrap at 78.
Interactive TTY output calls input() after each result block.

description: Saves CLI results to CSV or pretty JSON with inferred filenames.
heuristics: _save_data uses the provided extension or defaults to {function}_{query}_{UTCtimestamp}.
_save_json writes UTF-8 JSON with ensure_ascii=False and indent=2.
_save_csv uses the first row's keys as headers and csv.QUOTE_MINIMAL.

description: Downloads result targets concurrently into a timestamped directory.
heuristics: _download_results defaults the output directory to {function}_{query}_{UTCtimestamp} and creates it.
It downloads res["image"] for image searches and res["href"] for everything else.
Filenames come from the URL tail, are unquoted, prefixed with the result index, and truncated to 200 chars.

description: Serves search over FastAPI GET and POST endpoints without blocking the event loop.
heuristics: api.py defines GET and POST handlers for /search/text, /search/images, /search/news, /search/videos, and /search/books.
Each handler runs DDGS work inside asyncio.to_thread(lambda: DDGS(...).method(...)).
Failures are logged and re-raised as HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="... failed: ...").

description: Serves URL extraction over FastAPI GET and POST endpoints.
heuristics: POST /extract accepts ExtractRequest with url and format; GET /extract takes url and fmt query args.
Both handlers call DDGS.extract inside asyncio.to_thread.
Exceptions become HTTP 500 responses with detail starting "Extraction failed:".

description: Exposes DDGS search and extract operations as FastMCP tools.
heuristics: mcp.py creates FastMCP("ddgs-search").
@mcp.tool() wraps search_text, search_images, search_news, search_videos, search_books, and extract_content.
Each MCP tool calls DDGS in asyncio.to_thread and returns plain lists or dicts.

description: Build the shared researcher prompt with current date, rigor rules, and speculation guidance.
heuristics: `systemPrompt()` injects `new Date().toISOString()` into the prompt as the current date.
It tells the model to assume the user is right when given post-cutoff news.
It allows speculation or prediction, but explicitly says to flag it.

description: Choose the active structured-output model from custom, Fireworks R1, or OpenAI o3-mini.
heuristics: If `CUSTOM_MODEL` is set, it is created through the OpenAI-compatible provider with `structuredOutputs: true`.
Otherwise `getModel()` prefers Fireworks DeepSeek R1, then falls back to OpenAI `o3-mini` with `reasoningEffort: 'medium'`.
If no provider is configured, `getModel()` throws `No model found`.

description: Turn the user prompt into distinct SERP queries with explicit research goals.
heuristics: `generateSerpQueries` uses a schema requiring `{ query, researchGoal }` for each generated search.
The prompt enforces unique queries and caps output to `numQueries`, then the code slices again to that limit.
When prior learnings exist, they are appended so later queries become more specific.

description: Run Firecrawl web searches for each SERP query with markdown scraping enabled.
heuristics: Each branch calls `firecrawl.search(serpQuery.query, { timeout: 15000, limit: 5, scrapeOptions: { formats: ['markdown'] } })`.
Search branches are executed inside `Promise.all` and wrapped by `p-limit`.
Timeout and non-timeout search errors are caught per branch so the overall run continues.

description: Pull markdown bodies and source URLs from Firecrawl search results for synthesis.
heuristics: Result processing reads `result.data.map(item => item.markdown)` and removes falsy entries with `compact()`.
Each markdown snippet is trimmed with `trimPrompt(content, 25_000)` before synthesis.
Visited sources come from `result.data.map(item => item.url)` and are accumulated for final output.

description: Shrink oversized prompts to fit model context limits before LLM calls.
heuristics: `trimPrompt` measures token length with `o200k_base` and compares it to `CONTEXT_SIZE` or `128000`.
It estimates a shorter character cutoff with `overflowTokens * 3`, but never goes below `MinChunkSize` 140.
If splitting does not shorten the text, it hard-cuts and recursively retries until the prompt fits.

description: Recursively split long text on semantic separators so trimming keeps coherent chunks.
heuristics:
Separator priority is `

`, `
`, `.`, `,`, `>`, `<`, space, then character-level split.
The splitter throws if `chunkOverlap >= chunkSize`.
Oversized segments are recursively re-split and smaller ones are merged with overlap-aware windows.

description: Extract dense learnings, including dates and metrics, from scraped search markdown.
heuristics: `processSerpResult` asks for concise, information-dense, unique learnings from `<content>` blocks.
Its prompt explicitly requires entities plus exact metrics, numbers, or dates in the learnings.
The synthesis LLM call runs with `AbortSignal.timeout(60_000)` and returns schema-checked arrays.

description: Propose next research questions from current results to steer deeper recursion.
heuristics: `processSerpResult` returns `followUpQuestions` alongside learnings in the same structured call.
The default follow-up budget is 3, but recursive callers pass `numFollowUpQuestions: newBreadth`.
Questions are generated from the SERP markdown contents for the current query, not from the original prompt alone.

description: Assemble the next recursive prompt from prior goals and fresh follow-up directions.
heuristics: The recursive prompt starts with `Previous research goal: ${serpQuery.researchGoal}`.
It appends every follow-up question under `Follow-up research directions:` with newline joins.
The composed query is `.trim()`med before being passed back into `deepResearch`.

description: Limit parallel search branches to the configured Firecrawl concurrency budget.
heuristics: Concurrency is controlled by `FIRECRAWL_CONCURRENCY` and defaults to `2`.
Every SERP branch is wrapped with `pLimit(ConcurrencyLimit)` before `Promise.all`.
A failed branch returns empty `learnings` and `visitedUrls` instead of cancelling sibling work.

description: Re-enter the research loop with halved breadth, lower depth, and accumulated state.
heuristics: Each branch computes `newBreadth = Math.ceil(breadth / 2)` and `newDepth = depth - 1`.
Recursion only continues when `newDepth > 0`; otherwise the branch returns accumulated state.
Recursive calls carry forward `learnings`, `visitedUrls`, and the `onProgress` callback.

description: Combine branch learnings and visited URLs into shared recursive research state.
heuristics: Within each branch, `allLearnings` is built as prior learnings plus newly synthesized learnings.
Within each branch, `allUrls` is built as prior visited URLs plus URLs from the current search.
After `Promise.all`, branch outputs are flattened with `results.flatMap(...)` into one result object.

description: Remove duplicate learnings and URLs after parallel branches finish.
heuristics: Final learnings are deduplicated with `[...new Set(results.flatMap(r => r.learnings))]`.
Final URLs are deduplicated with `[...new Set(results.flatMap(r => r.visitedUrls))]`.
Deduping happens only at the end of a `deepResearch` call, after all branches resolve.

description: Emit structured progress updates as depth, breadth, and query counters change.
heuristics: `ResearchProgress` tracks depth, breadth, query counts, and the current query string.
`reportProgress` mutates a shared `progress` object via `Object.assign` and then calls `onProgress?.(progress)`.
Progress is updated after query generation and again on deeper or terminal branch completion.

description: Write a long markdown report from all learnings and append a source list.
heuristics: Every learning is wrapped in `<learning>` tags before being inserted into the report prompt.
The report prompt asks for a detailed markdown report of 3 or more pages and to include all learnings.
After model generation, the code appends a `## Sources` section listing every visited URL.

description: Write the exact short answer using all accumulated learnings and the user's format.
heuristics: The prompt tells the model to output only the answer and keep it to a few words or at most a sentence.
It explicitly says to follow the user's requested format, including LaTeX or fixed answer choices.
The schema returns a single `exactAnswer` string, which is written directly to `answer.md` in CLI mode.

description: Gather query, breadth, depth, mode, and optional clarifications from the terminal.
heuristics: The CLI prompts for query, breadth, depth, and `report/answer`, defaulting breadth to `4` and depth to `2`.
Any mode other than the literal string `answer` is treated as report mode.
In report mode, it asks generated follow-up questions and injects the replies into `combinedQuery`.

description: Builds the active query from request body or the last chat message when no explicit query is sent.
heuristics: The route prefers body.query when present.
If no query is passed, it joins text parts from the last message with spaces.
If parts are missing, it falls back to lastMessage.content for v4-style payloads.

description: Rejects empty search requests before any provider calls are made.
heuristics: The route defaults messages to an empty array before extraction.
If the derived query is falsy, it returns status 400 with error 'Query is required'.

description: Resolves provider keys from request overrides and server env vars before search starts.
heuristics: Firecrawl uses body.firecrawlApiKey first, then process.env.FIRECRAWL_API_KEY.
Groq is read only from process.env.GROQ_API_KEY.
Missing Firecrawl or Groq keys short-circuit with status 500 JSON errors.

description: Creates the AI SDK chat transport that posts chat turns to the Fireplexity search route.
heuristics: useChat is initialized with DefaultChatTransport.
The transport posts to /api/fireplexity/search.
The request body includes firecrawlApiKey only when the local key string is non-empty.

description: Blocks sendMessage until a Firecrawl key is available and opens the key modal instead.
heuristics: Both handleSearch and handleChatSubmit return early when hasApiKey is false.
Blocked submits store the current input in pendingQuery and set showApiKeyModal to true.

description: Saves a user-supplied Firecrawl key locally so later searches can reuse it.
heuristics: handleApiKeySubmit writes the key to localStorage under firecrawl-api-key.
Startup logic reads the same localStorage key only when the server env check reports no Firecrawl key.

description: Resumes a queued search once the user saves a Firecrawl key.
heuristics: After saving a key, the client sets hasApiKey true and closes the modal.
If pendingQuery is set, it immediately calls sendMessage with that text and then clears pendingQuery.

description: Treats later turns as follow-ups so the model gets prior chat context.
heuristics: The route uses messages.length > 2 as the follow-up test.
Follow-up turns still run a fresh Firecrawl search and only change how prior chat history is added to prompting.

description: Runs one Firecrawl search over web, news, and images for each query.
heuristics: The route POSTs to https://api.firecrawl.dev/v2/search.
It always requests sources ['web','news','images'] with limit 6.
scrapeOptions require markdown, onlyMainContent true, and maxAge 86400000.

description: Normalizes Firecrawl web hits into the source card shape used by the UI and prompts.
heuristics: Web titles fall back to the URL when item.title is missing.
Descriptions prefer item.description, then item.snippet.
siteName is derived from new URL(item.url).hostname and items without URLs are filtered out.

description: Normalizes news hits for the side rail and stored per-turn source payloads.
heuristics: publishedDate is copied from the API date field.
source prefers item.source and otherwise falls back to the item's hostname.
News thumbnails map from imageUrl and URL-less items are dropped.

description: Normalizes image hits and drops incomplete entries before the gallery renders.
heuristics: Entries are discarded when url or imageUrl is missing.
thumbnail is always taken from imageUrl.
The transformed object preserves imageWidth, imageHeight, and position before a final Boolean filter.

description: Streams transient progress messages so the UI can show live search state.
heuristics: The route writes three transient data-status parts with ids status-1 to status-3.
The messages are 'Starting search...', 'Searching for relevant sources...', and 'Analyzing sources and generating answer...'.

description: Streams sources, news, and images as persistent data parts before the answer arrives.
heuristics: The route writes one persistent data-sources part containing sources, newsResults, and imageResults.
It waits 300ms after sending sources so source UI can render before answer generation proceeds.

description: Accepts only safe-looking image URLs before thumbnails or favicons are rendered.
heuristics: Undefined URLs are rejected immediately.
URLs containing lookaside.instagram.com, google_widget/crawler, or /seo/ are blocked.
Only http and https protocols pass validation.

description: Scores source paragraphs against query keywords to keep only the most relevant context.
heuristics: The selector always keeps the first two paragraphs as the intro and the last paragraph as the conclusion.
Keywords must be longer than 3 characters and skip common question words like what, when, where, and how.
Middle paragraphs are scored by keyword hit count and only the top three are kept.

description: Builds the LLM context block from ranked source snippets and numbered citations.
heuristics: Each source is formatted as [n] title, then URL, then selected content.
The route prefers source.markdown over source.content when building context.
Individual snippets are capped via selectRelevantContent(content, query, 2000) and joined with --- separators.

description: Converts prior UI messages into model messages for follow-up turns.
heuristics: History conversion only runs on the follow-up branch.
The route calls convertToModelMessages(messages.slice(0, -1)) so the current user turn is rebuilt with fresh search context.

description: Prompts Groq to answer naturally while citing sources inline as numbered references.
heuristics: The system prompt forbids LaTeX-style numbers for plain numeric text.
The prompt requires inline citations in [1] form and explicitly bans CITATION_1-style output.
The answer model is moonshotai/kimi-k2-instruct with temperature 0.7 and maxRetries 2.

description: Streams model tokens directly into the UI message while the answer is still generating.
heuristics: Answer generation uses streamText rather than a buffered completion.
The writer merges result.toUIMessageStream() into the UI stream.
The route later awaits result.text so the fully streamed answer can seed follow-up generation.

description: Generates up to five useful next questions after the main answer finishes.
heuristics: A separate generateText call asks for five natural questions and says to skip simple greetings or acknowledgments.
On follow-up turns, the prompt tells the model to consider full conversation history and avoid repeating prior questions.
The user prompt includes the first 500 characters of the answer plus available source titles.

description: Streams parsed follow-up questions back to the client as structured suggestion data.
heuristics: The response text is split by newline, trimmed, empties removed, and sliced to 5 items.
The final list is sent as a data-followup part with a questions array.

description: Detects stock intent and resolves company names or ticker mentions to exchange-prefixed symbols.
heuristics: Ticker detection returns null unless market keywords or market regex patterns are present.
It recognizes direct patterns like $AAPL, AAPL stock, and NASDAQ:AAPL.
Company-name matching sorts the map by longest names first to reduce partial matches.

description: Streams a detected ticker so the client can render finance UI alongside the answer.
heuristics: If detectCompanyTicker(query) returns a symbol, the route writes a data-ticker part.
The ticker payload shape is { symbol } and it is emitted before source context assembly completes.

description: Checks whether a ticker looks like EXCHANGE:SYMBOL before chart rendering.
heuristics: The validator only accepts exchange prefixes like NYSE, NASDAQ, AMEX, XETR, HKEX, LSE, TSE, ASX, NSE, and BSE.
The symbol portion is limited to 1 to 5 uppercase alphanumeric or dot characters.
The widget still renders even when validation fails, so validation is advisory.

description: Renders a TradingView chart when a current or stored assistant turn has a ticker.
heuristics: Past turns read their ticker from messageData for the matching assistant index.
The current chart only appears when currentTicker exists and the latest visible pair is user then assistant.
The chart receives the detected light or dark theme string.

description: Loads the TradingView embed client-side only to avoid SSR issues.
heuristics: StockChart imports the widget with next/dynamic and ssr set to false.
The loading fallback is a fixed 300px bordered placeholder.
The embed script uses interval D, timezone Etc/UTC, and allow_symbol_change true.

description: Submits a first search from the hero input and enters chat mode.
heuristics: handleSearch ignores blank trimmed input.
Once allowed, it sets hasSearched to true before sendMessage({ text: input }).
The hero input is cleared immediately after the send.

description: Submits in-chat questions while preserving the previous assistant turn's source state.
heuristics: handleChatSubmit snapshots sources, news, images, follow-ups, and ticker under the last assistant index before sending.
It ignores blank trimmed input and clears the chat input after sendMessage.

description: Extracts plain text from AI SDK UI messages by concatenating only text parts.
heuristics: getMessageContent returns an empty string when a message has no parts.
It filters parts where type equals text and joins their text values into one string.

description: Replays all parts on each stream update to rebuild the latest structured message state.
heuristics: The effect skips processing when the last message parts length matches lastDataLength.current.
Each update rescans the whole last message to capture the latest sources, ticker, follow-ups, and status.
State is updated from the last seen data part of each type rather than appending partial deltas.

description: Clears live search UI when a new assistant response starts streaming.
heuristics: When status becomes streaming, the client compares assistant message count to currentMessageIndex.
If the count changed, it clears status, sources, news, images, follow-ups, and currentTicker.
The same branch resets lastDataLength to zero for the new turn.

description: Stores sources and finance metadata per assistant turn so older answers keep their context.
heuristics: messageData is a Map keyed by assistant index.
Streaming updates merge new source or ticker data into any existing entry for the current index.
Before a new follow-up is sent, the current visible turn is snapshotted into the map.

description: Keeps the newest search state in view as messages and source sections appear.
heuristics: A useEffect runs when messages, sources, or followUpQuestions change.
Scrolling waits 100ms, then calls scrollTo with top equal to scrollHeight and behavior smooth.
Form submission also triggers a delayed smooth scroll to bottom.

description: Replays the last user question to regenerate the current answer.
heuristics: handleRewrite finds the most recent user message by reversing messages.
It repopulates the input with that message's extracted text.
It submits the form after a 100ms delay via formRef.current?.requestSubmit().

description: Copies assistant text to the clipboard and shows a short copied state.
heuristics: Copy uses navigator.clipboard.writeText(content).
The UI stores copiedMessageId and clears it after 2000ms.
Both historical answers and the current answer use the same copy flow.

description: Turns a suggested follow-up into a submitted query with one click.
heuristics: Clicking a related question sets the input to that suggestion text.
The form is auto-submitted after 50ms so the input state lands first.

description: Renders prior turns plus the current streaming query, sources, answer, and follow-ups.
heuristics: Historical messages are grouped into user and assistant pairs by stepping through messages two at a time.
The live status row only appears when searchStatus is non-empty.
While streaming after a user turn, the UI shows a dedicated 'Generating answer...' block.

description: Renders streamed markdown and turns inline citations into hoverable superscripts.
heuristics: MarkdownRenderer uses Streamdown with parseIncompleteMarkdown enabled.
Custom paragraph and list renderers pass text through a citation processor before display.
Bracketed citations like [2] become sup elements with class citation and a data-citation attribute.

description: Normalizes model citation placeholders into the bracketed form the UI expects.
heuristics: The renderer first rewrites CITATION_1-style tokens into a temporary ___CITATION_1___ marker.
A second replacement converts the temporary marker into [1] form.
The two-step rewrite avoids double-processing already normalized citations.

description: Shows source metadata when the user hovers a citation superscript.
heuristics: The tooltip hook listens to document mouseover and mouseout events.
Citation numbers are read from data-citation first, then from text like [3].
Hovered citations resolve their source with sources[citationNumber - 1].

description: Positions citation tooltips above by default and flips or clamps them to stay visible.
heuristics: Tooltip layout assumes width 320, height 100, and viewport padding 10.
Tooltips render above the citation unless there is not enough room, then they flip below.
Horizontal position is clamped so the tooltip never extends past the viewport edges.

description: Renders the primary web source cards with thumbnails, favicons, domains, and char counts.
heuristics: The chat UI shows at most five main source cards per turn.
If more than five sources exist, entries six through ten appear as favicon chips with a +N more label.
Displayed domains prefer siteName and otherwise strip www. from the hostname.

description: Renders up to five news hits in a horizontal mobile rail or stacked desktop list.
heuristics: NewsResults renders results.slice(0, 5).
Thumbnails only show when isValidImageUrl passes.
Dates are parsed defensively and omitted when new Date(dateStr) is invalid.

description: Renders up to six image hits as a responsive gallery with source overlays and modal launch.
heuristics: ImageResults renders results.slice(0, 6).
Mobile uses a horizontal scroller while larger screens switch to a grid.
Clicking a tile stores selectedImage from the thumbnail URL and title.

description: Opens a full-screen image preview that closes on backdrop click or Escape.
heuristics: The modal adds an Escape key listener only while open.
While open, document.body.style.overflow is set to hidden and restored on cleanup.
Backdrop clicks close the modal, but clicks inside the dialog stop propagation.

description: Animates source character counts from zero to the final value as cards appear.
heuristics: The counter updates with requestAnimationFrame.
Its easing curve is 1 - (1 - progress)^4.
The default animation duration is 2000ms and zero counts do not animate.

description: Falls back to a globe icon when a favicon is missing or fails to load.
heuristics: FaviconImage returns a Globe icon immediately when src is missing.
Image onError flips local error state so the Globe replaces the favicon.
Favicon images are loaded unoptimized and lazily.

description: Maps provider failures to shorter user-facing messages before streaming them to the client.
heuristics: The route has custom error text for statuses 401, 402, 429, and 504.
Unknown statuses fall back to the original error message.
Mapped errors are emitted as a transient data-error part that may also include suggestion and statusCode.

description: Maps status codes to titles, guidance text, and help links for recoverable UI errors.
heuristics: getErrorMessage looks up a static ErrorMessages table by status code.
Unknown codes default to the 500 entry.
Each mapped entry includes title, message, action, and actionUrl.

description: Renders a retryable inline error panel with context, cooldown hints, and support links.
heuristics: ErrorDisplay extracts retry-after seconds from error.message using 'retry after Ns'.
The retry button is hidden for 402 credit-exhausted errors.
The panel always shows the mapped action link and can also show an optional context string.

description: Resolve MCP mode from args, legacy iteration settings, config, or defaults before research starts.
heuristics: Accepts fast, deep, and disabled, with fast as the final fallback.
Maps optimized to fast and comprehensive to deep for backward compatibility.
Treats mcp_max_iterations 0 as disabled, 1 as fast, and -1 as deep.

description: Auto-add the MCP retriever unless the user explicitly set RETRIEVER in the environment.
heuristics: Only injects mcp when RETRIEVER is not present in os.environ.
Adds mcp to existing cfg.retrievers instead of replacing the list.
Falls back to cfg.retrievers = mcp when no retrievers are configured.

description: Create a stable per-run research id from the query plus the current timestamp.
heuristics: Builds IDs as research_<12-char-md5> from query plus time.time().
Caches the generated id on self._research_id so it is reused in the session.

description: Map a report type to the correct prompt generator and fall back safely on invalid values.
heuristics: Uses report_type_mapping to look up the prompt method name on the prompt family.
Warns and falls back to research_report when the report type is unknown.

description: Run the full research flow, including agent selection, retrieval, curation, and optional image planning.
heuristics: Branches immediately to _handle_deep_research when report_type is deep.
Clears visited_urls at the start of a new standard research run.
Pre-generates report images after context gathering and before report writing.

description: Write the final report from gathered context and any pre-generated images.
heuristics: Passes existing_headers and relevant_written_contents only for subtopic reports.
Logs available_images_count before writing and images_embedded after completion.

description: Accumulate total and per-step API costs during research and report generation.
heuristics: Rejects non-numeric cost values with ValueError.
Attributes each cost to the current _current_step in step_costs.
Logs cost_update events through log_handler when present.

description: Load default or JSON config files and merge custom keys over the built-in defaults.
heuristics: Returns DEFAULT_CONFIG when CONFIG_PATH is missing or the file does not exist.
Merges custom JSON over DEFAULT_CONFIG so missing keys still exist.
Prints a .json hint when a named config path is missing without the extension.

description: Override config file values with environment variables and coerce them to typed settings.
heuristics: Reads BaseConfig annotations to convert env strings into bool, int, float, list, dict, or None.
Lets environment variables win over JSON config values for every key.
Parses RETRIEVER separately and falls back to tavily if validation fails.

description: Split and validate the configured retriever list against installed retriever names.
heuristics: Splits retrievers on commas and strips whitespace from each name.
Compares parsed names to get_all_retriever_names from the retrievers directory.
Raises ValueError listing every invalid retriever and the valid options.

description: Parse llm specs in provider:model form and reject unsupported provider strings.
heuristics: Splits only on the first colon so model ids may contain colons later.
Asserts the provider is in _SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS before returning.
Raises a usage hint like openai:gpt-4o-mini when the format is wrong.

description: Parse embedding specs in provider:model form and validate the provider name.
heuristics: Uses memory._SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS to validate embedding providers.
Raises a usage hint like openai:text-embedding-3-large on bad input.

description: Normalize reasoning effort to a supported enum value for reasoning-capable models.
heuristics: Defaults to medium when REASONING_EFFORT is unset.
Rejects values outside the ReasoningEfforts enum with a ValueError.

description: Ensure the configured document directory exists before local or hybrid runs.
heuristics: Calls os.makedirs(self.doc_path, exist_ok=True) for validation.
Falls back to DEFAULT_CONFIG DOC_PATH if validation raises in _set_doc_path.

description: List named config presets from the variables directory alongside the built-in default.
heuristics: Always includes default even if no JSON files are present.
Strips the .json suffix from files under Config.CONFIG_DIR.

description: Select the active prompt family implementation and fall back to default on invalid names.
heuristics: Accepts either PromptFamily enum values or raw strings.
Maps granite3.1 and granite3.2 to the Granite3PromptFamily class.
Warns and returns PromptFamily() when the name is invalid.

description: Ask the LLM to choose the best research agent persona and role prompt for a task.
heuristics: Prepends parent_query to the child query when one exists.
Uses smart_llm with temperature 0.15 and auto_agent_instructions as the system prompt.
Expects JSON with server and agent_role_prompt fields.

description: Recover malformed agent-selection JSON and fall back to a safe default researcher persona.
heuristics: Tries json_repair.loads before regex extraction.
Accepts only repaired payloads that contain both server and agent_role_prompt.
Falls back to Default Agent with a fixed critical-thinker role prompt.

description: Pull the first JSON object out of a noisy LLM response when direct parsing fails.
heuristics: Uses re.search with the pattern \{.*?\} and DOTALL.
Returns None immediately when the response is empty.

description: Run the first search pass that seeds planning with current search results.
heuristics: Passes the researcher instance only when the retriever name contains mcpretriever.
Otherwise instantiates the retriever with query and query_domains only.

description: Turn initial search results into a set of sub-queries for the research run.
heuristics: Skips sub-query generation and returns [query] when MCP is the only retriever.
Logs a different path when MCP is combined with other retrievers.
Delegates actual query generation to generate_sub_queries otherwise.

description: Generate research sub-queries from the original task and initial search context.
heuristics: Builds the prompt with prompt_family.generate_search_queries_prompt and cfg.max_iterations.
Tries the strategic LLM first, then retries with strategic_token_limit, then falls back to the smart LLM.
Parses the final LLM response with json_repair.loads.

description: Short-circuit sub-query planning when MCP alone will handle the research task.
heuristics: Detects MCP-only mode when retriever_names length is 1 and the name is mcp or MCPRetriever.
Returns the original query as the only planned query in that case.

description: Generate ordered report subtopics from gathered research data using a structured parser.
heuristics: Uses a PydanticOutputParser over the Subtopics model.
Requests reasoning_effort high for models in SUPPORT_REASONING_EFFORT_MODELS.
Returns the existing subtopics unchanged if parsing or invocation fails.

description: Draft markdown section headers for a subtopic before writing its full section content.
heuristics: Calls prompt_family.generate_draft_titles_prompt with current_subtopic, main query, and context.
Uses the smart LLM and splits the result on newline characters.

description: Assemble the final report-writing prompt from report type, context, tone, and optional images.
heuristics: Uses get_prompt_by_report_type to select the prompt builder for the current report_type.
Switches to custom_prompt plus Context when custom_prompt is supplied.
Appends an AVAILABLE IMAGES block with exact markdown syntax when pre-generated images exist.

description: Collect and randomize fresh URLs from each non-MCP retriever for a sub-query.
heuristics: Skips retriever classes whose name contains mcpretriever.
Runs retriever.search in a thread with cfg.max_search_results_per_query.
Shuffles the deduped URL list before scraping.

description: Filter candidate URLs against visited_urls so the same source is not scraped twice.
heuristics: Adds unseen URLs to researcher.visited_urls as soon as they are accepted.
Streams an added_source_url event for each newly accepted URL in verbose mode.

description: Search for a sub-query, scrape the resulting pages, and load them into the vector store if present.
heuristics: Calls _search_relevant_source_urls before any scraping work.
Loads scraped_content into the vector store immediately after browsing when one is configured.

description: Compress scraped pages down to the most relevant context for a given query.
heuristics: Builds a ContextCompressor with the researcher's embedding backend.
Requests async_get_context(query, max_results=10) when compressing scraped pages.

description: Search a loaded vector store with planned sub-queries and merge the retrieved context.
heuristics: Plans sub-queries first and appends the original query unless this is a subtopic report.
Uses VectorstoreCompressor.async_get_context with max_results 8 per sub-query.

description: Scrape a user-supplied source URL list and compress the pages into query-relevant context.
heuristics: Filters the supplied URLs through _get_new_urls before browsing.
Loads scraped content into the vector store before similarity compression when available.

description: Merge web context and MCP results into one prompt-ready context block with citations.
heuristics: Places web context first and joins MCP entries after it.
Formats each MCP item as content plus a Source citation line, skipping URL text for mcp://llm_analysis.

Separates MCP entries with 

---

.

description: Cache MCP context once in fast mode so later sub-queries can reuse the same tool results.
heuristics: Only fills _mcp_results_cache when mcp_strategy is fast or an unknown mode falls back to fast.
Stores each cached entry as content, url, title, query, and source_type=mcp.

description: Reuse cached MCP context for later sub-queries instead of re-running MCP tools each time.
heuristics: Activates only when mcp_strategy is fast and _mcp_results_cache is populated.
Copies the cached list before reuse so later mutations do not affect the cache.

description: Augment explicit source URLs with additional web search context when broader coverage is requested.
heuristics: Runs _get_context_by_web_search(query, [], query_domains) after scraping the provided URLs.
Concatenates the additional web context onto the source-url context only when complement_source_urls is true.

description: Translate GPT Researcher MCP configs into the MultiServerMCPClient server config shape.
heuristics: Infers websocket transport from ws or wss URLs and streamable_http from http or https URLs.
Splits string args into a list for stdio servers.
Copies env, token, and connection_headers fields into the langchain config when provided.

description: Lazily create and reuse a MultiServerMCPClient for the configured MCP servers.
heuristics: Guards client creation with an asyncio.Lock.
Returns None instead of raising when langchain-mcp-adapters is missing or configs are empty.
Caches the created MultiServerMCPClient on self._client.

description: Load every available tool exposed by the connected MCP servers.
heuristics: Calls client.get_tools() after ensuring a client exists.
Returns an empty list when tool loading fails or no tools are available.

description: Use an LLM to pick the few MCP tools most relevant to the current query.
heuristics: Builds a tools_info list containing index, name, and description for each tool.
Caps max_tools to the number of loaded tools when fewer are available.
Parses selected_tools from JSON and keeps only indices within the all_tools range.

description: Choose MCP tools by keyword scoring when LLM-based selection fails.
heuristics: Scores tool names +3 and descriptions +1 for research verbs like search, read, fetch, and query.
Sorts scored tools descending and returns the top max_tools entries.

description: Run selected MCP tools with an LLM and turn the outputs into research results.
heuristics: Binds selected_tools to the strategic LLM before the first model call.
Executes each returned tool_call via ainvoke, invoke, or direct call fallback.
Appends the LLMs own analysis as an extra result with href mcp://llm_analysis.

description: Normalize raw MCP tool outputs into title, href, and body search-result records.
heuristics: Prefers structured_content.results when the tool returns an MCP wrapper.
Falls back to concatenating text parts from content arrays when no structured results exist.
Generates synthetic mcp:// tool URLs for dict, list, and scalar outputs that lack URLs.

description: Stream MCP stage updates, warnings, and result summaries to logs and websockets.
heuristics: Prefixes stage starts with emojis like Stage 1 and Stage 2 in stream_stage_start.
Uses stream_research_results to include total_chars when that number is known.
Falls back to logger-only mode when no websocket is attached.

description: Search Tavily and normalize results into href and body fields for downstream scraping.
heuristics: Calls the Tavily /search API with search_depth basic and include_domains=self.query_domains.
Returns obj.url as href and obj.content as body from results.
Catches any API failure and returns an empty list.

description: Run a DuckDuckGo text search for lightweight web retrieval without API keys.
heuristics: Requires the ddgs package before instantiation.
Calls DDGS.text with region wt-wt and max_results.
Ignores query_domains and returns [] on provider errors.

description: Search Google Custom Search and optionally constrain results to allowed domains.
heuristics: Builds a domain filter like (site:a OR site:b) query when query_domains are supplied.
Uses google_api_key and google_cx_key from headers before falling back to env vars.
Skips youtube.com links during normalization.

description: Query a SearxNG instance and normalize its JSON results for the research pipeline.
heuristics: Requires SEARX_URL and auto-appends a trailing slash if missing.
Calls /search with q and format=json and Accept application/json.
Returns only url as href and content as body from each result.

description: Query the Bing Web Search API with strict safety and normalize web page results.
heuristics: Sends responseFilter Webpages, safeSearch Strict, setLang en-GB, and textFormat HTML.
Skips youtube.com URLs during normalization.
Logs parsing failures and returns an empty list instead of raising.

description: Search Serper with domain, locale, time-range, and exclude-site filters.
heuristics: Appends -site: exclusions from SERPER_EXCLUDE_SITES directly into the query text.
Adds site:domain filters for query_domains before the POST request.
Maps SERPER_REGION, SERPER_LANGUAGE, and SERPER_TIME_RANGE to gl, hl, and tbs.

description: Search SerpApi and normalize organic Google-style results with optional site filters.
heuristics: Adds site:domain constraints directly into the q parameter when query_domains exist.
Processes only organic_results and stops after max_results non-YouTube hits.
Returns title, link as href, and snippet as body.

description: Search SearchApi and normalize organic results from its Google engine backend.
heuristics: Calls /api/v1/search with engine=google and X-SearchApi-Source=gpt-researcher.
Skips youtube.com links and counts only accepted results toward max_results.
Uses a 20 second request timeout.

description: Search Exa with neural retrieval and optional domain filters for web evidence.
heuristics: Calls Exa.search with type=neural, use_autoprompt=False, and include_domains=self.query_domains.
Normalizes only url to href and text to body from results.results.

description: Search BoCha web results with summaries enabled and normalize them for research use.
heuristics: POSTs to /v1/web-search with freshness=noLimit and summary=True.
Reads data.webPages.value from the JSON response and maps name, url, and snippet.

description: Search arXiv metadata and return paper summaries linked to PDF URLs.
heuristics: Supports Relevance and SubmittedDate sorts via arxiv.SortCriterion.
Returns result.pdf_url as href and result.summary as body for each hit.

description: Search Semantic Scholar and keep only open-access papers with downloadable PDFs.
heuristics: Requests title, abstract, venue, year, authors, and open access fields from the API.
Filters results to isOpenAccess entries that also expose openAccessPdf.
Uses the PDF URL as href and abstract as body.

description: Search PubMed Central or PubMed full-text records and fetch full article XML bodies.
heuristics: Adds ffrft or pmc full-text filters when PUBMED_DB is pubmed.
Populates extra esearch params from PUBMED_ARG_* environment variables.
Fetches full text from efetch in XML and concatenates title, abstract, and body.

description: Call a custom retriever endpoint and pass through its JSON response for retrieval.
heuristics: Requires RETRIEVER_ENDPOINT to be set or raises ValueError.
Collects RETRIEVER_ARG_* env vars into lowercase request params.
Adds the query string under the query parameter on each GET call.

description: Fetch and parse a standard HTML page with BeautifulSoup for text, images, and title.
heuristics: Requests the page with session.get timeout=4.
Parses with lxml using the responses declared encoding.
Runs clean_soup, get_text_from_soup, get_relevant_images, and extract_title in order.

description: Load JavaScript-heavy pages in Selenium, scroll them fully, and extract cleaned page content.
heuristics: Visits Google first and saves cookies before loading the target page.
Waits for a body tag, then scrolls until document height stops increasing.
Special-cases PDFs and arXiv URLs into dedicated PDF scraping helpers.

description: Scrape dynamic pages with a pooled zendriver browser and per-domain rate limits.
heuristics: Maintains up to 5 browsers and creates new ones only past a load threshold of 8.
Uses one asyncio.Semaphore per effective domain and sleeps 0.6 to 1.2s on contention.
Scrolls in random 46 to 97 percent steps until the bottom or 500 percent total scroll.

description: Use FireCrawl to fetch markdown page content while extracting images from the live HTML page.
heuristics: Calls firecrawl.scrape with formats=[markdown].
Treats metadata.error or non-200 metadata.status_code as a scrape failure.
Fetches the original page again with requests to derive images via BeautifulSoup.

description: Extract a page with Tavily and enrich it with local HTML-derived images and title data.
heuristics: Calls tavily_client.extract on a single URL and aborts on failed_results.
Uses results[0].raw_content as the extracted page text.
Fetches the page HTML separately to score images and read the title.

description: Download or open a PDF and extract the text from all pages with PyMuPDFLoader.
heuristics: Retries URL downloads without SSL verification if the verified request fails with SSLError.
Streams remote PDFs to a temp file in 8192-byte chunks before loading.
Joins every page.page_content so cover-page-only PDFs still pass length checks.

description: Pull an arXiv paper with LangChain and prepend publication metadata to the extracted text.
heuristics: Uses ArxivRetriever(load_max_docs=2, doc_content_chars_max=None).
Builds the context as Published + Author + Content from docs[0].metadata and page_content.

description: Load a page with WebBaseLoader and combine the text with HTML-derived images and title.
heuristics: Sets loader.requests_kwargs = {verify: False} before loading.
Concatenates page_content from every loaded doc into one content string.

description: Walk local files or explicit paths and load supported document types into raw_content records.
heuristics: Supports pdf, txt, doc, docx, pptx, csv, xls, xlsx, md, html, and htm.
Uses os.walk for directory inputs and ignores non-files in explicit lists.
Raises a failure if no pages with content are loaded.

description: Download remote documents to temp files and load them with extension-specific loaders.
heuristics: Uses aiohttp with User-Agent Mozilla/5.0 and timeout=6 for each URL.
Infers the temp-file suffix from the URL path before the query string.
Always deletes the temp file in _load_document finally.

description: Convert LangChain Document objects into GPT Researcher raw_content and url records.
heuristics: Uses metadata_source_index=title by default to fill the url field.
Copies page_content directly into raw_content without extra chunking.

description: Download every blob from an Azure container into temp files for the existing loader stack.
heuristics: Creates the BlobServiceClient from a connection string.
Downloads each blob fully with readall into a temp directory and returns local paths.

description: Drop URLs whose string contains any domain fragment listed in config.excluded_domains.
heuristics: Uses a simple substring test against every excluded domain entry.
Keeps all URLs that do not match any excluded fragment.

description: Strip navigation, scripting, and other low-value HTML before text extraction.
heuristics: Removes script, style, footer, header, nav, menu, sidebar, and svg tags entirely.
Also deletes any tag whose class list contains nav, menu, sidebar, or footer.

description: Flatten cleaned HTML into newline-separated text with excess whitespace removed.
heuristics: Calls soup.get_text(strip=True, separator=\n).
Collapses runs of 2 or more whitespace characters with a regex.

description: Read the page title from parsed HTML when the title tag is present.
heuristics: Returns soup.title.string when soup.title exists, else an empty string.

description: Score and rank page images by classes, size hints, and URL validity.
heuristics: Assigns score 4 to images with classes like hero, featured, thumbnail, main, or content.
Scores width and height buckets, skipping very small images below 500x300.
Returns only the top 10 images sorted by descending score.

description: Hash an image URL into a stable identity that ignores most CDN query noise.
heuristics: Builds the identity from the filename plus only the url query parameter when present.
Uses md5 over that reduced image identifier string.

description: Keep the best unique images from scraped pages and avoid duplicates already in the session.
heuristics: Sorts candidate images by score descending before selection.
Dedupes by get_image_hash and skips URLs already in current_research_images.
Stops as soon as k unique images have been accepted.

description: Return the main content field from scraped HTML items for downstream processing.
heuristics: Current implementation returns the raw html_content unchanged as a placeholder.
Is called only on items whose status equals success in process_scraped_data.

description: Normalize scrape records by extracting content from successful pages and preserving failures.
heuristics: Transforms only items with status == success into url, content, status records.
Copies failed items through unchanged instead of dropping them.

description: Format retrieved documents into prompt-safe context blocks before synthesis.
heuristics: Default PromptFamily pretty_print_docs emits Source, Title, and Content lines for each Document.
Granite prompt families wrap formatted documents in model-specific document tokens instead of plain text.

description: Join local-document context and web context into one model-ready context payload.
heuristics: Default prompts label the two parts as Context from local documents and Context from web sources.
Granite3 strips or rewraps special document tokens when merging the two contexts.

description: Instantiate the embedding backend for similarity search from the configured provider and model.
heuristics: Supports provider-specific constructors like OpenAIEmbeddings, AzureOpenAIEmbeddings, and OllamaEmbeddings.
Custom mode defaults OPENAI_BASE_URL to http://localhost:1234/v1 and key to custom.
Raises Embedding not found for unsupported providers.

description: Split raw documents into fixed-size overlapping chunks before vector indexing.
heuristics: Uses RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter with chunk_size=1000 and chunk_overlap=200 in VectorStoreWrapper.
Preserves source metadata from the original raw_content records on each chunk.

description: Convert raw documents to LangChain docs, split them, and add them to the vector store.
heuristics: Maps raw_content to page_content and url to metadata.source before splitting.
Calls vector_store.add_documents on the split chunk set, not the unsplit originals.

description: Fetch the top semantic matches from the vector store for a query and optional filter.
heuristics: Delegates directly to vector_store.asimilarity_search(query=query, k=k, filter=filter).

description: Skip embedding compression for small document sets and return them directly.
heuristics: Activates when total_chars is below COMPRESSION_THRESHOLD, default 8000.
Also requires len(documents) <= max_results before taking the fast path.

description: Filter document chunks by embedding similarity before building prompt context.
heuristics: Uses EmbeddingsFilter with similarity_threshold from SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD or 0.35.
Runs after a RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter with chunk_size=1000 and overlap=100.

description: Find prior written sections similar to a subtopic and its planned section titles.
heuristics: Queries on current_subtopic plus every draft_section_title and unions the results.
Formats each hit as Title: <section_title> and Content: <page_content>.

description: Estimate embedding spend from the tokenized size of documents being embedded.
heuristics: Uses tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model) to count tokens.
Multiplies total tokens by EMBEDDING_COST = 0.02 per million tokens.

description: Trim context lists to a word budget while keeping the newest items first.
heuristics: Processes the list in reverse so recent items are preserved first.
Stops at MAX_CONTEXT_WORDS, which is 25000 by default.

description: Generate the main report body from gathered context using the selected report prompt.
heuristics: Uses the smart LLM with stream=True and temperature 0.35 for report generation.
Falls back to a single user message containing agent_role_prompt plus content if the system+user call fails.

description: Write a subtopic-only report section that avoids overlap with previously written sections.
heuristics: Passes main_topic, existing_headers, and relevant_written_contents into the subtopic prompt.
Uses the report_type == subtopic_report branch inside generate_report.

description: Write a bibliography-style resource report that evaluates how each source helps answer the question.
heuristics: Selects generate_resource_report_prompt through get_prompt_by_report_type.
Requires source URLs as hyperlinks for web reports and source document names for non-web reports.

description: Write a markdown outline that structures the final report without writing the full narrative yet.
heuristics: Selects generate_outline_report_prompt through get_prompt_by_report_type.
Requests main sections, subsections, and key points with a minimum total_words budget.

description: Write a final report from hierarchical deep-research context with branch-level citations preserved.
heuristics: Uses the deep-specific prompt that prioritizes deeper findings and branch connections.
Assumes the current date inside the prompt and requires in-text hyperlink citations.

description: Write the report introduction from the gathered research summary and selected role prompt.
heuristics: Uses the smart LLM with temperature 0.25 and stream=True.
Builds the user prompt with prompt_family.generate_report_introduction.

description: Write a concise report conclusion that recaps findings and implications with citations.
heuristics: Uses the smart LLM with temperature 0.25 and stream=True.
Builds the user prompt with prompt_family.generate_report_conclusion.

description: Build a nested markdown table of contents from the report header structure.
heuristics: Parses markdown into HTML headers first via markdown.markdown.
Indents child headers by four spaces per nesting level and prefixes each bullet with -.

description: Append a References section that lists every visited URL as a markdown link.
heuristics: Always appends a ## References heading at the end of the report.
Formats each URL as - [url](url).

description: Extract the nested markdown header tree from a report for TOC and overlap checks.
heuristics: Converts markdown to HTML and reads h1-h6 tags line by line.
Maintains a stack so lower-level headers become children of the nearest higher-level header.

description: Split markdown into titled section payloads for similarity checks across subtopic drafts.
heuristics: Uses a regex over rendered HTML to capture each header and its following content block.
Strips HTML tags from the captured content before storing written_content.

description: Rank and keep the strongest sources for writing when source curation is enabled.
heuristics: Calls the smart LLM with temperature 0.2 and max_tokens 8000 for curation.
Falls back to the original source_data if JSON parsing or LLM curation fails.

description: Initialize image generation only when the model is configured and the provider is available.
heuristics: Requires both image_generation_model and image_generation_enabled to proceed.
Drops the provider back to None when is_available is false or initialization raises.

description: Plan and pre-generate report images from research context before report writing begins.
heuristics: Returns immediately when image generation is not enabled.
Identifies 2 to 3 image concepts first, then generates them in parallel with asyncio.gather.
Uses image_generation_style from config, defaulting to dark.

description: Analyze research context or report sections to find concepts worth illustrating.
heuristics: Truncates context to 6000 characters before the planning prompt.
Focuses the prompt on architectures, workflows, charts, and conceptual illustrations.
Skips image suggestions entirely when no report sections can be extracted.

description: Parse LLM image-plan JSON and keep only valid concept records up to the image cap.
heuristics: Strips surrounding ```json code fences before json.loads.
Keeps only dict entries that contain at least title and prompt.
Limits the kept concepts to self.max_images.

description: Generate multiple report images concurrently from planned visualization concepts.
heuristics: Each task calls image_provider.generate_image with num_images=1.
Adds title and section_hint from the concept onto the returned image_info.
Filters out None results after asyncio.gather completes.

description: Expose pre-generated images to the writer so it can embed them directly in the report markdown.
heuristics: Appends an AVAILABLE IMAGES section with exact markdown for every generated image.
Includes section_hint text so the model knows where each image belongs.

description: Replace [IMAGE: ...] placeholders with generated markdown images or remove them on failure.
heuristics: Matches placeholders with the regex \[IMAGE:\s*([^\]]+)\].
Limits processed placeholders to self.max_images.
Removes placeholders entirely when generation is disabled or an image call fails.

description: Insert generated images back into the report immediately after their target section headers.
heuristics: Maps section_header values to generated image records before rewriting the report.
Inserts a blank-line-separated ![alt_text](url) line after matching H2 or H3 headers.

description: Expose generated image metadata to the caller and stream inline image data to the frontend.
heuristics: Stores the final images on self.generated_images for later retrieval.
Streams generated_images inline_images payloads containing url and alt when verbose mode is on.

description: Generate clarifying follow-up questions that expand the deep-research search space.
heuristics: First gathers initial search results from every configured retriever.
Injects the current timestamp into the prompt so the questions account for recency.
Parses only lines that start with Question: from the LLM response.

description: Generate targeted SERP queries and research goals for each deep-research branch.
heuristics: Requests Query: and Goal: pairs from the strategic LLM.
Parses the response line by line and caps the list to num_queries.

description: Extract key learnings, follow-up questions, and source citations from a branch context.
heuristics: Parses Learning [source_url]: lines first and falls back to URL regex extraction if needed.
Returns only the first num_learnings learnings and follow-up questions.
Stores citations in a dict keyed by the exact learning text.

description: Recurse through breadth and depth levels by spawning nested research branches from new goals.
heuristics: Creates a fresh GPTResearcher per SERP query and runs standard web research inside each branch.
Builds the next recursive query from the branch researchGoal plus all followUpQuestions.
Continues recursion only while depth > 1.

description: Reduce branch fan-out at each recursion level so deeper research stays tractable.
heuristics: Computes new_breadth as max(2, breadth // 2) before deeper recursion.

description: Throttle concurrent deep-research branches with a semaphore-driven concurrency limit.
heuristics: Uses asyncio.Semaphore(self.concurrency_limit) around each branch runner.
The concurrency limit defaults from cfg.deep_research_concurrency, or 2 when unset.

description: Accumulate learnings, citations, sources, URLs, and context across every deep-research branch.
heuristics: Extends all_learnings and all_sources and updates all_citations and all_visited_urls from each result.
Trims the merged context list back to the word budget before returning.

description: Track current query, breadth, depth, and completed branch counts during deep research.
heuristics: ResearchProgress starts at current_depth=1 and current_breadth=0.
Calls on_progress before branch work, after each branch, and after breadth updates.

description: Instantiate a unified chat model wrapper for the configured provider and model settings.
heuristics: Routes through GenericLLMProvider.from_provider based on the llm_provider string.
OpenAI mode honors OPENAI_BASE_URL if it is present in the environment.
The provider loader auto-installs missing LangChain packages via _check_pkg.

description: Retry chat completions with backoff and empty-response handling before surfacing a hard failure.
heuristics: Uses up to 10 attempts unless stream and websocket are both set, which drops to 1.
Sleeps with exponential backoff capped at 8 seconds between attempts.
Treats an empty response as a retryable failure.

description: Stream LLM output incrementally to the websocket in paragraph-sized chunks.
heuristics: Buffers streamed content until a newline appears, then flushes the accumulated paragraph.
Sends websocket messages as {type: report, output: content} when a websocket exists.

description: Estimate LLM spend from tokenized inputs and outputs using fixed pricing constants.
heuristics: Counts tokens with the o200k_base encoding.
Prices input tokens at 0.000005 and output tokens at 0.000015 each.

description: Bind callable tools to a LangChain chat model so the model can issue tool calls.
heuristics: Converts role/content dicts into SystemMessage, HumanMessage, and AIMessage objects first.
Calls llm_provider_instance.llm.bind_tools(tools) before invocation.

description: Execute model-issued tool calls, append ToolMessages, and ask the model for a final answer.
heuristics: Supports tool.ainvoke, tool.invoke, and direct callable execution paths.
Appends each tool result as a ToolMessage with the original tool_call_id.
Runs a second llm_with_tools.ainvoke after all tool results are appended.
