description: Wrap a search function as a standardized LLM tool that returns readable search snippets.
heuristics: Formats at most 5 results with Title, Content preview, and URL lines.
Returns specialized user-facing errors for API key, timeout, and rate-limit failures.

description: Wrap an arbitrary function as a named tool with safe, user-facing error messages.
heuristics: Returns Tool executed successfully when the function returns None.
Maps validation or invalid errors to bad-input guidance and missing errors to not-found guidance.

description: Detect whether the active LLM provider is in the allowlist for tool calling support.
heuristics: Allows openai, anthropic, google_genai, azure_openai, fireworks, and groq.
supports_tools is a simple membership test against that provider list.

description: Serve the research backend with FastAPI, static mounts, and CORS configured for the frontend.
heuristics: Creates outputs on startup and mounts it at /outputs.
Mounts /site and /static from the frontend directory when they exist.
Uses CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS or defaults to localhost:3000, 127.0.0.1:3000, and app.gptr.dev.

description: Accept, queue, send, and cleanly tear down websocket connections for research sessions.
heuristics: Creates one asyncio.Queue and sender task per accepted websocket.
Treats a queued None message as the sender shutdown signal.
Removes tasks and queues before closing the websocket during disconnect.

description: Keep websocket sessions alive with periodic ping and pong heartbeats.
heuristics: Frontend sends ping every 30000 ms while the socket is open.
Backend replies with pong when it receives the literal ping message.

description: Stream structured research events to the client while persisting a JSON event log on disk.
heuristics: Writes an outputs/task_*.json file with timestamp, events, and content buckets at startup.
Appends logs-type payloads to the events list and merges other payloads into content.

description: Route incoming report requests to the single-agent, detailed, or multi-agent execution path.
heuristics: Selects run_multi_agent_task for report_type multi_agents, DetailedReport for detailed_report, else BasicReport.
Appends mcp to RETRIEVER and sets MCP_STRATEGY in os.environ when MCP is enabled.

description: Persist reports and chat history in a JSON file with safe concurrent updates.
heuristics: Wraps all read and write operations in a single asyncio.Lock.
Writes through a .tmp file and replaces the main file atomically.
Returns an empty dict when the backing JSON file is missing or malformed.

description: Proxy Next.js API requests to the backend service while preserving status and error details.
heuristics: Uses NEXT_PUBLIC_GPTR_API_URL or falls back to http://localhost:8000.
Forwards GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE requests for /api/reports and report chat routes.
Wraps backend failures into NextResponse.json with the backend status code when possible.

description: Upload a document into DOC_PATH and immediately validate it through the document loader.
heuristics: Writes the upload to DOC_PATH using the basename of file.filename.
Calls DocumentLoader(DOC_PATH).load() after saving to verify the corpus still loads.

description: Delete an uploaded document from DOC_PATH by sanitized basename and report whether it existed.
heuristics: Builds the deletion target with os.path.basename(filename) under DOC_PATH.
Returns a 404 JSONResponse when the file does not exist.

description: Export a finished report to markdown, PDF, and DOCX output files.
heuristics: PDF export rewrites /outputs image URLs to absolute paths before md2pdf runs.
DOCX export converts markdown to HTML with mistune then feeds HtmlToDocx.
Markdown export writes UTF-8 text with replacement for bad characters.

description: Answer follow-up chat questions against an existing report with citations and optional search tools.
heuristics: Builds one system prompt containing product context, citation instructions, current time, and the full report text.
Filters message history to only role and content before sending it to the LLM.
Falls back to a canned apology when the model returns empty content.

description: Run a lightweight Tavily search from chat when the report lacks fresh or current information.
heuristics: Uses TavilyClient.search(query=query, max_results=5) when TAVILY_API_KEY is configured.
Returns a disabled-search error and empty results when Tavily is unavailable.

description: Attach quick-search query and source metadata so the frontend can show what the assistant searched.
heuristics: Stores search_metadata as query plus a list of title, url, and 200-char content previews.
Re-runs quick_search(query) after tool execution to repopulate self.search_metadata for the response.

description: Append report chat messages to backend history and keep a local fallback copy in localStorage.
heuristics: POSTs each message to /api/reports/{id}/chat before updating local state.
If the server call fails, still appends the message to state and localStorage.

description: Hydrate the chat agent with the full report so follow-up answers stay grounded in prior research.
heuristics: Stores the raw report on ChatAgentWithMemory.report at initialization.
Injects the full Report text into the system prompt for every chat turn.

description: Proxy chat requests through Next.js routes to the backend chat endpoints.
heuristics: For /api/chat, forwards the parsed JSON body directly to backend /api/chat.
For report chat routes, validates that the id param exists before proxying.

description: Open the research websocket, send the start payload, and wire incoming events into UI state.
heuristics: Builds ws or wss from getHost by swapping the http scheme and appending /ws.
Closes any existing open socket before creating a new one.
Sends start plus a JSON payload containing task, report settings, domains, and MCP config on open.

description: Hydrate history from localStorage first, then reconcile it with the server-side report store.
heuristics: Loads local researchHistory immediately for fast paint before fetching the backend.
Requests /api/reports filtered by the local id list instead of fetching the full server history.
Uploads local-only reports back to the server if they have both question and answer fields.

description: Load a single research artifact by id from the backend with a localStorage fallback.
heuristics: Calls /api/reports/{id} first and returns data.report on success.
Falls back to searching localStorage researchHistory when the backend returns 404 or throws.

description: Render the sidebar that lists prior research sessions, timestamps, and delete actions.
heuristics: Uses formatDistanceToNow to show relative timestamps when possible.
Closes automatically when a mousedown lands outside sidebarRef.
Links each item to /research/{id} and shows the delete button only on hover.

description: Render source links as either compact hostname chips or full source cards.
heuristics: Compact mode parses the URL and strips a leading www. from the displayed hostname.
Full mode shows loading skeleton cards when the sources list is empty.

description: Render streaming agent logs and keep the log viewport pinned to the newest entry.
heuristics: Uses a ref and scrollTop = scrollHeight whenever the logs prop changes.
Renders the feed inside a container capped at 500px height.

description: Render related images in a responsive grid with modal viewing and broken-image filtering.
heuristics: Drops broken images from validImages when an img onError fires.
Wraps next and previous modal navigation around the ends of the image list.

description: Render and persist report-type, source, tone, domain, layout, and file-upload research settings.
heuristics: Keeps domain filters in localStorage under domainFilters and mirrors them into chatBoxSettings.domains.
Shows FileUpload only for local and hybrid sources.
Shows DomainFilter only for web and hybrid sources.

description: Validate, edit, and toggle MCP server JSON config from the frontend settings panel.
heuristics: Requires each MCP config entry to have a name and either command or connection_url.
Treats empty or [] as a valid empty configuration.
Includes built-in github, tavily, and filesystem presets that rewrite the JSON textarea.

description: Switch between research, copilot, and mobile layouts based on settings and screen width.
heuristics: Uses MobileLayout whenever window.innerWidth < 768.
Uses CopilotLayout only when chatBoxSettings.layoutType equals copilot.
Falls back to ResearchPageLayout for desktop research mode.

description: Run the LangGraph research team across planning, human review, section research, writing, and publishing.
heuristics: Builds a StateGraph with browser, planner, human, researcher, writer, and publisher nodes.
Routes human feedback None to researcher and non-empty feedback back to planner.
Starts the workflow at browser and ends after publisher.

description: Run the AG2 version of the research team with ConversableAgents and a UserProxy reviewer.
heuristics: Creates AG2 agents for chief_editor, editor, researcher, reviewer, reviser, writer, publisher, and human.
Uses GroupChat max_round=1 with manual speaker selection.
Switches the Human agent to ALWAYS input mode only when include_human_feedback is enabled.

description: Plan the report title, date, and section list from initial research and optional human feedback.
heuristics: Injects todays date into the planning prompt.
Explicitly forbids introduction, conclusion, and references in the planned sections.
Asks the model to return only JSON with title, date, and sections.

description: Request human review of the planned section list before parallel section research begins.
heuristics: Streams a human_feedback request over the websocket when one is attached.
Normalizes any answer containing no to None so the workflow can accept the plan.

description: Run section-level research drafts in parallel and return the final accepted draft for each section.
heuristics: LangGraph mode runs a per-section researcher-reviewer-reviser graph and gathers all chains concurrently.
AG2 mode uses asyncio.gather over _run_section for every planned section.

description: Review section drafts against explicit guidelines and request revision only when needed.
heuristics: Joins task.guidelines into the review prompt and returns None when the draft is acceptable.
Treats any model response containing the string None as an accepted draft.

description: Revise a section draft from reviewer notes and return both the new draft and revision notes.
heuristics: Requires the reviser model output to be JSON with draft and revision_notes fields.
The prompt tells the reviser to keep all other aspects of the draft the same.

description: Write introduction, table of contents, conclusion, sources, and layout headers around section drafts.
heuristics: WriterAgent asks for introduction, conclusion, table_of_contents, and sources as JSON.
Can rewrite header labels through revise_headers when follow_guidelines is true.
PublisherAgent.generate_layout assembles title, date, intro, TOC, sections, conclusion, and references into markdown.

description: Split broad requests into single searchable subquestions before adding graph nodes.
heuristics: GRAPH_PROMPT forbids multi-part search nodes and gives A/B/C comparison as a bad example
GRAPH_PROMPT allows parallel search only for unrelated subquestions

description: Have the planner answer by writing Python that mutates a live search graph.
heuristics: GRAPH_PROMPT frames the planner as a programmer using WebSearchGraph in a Jupyter-style environment
graph_fewshot_example ends each code block with graph.node(...) calls to fetch newly added nodes
ExecutionAction extracts fenced code and execs it directly

description: Seed the search graph with the original user question as the root node.
heuristics: graph_fewshot_example starts with graph.add_root_node(node_content=question, node_name=root)
WebSearchGraph.add_root_node stores the node as type root and creates an empty adjacency list

description: Create a named search node for each focused follow-up question.
heuristics: graph_fewshot_example says node names should be meaningful when calling graph.add_node
WebSearchGraph.add_node stores each node as type searcher and initializes adjacency_list[node_name] = []

description: Link root and subquery nodes so the UI can show search dependencies.
heuristics: GRAPH_PROMPT exposes add_edge(start_node, end_node) as the way to connect nodes
WebSearchGraph.add_edge appends a uuid-tagged edge with initial state 2

description: Close planning by adding a standalone response node when enough evidence exists.
heuristics: GRAPH_PROMPT says response must be added separately and never in the same turn as other nodes
WebSearchGraph.add_response_node creates a node with type end and pushes it into the response queue

description: Prepare the final summarization prompt that turns node answers into the user-facing answer.
heuristics: FINAL_RESPONSE prompts demand a detailed, well-structured answer with citations kept in the same [[int]] format
MindSearchAgent sends summary_prompt only after finish_condition sees add_response_node in planner output

description: Build the searcher prompt that teaches the subagent to search and cite results.
heuristics: searcher_system_prompt injects tool_info and requires plugin calls inside action_start plugin blocks
searcher_system_prompt requires every key point to cite source ids like [[3]][[8]]

description: Pass answered parent nodes into later subsearches so follow-up queries inherit context.
heuristics: SearcherAgent.forward prepends history entries using user_context_template before the current question
user_context_template formats each prior item as Historical Problem and Answer or its Chinese equivalent

description: Instantiate the configured LLM backend for planner and searcher agents.
heuristics: init_agent deep-copies a config from mindsearch.agent.models by model_format and calls create_object on it
When use_async is true, init_agent rewrites the class name to lagent.llms.Async plus the original class name

description: Reuse LLM client objects across agent initializations for the same model and mode.
heuristics: LLM is a module-level dict keyed by model_format then mode
init_agent only creates a backend when LLM[model_format][mode] is missing

description: Switch the whole agent stack between synchronous and asynchronous execution paths.
heuristics: init_agent sets mode to async if use_async else sync
Async mode selects AsyncMindSearchAgent and AsyncWebBrowser and also rewrites the LLM class to its async variant

description: Configure the web search plugin with engine-specific credentials and result limits.
heuristics: init_agent always builds one browser plugin with topk 6
TencentSearch reads TENCENT_SEARCH_SECRET_ID and TENCENT_SEARCH_SECRET_KEY, while other engines read WEB_SEARCH_API_KEY

description: Wrap lagent agents so every intermediate model chunk is yielded as AgentMessage objects.
heuristics: StreamingAgentMixin and AsyncStreamingAgentMixin call update_memory before and after forwarding
Non-AgentMessage outputs are normalized into AgentMessage(sender=self.name, content=response, stream_state=model_state)

description: Translate model and tool events into agent stream states the UI understands.
heuristics: StreamingAgentForInternLM maps tool_type plugin to PLUGIN_START and code to CODING while a tool is streaming
When a tool stream ends, it restores stream_state from the previous agent state before emitting the return event

description: Run plugin or code actions after the planner emits a structured tool call.
heuristics: StreamingAgentForInternLM reads message.formatted tool_type and dispatches to the matching executor
MindSearchAgent replaces the default code executor with ExecutionAction to run planner-generated graph code

description: Persist prompt and response history per session so later turns can reuse state.
heuristics: StreamingAgentMixin calls update_memory(message, session_id=session_id) before and after generation
WebSearchGraph stores each searcher agent state_dict under node memory for later replay

description: Stop the planning loop when the planner decides the graph is ready to summarize.
heuristics: MindSearchAgent finish_condition defaults to checking whether add_response_node appears in planner content
When no tool_type is present, MindSearchAgent marks the message END and returns immediately

description: Create per-node searcher agents with the shared LLM and browser plugin config.
heuristics: WebSearchGraph.add_node deep-copies SEARCHER_CONFIG plugins before instantiating a SearcherAgent
Each node gets its own random session_id between 0 and 999999

description: Execute planner-emitted Python against the live graph and return requested node states.
heuristics: ExecutionAction runs exec(command, global_dict, local_dict) after extracting the code block
It returns the graph.node results plus graph.nodes and graph.adjacency_list

description: Ignore redundant WebSearchGraph imports inside planner code before execution.
heuristics: extract_code removes any from module import WebSearchGraph line with regex before exec

description: Pull runnable Python out of planner messages that may contain markdown fences.
heuristics: extract_code prefers triple-backtick blocks, then single-backtick snippets, and falls back to raw text
The triple-backtick regex is re.search on a fenced block pattern with re.DOTALL

description: Launch independent search nodes concurrently instead of waiting for each one serially.
heuristics: Sync mode submits each _search_node_stream to a ThreadPoolExecutor with max_workers 10
Async mode uses asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe on one of the shared search loops chosen at random

description: Emit incremental graph snapshots while node searchers are still running.
heuristics: ExecutionAction yields a STREAM_ING AgentMessage whenever all non-root non-response nodes have a response field
Yielded payloads include deep copies of graph.nodes and graph.adjacency_list

description: Track outstanding node searches so graph execution knows when to stop draining.
heuristics: WebSearchGraph increments n_active_tasks every time add_node launches a search worker
ExecutionAction decrements n_active_tasks when the queue receives the sentinel tuple with node_name None

description: Mark each edge as pending, active, or done based on the child node stream state.
heuristics: ExecutionAction sets neighbor state 2 when the child node has no response yet
It sets state 3 when child response.stream_state equals AgentStatusCode.END, otherwise state 1

description: Snapshot each node response, memory, and session id as search output arrives.
heuristics: Both search modes write searcher_message.model_dump() into nodes[node_name][response] on every chunk
They also store agent.state_dict(session_id=session_id) and the session_id on the node

description: Feed answered predecessor nodes into a child search so it can ask a contextual follow-up.
heuristics: add_node scans adjacency_list for incoming edges whose start node already has a response
Those parents are converted into question and answer dicts and passed as history to the SearcherAgent

description: Maintain a pool of background asyncio loops for async search workers.
heuristics: AsyncMindSearchAgent.__init__ sets WebSearchGraph.is_async = True and calls WebSearchGraph.start_loop()
start_loop creates daemon threads running new_event_loop and loop.run_forever until there are n loops, default 32

description: Append a graph edge and immediately surface the adjacency update to listeners.
heuristics: add_edge appends dict(id=str(uuid.uuid4()), name=end_node, state=2) to adjacency_list[start_node]
It pushes the updated start node and its outgoing edges into searcher_resp_queue for streaming

description: Clear all graph nodes and edges before starting over.
heuristics: WebSearchGraph.reset replaces nodes with an empty dict and adjacency_list with defaultdict(list)

description: Call the browser plugin to search the web for a node question.
heuristics: searcher_system_prompt requires tool calls through the plugin syntax rather than plain prose
init_agent configures the browser plugin with searcher_type from search_engine and topk 6

description: Pick the most useful search hits for deeper reading before writing the node answer.
heuristics: fewshot_example asks the model to inspect coarse hits, justify relevance, then call select on specific indexes
The example narrows from broad search results to pages 3 and 13 before answering

description: Encode tool observations as JSON strings that the UI can replay and parse.
heuristics: Gradio formatter dumps dict plugin payloads with json.dumps ensure_ascii=False and indent 4
React stashNodeInfo parses response.content as JSON to rebuild search result cards by id

description: Renumber node-local [[id]] citations into one graph-wide citation namespace.
heuristics: update_ref collects unique citation numbers with a regex over [[number]] markers
It rewrites each citation to a new id plus ptr and returns the next pointer offset

description: Merge every search node answer into one reference packet for final synthesis.
heuristics: generate_references_from_graph skips root and response nodes when building the summary input
Each node contributes a markdown section starting with ## question followed by the rewritten answer text

description: Build the final citation-id to URL metadata map that frontends use for popovers.
heuristics: MindSearchAgent stores the merged reference URL dict under formatted ref2url
React CustomMarkdown expects each ref entry to expose url, title, and summ fields

description: Trim streamed node payloads so SSE only sends the active node and safe metadata.
heuristics: postprocess_agent_message drops ref2url from node updates and keeps only the current node in formatted node
For non-node messages it removes the whole node field from formatted

description: Strip raw action tokens and private memory fields before sending tool traces to clients.
heuristics: Backend splits thought at action_start and action at action_end before streaming to clients
For current nodes it removes memory and session_id from the node payload before returning it

description: Parse backend host, port, language, model format, search engine, and async mode flags.
heuristics: parse_arguments defines host, port, lang, model_format, search_engine, and asy flags
asy uses action store_true to switch the server to the async agent route

description: Validate incoming generation payloads and fill in defaults with Pydantic.
heuristics: GenerationParams requires inputs to be either a string or a list of dicts
agent_cfg defaults to an empty dict when omitted

description: Assign a random session id when the client does not provide one.
heuristics: GenerationParams.session_id uses Field(default_factory=lambda: random.randint(0, 999999))

description: Expose the generation stream as an SSE endpoint on /solve.
heuristics: app.add_api_route wires solve to run_async if args.asy else run using POST
Both handlers return EventSourceResponse(generate(), ping=300)

description: Bridge a synchronous agent generator into an async SSE response.
heuristics: run pushes sync agent outputs into a janus.Queue from a threadpool worker
async_generator_wrapper awaits queue.async_q.get() until it receives a None sentinel

description: Stop streaming when the SSE client disconnects.
heuristics: Both run and run_async break the event loop when request.is_disconnected() becomes true

description: Convert backend exceptions into structured SSE error payloads.
heuristics: On exception, generate yields data containing error msg and error details as JSON
The sync wrapper logs the exception with logging.exception before sending the error object

description: Remove per-session agent memory after a stream finishes or aborts.
heuristics: Both SSE handlers call agent.agent.memory.memory_map.pop(session_id, None) in finally
The sync handler also closes the janus queue and awaits queue.wait_closed()

description: Submit the staged question to solve and start a new streaming exchange.
heuristics: createSseChat copies stashedQuestion into question, clears the input, and sets currentNodeName to customer-0
startEventSource posts a JSON body with inputs question using fetchEventSource

description: Cap the number of stored conversations before forcing the user into a new chat.
heuristics: startEventSource blocks new requests when qaList.length is greater than 4
When capped, it sets newChatTip and shows the warning 对话数已达上限，请在新对话中聊天

description: Consume backend SSE messages and fold each chunk into the live UI state.
heuristics: onmessage parses ev.data as JSON and routes non-END chunks through formatData
When response.stream_state equals 0 it marks chatIsOver and stores chatIsOver on the current formatted item

description: Persist incremental node traces in localStorage so the side panel can reopen them.
heuristics: stashNodeInfo writes node snapshots into window.localStorage.stashedNodes keyed by nodeName
It derives searchList and conclusionRef by JSON-parsing response.content into id-indexed results

description: Rebuild a finished node timeline from stored agent memory for history replay.
heuristics: formatHistoryNode reads memory 0 as thinking, memory 2 as reading, and memory 4 as the conclusion
It rebuilds selectedIds from memory 2 formatted action parameters select_ids

description: Infer that planning is done once the adjacency list contains a response edge.
heuristics: checkNodesOutputFinish stringifies adjList and looks for the substring name response
Once found, it sets isEnd true and later auto-hides the right panel

description: Keep the latest streamed text visible while a response is still arriving.
heuristics: When chatIsOver is false, a 50ms interval calls keepScrollTop
keepScrollTop scrolls chatArea so messageWindowId stays within view plus a 30px buffer

description: Track the currently selected node and reopen its right panel snapshot on updates.
heuristics: A useEffect reloads currentNodeInfo from localStorage.stashedNodes whenever currentNodeName changes
If currentNodeName is not customer-0, the effect also forces showRight true

description: Show or hide the right-side reasoning drawer without leaving the main chat.
heuristics: toggleRight flips showRight and the UI swaps between ChatRight and a floating think-progress icon
A separate effect clears activeNode whenever showRight becomes false

description: Start a clean session by reloading the page and clearing transient state.
heuristics: openNewChat calls location.reload()
The mount effect clears localStorage.stashedNodes and localStorage.reformatStashedNodes

description: Open a node from the mind map unless the answer is finalizing or the node is disabled.
heuristics: handleNodeClick returns immediately when isEnd and chatIsOver is false to block interaction during final answer streaming
MindMapItem ignores clicks on state 0 nodes and on the labels 原始问题 and 最终回复

description: Open a stored node trace from a previous conversation without needing live SSE state.
heuristics: handleNodeClick checks qaList[idx].nodes[node] and routes history selections into historyNode
ChatRight.handleReceiveHistory hydrates the panel from the replay object instead of live nodeInfo

description: Render completed and in-flight Q and A sessions in one scrollable transcript.
heuristics: MindSearchCon maps qaList to SessionItem components and appends the current formatted item as a live SessionItem
When a round ends, formatted is appended to qaList and initPageState resets the live slot

description: Route the React app to the MindSearch page and redirect unknown paths home.
heuristics: routes.tsx binds path slash to MindSearchCon
The wildcard route redirects every other path back to slash

description: Convert the backend adjacency list into the recursive tree structure used by the mind map.
heuristics: Answer.generateMapData recurses through adjList[item.name] to build children arrays
convertTreeData wraps that tree in a synthetic root node named 原始问题 with state 3

description: Measure rendered node positions to size the mind map and place the final response node.
heuristics: MindMapGraph.generateWidth scans all article elements and expands width to maxRight minus firstLeft plus 200
generateEndStyle measures the first and last endline boxes to compute the response column top and height

description: Render the graph as nested recursive list items with branch lines and leaf markers.
heuristics: MindMapItem renders children by recursively nesting unordered lists
When isEnd is true, leaf nodes add an endline div so the final response connector can be drawn

description: Highlight the currently selected node in the mind map and in the source list.
heuristics: MindMapItem adds styles.active and a dot when item.name matches activeNode from context
ChatRight.highLightSearchList marks selected result ids with highLight true and sorts them to the top

description: Disable node and panel interaction while the final answer is still streaming.
heuristics: MindMapItem adds styles.forbidden when isEnd is true and chatIsOver is false
ChatRight.hideRight returns early if isOutputing is true

description: Render markdown responses while allowing injected HTML wrappers for citations.
heuristics: CustomMarkdown uses ReactMarkdown with rehypeRaw enabled
When refList exists, it swaps in custom i and span renderers for citation markup

description: Convert single [[id]] citations into clickable inline markers.
heuristics: replaceStr rewrites every [[n]] marker into an i tag with class f custom
CustomMarkdown maps those custom i tags to Popover-backed citation chips when refList is present

description: Collapse consecutive [[id]][[id]] citations into one grouped popover marker.
heuristics: mergeReplaceToDiv matches runs of consecutive [[n]] tokens with one regex and extracts the numbers
It emits a span with class mergeQuo and a comma-separated data-ids payload for the grouped popover

description: Show source metadata in popovers when the user hovers a citation marker.
heuristics: CustomI popovers list the matched refList item url, title, and summ fields
CustomDiv popovers build grouped citation lists from props data-ids split by comma

description: Emphasize the source cards chosen for synthesis inside the reasoning drawer.
heuristics: highLightSearchList sets item.highLight true for ids included in selectedIds
SearchItem applies styles.highLight when item.highLight is truthy

description: Let users expand and collapse the thinking, reading, and synthesis sections.
heuristics: ChatRight stores collapseInfo as a three-item boolean array and flips entries in toggleCard(index)
Selecting sources expands the first two sections and keeps synthesis open with setCollapseInfo([false, false, true])

description: Render each search result as a clickable source card with url, title, and summary.
heuristics: SearchItem shows item.id, item.url, item.title, and item.summ in one card
Clicking a SearchItem calls window.open(item.url)

description: Parse SSE chunks from the backend into node-specific demo updates.
heuristics: predict.streaming strips the data prefix and skips ping heartbeat lines
Each decoded chunk yields current_node, the active node payload, and the adjacency list

description: Render planner thoughts, code, and execution results as separate Gradio chat messages.
heuristics: format_response inserts a Code Interpreter message when planner stream_state enters CODING
On CODE_RETURN or PLUGIN_RETURN it inserts an Execution message followed by a blank assistant slot

description: Render searcher thoughts, plugin calls, and observations in per-node Gradio threads.
heuristics: predict keys each searcher thread by a composite string of node name and node question
PLUGIN_START and PLUGIN_END messages are rendered as JSON under a Web Browser thought label

description: Draw the planner graph as an image whenever the adjacency list grows.
heuristics: create_search_graph lays out nodes breadth-first with root at x 0 and 5-unit horizontal layers
Edges to response are dashed grey arrows, while ordinary edges are solid light-blue arrows

description: Refresh the graph image only when the deduplicated node set changes.
heuristics: predict builds dedup_nodes from adjacency_list keys plus every edge target name
It redraws only when len(dedup_nodes) differs from node_cnt

description: Keep each search node bound to its own chat thread inside the demo.
heuristics: predict stores node_id2msg_idx so later chunks for the same node update the same message region
After insertions, it shifts later indexes by the number of newly inserted messages

description: Clear planner history and reset node count between demo runs.
heuristics: rst_mem clears the module-level PLANNER_HISTORY list if it is non-empty
rst_mem returns empty planner history, empty searcher history, and node count 0

description: Populate the Gradio demo with canned example questions users can click.
heuristics: The Gradio UI wires examples into gr.Examples with three preset prompts
Submitting an example routes through the same user handler as typed input

description: Draw the live search graph in Streamlit using a pyvis network.
heuristics: create_network_graph uses Network with height 500px, width 60 percent, and white background
Every node is added with color #FF5733 and size 25, then saved to a temp HTML file

description: Persist queries, responses, graphs, and node traces in st.session_state.
heuristics: The app initializes queries, responses, graphs_html, nodes_list, adjacency_list_list, history, and already_used_keys
Per-node traces are stored under dynamic keys like selected_node plus _info

description: Update the planner markdown pane as planner thought and code chunks arrive.
heuristics: For planner chunks, update_chat accumulates formatted thought and optional action text into session_info_temp
When CODE_RETURN arrives, it appends the accumulated planner text to responses[-1] and resets session_info_temp

description: Update the selected node pane as searcher thought and observation chunks stream in.
heuristics: STREAM_ING updates write response.formatted.thought into the last thought entry
PLUGIN_RETURN appends a new observation item, then starts a fresh blank thought entry

description: Let the user switch between node traces with a selectbox keyed per conversation and node.
heuristics: update_chat creates selected_node_key from the conversation count and the node name
display_chat_history replays the last selected node by reusing the stored key in already_used_keys

description: Replay the latest stored Streamlit conversation after rerenders.
heuristics: display_chat_history only iterates over the last query in session_state
It restores the saved graph HTML, planner markdown, and per-node observation expanders from session_state

description: Clear all stored Streamlit transcript state and dynamic placeholders.
heuristics: clean_history resets the main session_state lists and already_used_keys to empty
It deletes every session_state key ending with placeholder or _info

description: Model chat turns as typed Gradio objects with explicit user or assistant roles.
heuristics: ChatMessage extends Message with a required string content field
Message.role is restricted to the literals user and assistant

description: Model chatbot file attachments as first-class messages with FileData payloads.
heuristics: ChatFileMessage stores a FileData object plus optional alt_text
AgentChatbot._postprocess_chat_messages moves file paths into Gradio block cache before returning them

description: Attach tool labels and error flags to messages so the demo can style execution steps.
heuristics: ThoughtMetadata carries tool_name and error fields
format_response sets tool_name to labels like Code Interpreter, Web Browser, and Execution

description: Postprocess chatbot output so file messages are cached and returned in a ChatbotData envelope.
heuristics: AgentChatbot.postprocess flattens each message through _postprocess_chat_messages before wrapping it in ChatbotData
File messages are cached via move_resource_to_block_cache(path, block=self)

description: Wire textbox, buttons, and state objects into the Gradio chat event pipeline.
heuristics: ChatInterface._setup_events chains clear and save textbox, display input, then submit or stream response
The same submit pipeline is bound to textbox.submit and submit_btn.click

description: Provide retry, undo, and clear controls that mutate chat history correctly.
heuristics: retry reuses _delete_prev_fn, then re-displays the saved input and reruns submit_fn
clear_btn resets chatbot, chatbot_state, and saved_input to empty values in one click handler

description: Expose the chat interface as a programmatic Gradio API endpoint.
heuristics: ChatInterface._setup_api binds fake_api_btn to api_fn with api_name chat
The API returns JSON output plus updated chatbot_state for both streaming and non-streaming modes

description: Choose between docker compose and legacy docker-compose before launching.
heuristics: get_docker_command first runs docker compose version and falls back to docker-compose --version
If both checks fail, it prints DOCKER_COMPOSE_NOT_FOUND and exits

description: Verify that Docker and the chosen compose command are installed and runnable.
heuristics: check_docker_install runs docker --version plus the detected compose command with version
It returns False on FileNotFoundError or CalledProcessError instead of raising

description: Create the temp working directory where compose files and Dockerfiles are staged.
heuristics: config.py sets TEMP_DIR by calling FileSystemManager.ensure_dir on PACKAGE_DIR / temp
main creates TEMP_DIR again if missing before copying any templates

description: Copy launcher template files into the temp workspace before customization.
heuristics: copy_templates_to_temp copies every file from PACKAGE_DIR templates into TEMP_DIR
If a template is missing, it prints FILE_NOT_FOUND and exits with sys.exit(1)

description: Stage the selected backend Dockerfile as temp backend.dockerfile.
heuristics: copy_backend_dockerfile reads templates backend choice and writes it to TEMP_DIR backend.dockerfile
It raises FileNotFoundError if the source Dockerfile does not exist

description: Stage the React frontend Dockerfile as temp frontend.dockerfile.
heuristics: copy_frontend_dockerfile copies templates frontend react.dockerfile into TEMP_DIR frontend.dockerfile

description: Rewrite compose build contexts and Dockerfile paths to absolute temp and project paths.
heuristics: update_docker_compose_paths replaces build context dot-dot with PROJECT_ROOT
It rewrites every build dockerfile path to TEMP_DIR joined with the dockerfile basename

description: Rewrite the backend compose command with the chosen runtime flags.
heuristics: modify_docker_compose sets backend command to run python -m mindsearch.app with lang, model_format, and search_engine
It also forces backend env_file to include .env

description: Strip local-only settings when the user chooses a cloud-hosted model backend.
heuristics: For CLOUD_LLM_DOCKERFILE, modify_docker_compose deletes backend deploy and volumes if present

description: Add GPU reservations and cache mounts for a local model deployment.
heuristics: For LOCAL_LLM_DOCKERFILE, modify_docker_compose adds an nvidia device reservation with count 1
It also mounts /root/.cache:/root/.cache for local inference assets

description: Launch the backend and frontend stack with build and env-file support.
heuristics: run_docker_compose executes compose up -d --build against TEMP_DIR docker-compose.yaml
It passes --env-file TEMP_DIR .env and exits after cleanup if compose fails

description: Stop compose services and remove leftover project containers and networks.
heuristics: stop_and_remove_containers runs compose down -v --remove-orphans against the temp compose file
It also scans docker ps -a for names starting with the project prefix or service names and force removes them

description: Ensure launcher temp and translation directories exist before use.
heuristics: FileSystemManager.ensure_dir creates missing directories with parents=True and exist_ok=True
config.py eagerly creates TEMP_DIR and TRANSLATIONS_DIR at import time

description: Ensure the launcher .env file exists before reading or writing keys.
heuristics: FileSystemManager.ensure_file creates parent directories first and writes default_content when the file is missing
config.py sets ENV_FILE_PATH by ensuring TEMP_DIR slash .env exists

description: Read launcher configuration from the temp .env file with caching.
heuristics: utils.get_env_variable scans ENV_FILE_PATH line by line and falls back to os.getenv
read_env_file ignores commented lines and strips surrounding quotes from values

description: Validate and persist model or search API keys into the launcher .env file.
heuristics: save_api_key_to_env maps model formats like gpt4 to env vars such as OPENAI_API_KEY before writing
It rewrites the entire .env file as KEY=value lines after merging the new key

description: Validate API keys against provider-specific regex patterns before saving.
heuristics: validate_api_key uses a sk-prefixed pattern for OPENAI, QWEN, and SILICON model keys
Search keys use broader patterns, while Tencent secret id and key allow only alphanumerics

description: Normalize pasted API keys by trimming and removing embedded whitespace.
heuristics: clean_api_key does api_key.strip() and then removes internal whitespace with re.sub

description: List supported model formats for the selected cloud or local deployment type.
heuristics: get_model_formats returns internlm_silicon, qwen, and gpt4 for cloud deployment
It returns internlm_server, internlm_client, and internlm_hf for local deployment

description: Prompt for the launcher UI language before any other interactive choice.
heuristics: get_language_choice only prompts when LAUNCHER_INTERACTION_LANGUAGE is unset
It offers English and 中文 labels mapped to en and zh_CN

description: Prompt for the backend agent language independently from the launcher language.
heuristics: get_backend_language offers translated choices that map to backend values cn and en
The default backend language choice is cn

description: Prompt for cloud versus local model deployment using the launcher Dockerfiles.
heuristics: get_model_choice returns either CLOUD_LLM_DOCKERFILE or LOCAL_LLM_DOCKERFILE

description: Prompt for a concrete model format after the deployment type is chosen.
heuristics: get_model_format reads the valid options from get_model_formats(model)
Each select choice uses the same string as both its label and value

description: Prompt for a search engine and collect any required credentials.
heuristics: SEARCH_ENGINES marks DuckDuckGo as no-key and Tencent as requiring two env vars
get_search_engine prints SEARCH_ENGINE_CONFIGURED with the human-readable engine name after configuration

description: Offer to reuse stored API keys before prompting the user to replace them.
heuristics: handle_api_key_input asks CONFIRM_USE_EXISTING_API_KEY when a model key already exists
get_search_engine does the same check for search-engine credentials before calling the input helper

description: Persist the chosen launcher language into .env and update i18n immediately.
heuristics: set_language writes LAUNCHER_INTERACTION_LANGUAGE through set_env_variable
It then calls i18n.set locale to the selected language code

description: Load translated CLI strings from yaml files and expose them through a helper.
heuristics: setup_i18n appends TRANSLATIONS_DIR to i18n.load_path and uses filename_format locale.format
t(key, **kwargs) is a thin wrapper around i18n.t(key, **kwargs)

description: Restart the launcher process after a language change so prompts use the new locale.
heuristics: restart_program prints LANGUAGE_CHANGED_RESTARTING and then calls os.execl(python, python, *sys.argv)

description: Show how to consume the backend SSE stream directly from a Python client.
heuristics: backend_example posts to http://localhost:8002/solve with stream=True
It strips the data prefix and ping lines before json.loads(decoded)

description: Run a one-off terminal debugging session with a local MindSearchAgent instance.
heuristics: mindsearch.terminal wires LMDeployServer plus WebBrowser BingSearch topk 6 into MindSearchAgent
The script executes a hardcoded Shanghai weather query by iterating over agent output

description: Maps reasoning effort to token budget and retry count before the agent loop runs.
heuristics: reasoning_effort maps to low=100000/1, medium=500000/2, high=1000000/4 for budget and retries.
max_completion_tokens overrides the effort-based budget and forces maxBadAttempts=1.
The agent reserves 85% of the budget for the regular loop and keeps the rest for beast mode.

description: Builds the action-selection system prompt from context, URLs, limits, and beast mode.
heuristics: Visit prompts include a ranked <url-list> built from the top 20 scored URLs.
Search instructions prefer a single query and only add more when the question has distinct aspects.
Beast mode removes all tool actions and injects an answer-only override prompt.

description: Turns prior knowledge into messages so the model reasons over gathered evidence.
heuristics: Each knowledge item becomes a user question followed by an assistant answer message.
url and side-info items embed <answer-datetime> when updated is present.
url knowledge also embeds its first reference inside a <url> tag.

description: Picks one schema-bound action among search, visit, reflect, answer, or coding each step.
heuristics: Allowed actions are gated by state, such as weighted URL count, freshness checks, and prior failures.
The schema requires exactly one action enum value and only the matching payload object.
After a search step, answer is disabled for the immediate next step to avoid snippet-only answers.

description: Splits a question into orthogonal subproblems and merges their parallel answers.
heuristics: Parallel planning only runs when teamSize > 1 and the search step has produced soundbites.
The planner schema must return exactly teamSize subproblems.
Subresponses run with teamSize reset to 1, then references are deduped by URL before merge.

description: Forces a last-resort final answer when retries or budget are exhausted.
heuristics: The fallback runs when no final answer passed evaluation before the regular budget is spent.
Beast mode uses the agentBeastMode model with only answer enabled in the schema.
Its result is always marked isFinal=true, even if earlier checks failed.

description: Breaks the current question into a few new gap-filling subquestions.
heuristics: Reflect results are deduped and then chooseK-limited to MAX_REFLECT_PER_STEP=2.
Reflection is disabled whenever the current gaps array already exceeds MAX_REFLECT_PER_STEP.
The reflect schema explicitly bans generic meta-questions about verification or source reliability.

description: Removes near-duplicate questions or searches by embedding similarity.
heuristics: New and existing queries are embedded together in one batch before comparison.
A query is dropped when cosine similarity is at least 0.86 to an existing or accepted query.
If embedding calls fail or return empty data, all new queries are kept.

description: Expands a search request into short, orthogonal SERP queries with recency metadata.
heuristics: The query schema caps output at MAX_QUERIES_PER_STEP=5.
Each q should stay short, under 50 chars, and prefer keyword-style phrasing.
The prompt asks for seven persona-driven rewrites, including time-aware and native-language variants.

description: Decides whether a question needs definitive, fresh, plural, or complete checks.
heuristics: Completeness takes precedence over plurality when the question names explicit elements.
Freshness is triggered for current/latest/recent wording and time-sensitive domains.
The original question always gets an added strict evaluator with maxBadAttempts retries.

description: Groups SERP snippets into insight clusters that suggest concrete follow-up questions.
heuristics: The clustering schema limits output to MAX_CLUSTERS=5.
Each cluster must include insight, question, and urls fields.
executeSearchQueries stores every returned cluster as a knowledge item of type 'url'.

description: Summarizes why a search trajectory failed and suggests a better next move.
heuristics: The analyzer always returns recap, blame, and improvement fields.
Its output is added back into knowledge after a rejected answer.
Both blame and improvement are emitted as think updates through the action tracker.

description: Routes a SERP query to Jina, Arxiv, DuckDuckGo, Brave, or Serper.
heuristics: jina and arxiv requests call the Jina search API, with num=30 unless meta is set.
DuckDuckGo search is always run with SafeSearchType.STRICT.
A 401 from jina or arxiv is rewritten into 'Unauthorized Jina API key'.

description: Normalizes fetched snippets into a shared URL store and merges duplicates.
heuristics: Incoming URLs are normalized before they are used as allURLs keys.
A duplicate URL increments its weight instead of creating another record.
Descriptions for duplicates are merged with smartMergeStrings to preserve overlap.

description: Scores candidate URLs by frequency, path signals, boosts, and reranker hints.
heuristics: finalScore clamps the combined boosts between minBoost=0 and maxBoost=5.
Hosts in boostHostnames get an extra +2 added into hostnameBoost.
Identical title+description strings are deduped before rerankDocuments runs asynchronously.

description: Caps results per hostname so one site cannot dominate the visit queue.
heuristics: keepKPerHostname returns the original list unchanged when only one hostname exists.
Otherwise it keeps at most k results per hostname while preserving ranked order.
The agent applies this with k=2 before presenting visit candidates.

description: Reads full page content through Reader and exposes links and images.
heuristics: readUrl rejects empty strings and non-http(s) URLs before sending any request.
It sets X-With-Links-Summary=all when link extraction is requested.
Images are stripped with X-Retain-Images=none unless withAllImages is enabled.

description: Drops likely paywall or bot-block pages before they pollute knowledge.
heuristics: Only short content is classified, using a spamDetectLength threshold of 300 chars.
Pages pass automatically when content length is above 300 chars.
Hostnames from DNS, cert, or connection failures are recorded and later purged from allURLs.

description: Promotes page outlinks into the URL pool for future follow-up visits.
heuristics: Every extracted page link is normalized before being considered.
In-page links are added with weightDelta=0.1 instead of the default search weight 1.
Visited URLs are recorded even when a read later fails.

description: Canonicalizes URLs so search, read, and citation logic use stable keys.
heuristics: It strips utm_, ref, gclid, session IDs, default ports, and optional anchors.
It rejects google or baidu search-result links, example.com, and non-http(s) schemes.
twitter.com or x.com status URLs have trailing /analytics removed.

description: Converts host filters and URLs into comparable lowercase hostnames.
heuristics: Leading '*.' wildcard prefixes are stripped before comparison.
Bare host strings have 'www.' removed and are lowercased.
Full URLs are parsed for hostname, with fallback to cleaned input on parse failure.

description: Pulls URLs from chat text and keeps nearby text as a lightweight snippet.
heuristics: The regex only matches http/https URLs and trims trailing punctuation like '.' or ','.
Context windows default to 50 chars on each side of the URL.
Adjacent URL contexts are clipped to avoid overlap with neighboring matches.

description: Splits text into reusable chunks and records offsets for later matching.
heuristics: The default chunk mode is newline with minChunkLength=80.
It also supports punctuation, fixed-character, and regex splitting modes.
Chunks shorter than the minimum length are filtered out and get no stored position.

description: Selects the most question-relevant long-page snippets after a page is read.
heuristics: If the page is shorter than snippetLength*2, the whole page is returned unchanged.
Defaults are snippetLength=6000 chars and chunkSize=300 chars, with 2-5 snippets.
Used snippet windows are set to -Infinity so the same passage is not reused.

description: Adds best-guess content dates to knowledge and citations when confidence is high.
heuristics: getLastModified calls api-beta-datetime.jina.ai with a 10s timeout.
It only accepts bestGuess when confidence is at least 70.
qdr:h/d/w/m/y search filters are rendered into 'Between start and end' date ranges.

description: Matches answer chunks to source chunks so citations point at relevant evidence.
heuristics: Answer and web chunks shorter than minChunkLength=80 are ignored.
Matches must clear minRelScore and remain unique across both answer and web chunks.
If embeddings fail, the matcher falls back to Jaccard ranking instead.

description: Inserts footnote markers and repairs footnote blocks for clean cited output.
heuristics: Reference markers are inserted by answer-chunk position, with list and table edge-case handling.
Repeated or out-of-range footnote indices are renumbered sequentially.
When there are references but no inline markers, all citations are appended after a dedicated marker line.

description: Rejects answers that hedge, dodge the question, or defer instead of answering.
heuristics: Phrases like 'I don't know' or 'I cannot provide' are defined as non-definitive.
Balanced multi-perspective answers can still pass if they directly address the question.
The evaluation loop stops immediately on the first failed check.

description: Rejects answers whose dates are too old for the question's freshness demands.
heuristics: The evaluator compares answer dates against the current ISO timestamp.
Its rubric ranges from 0.1 days for real-time finance to infinity for static facts.
pass must be true only when days_ago is less than or equal to max_age_days.

description: Checks whether an answer returns enough distinct items for list-like questions.
heuristics: The prompt maps cues like 'few', 'many', 'top N', and 'examples' to expected counts.
Plurality only runs when completeness did not already trigger.
The response reports minimum_count_required and actual_count_provided.

description: Checks that every explicitly named aspect or entity in the question is covered.
heuristics: Only explicit named aspects count; implicit but relevant angles are ignored.
Conceptual matches via synonyms or paraphrases are acceptable.
Missing any explicitly named aspect causes the answer to fail.

description: Runs a rejection-biased final reviewer and loops improvements back into drafting.
heuristics: The original question always gets a strict evaluator with maxBadAttempts retries.
strict improvement_plan strings are injected back into later prompts as <reviewer-n> blocks.
When all strict retries are exhausted, the agent falls through to beast mode.

description: Polishes the drafted answer into publishable prose without dropping grounded facts.
heuristics: The finalizer bans bullet and numbered lists in its output instructions.
If the revised text is under 85% of the original length, the original draft is kept.
HTML tables must stay unfenced and use basic <table> syntax only.

description: Merges multiple subanswers into one article-style answer while pruning redundancy.
heuristics: If reduced output is under 60% of the total source length, it falls back to simple joining.
The reducer prompt says to preserve original sentences verbatim when possible.
Aggregated answers are currently joined directly in agent.ts instead of calling reduceAnswers().

description: Batches text or image inputs through Jina embeddings and tracks token usage.
heuristics: Embedding requests are processed in batches of 32 items.
Empty or all-blank input returns an empty embedding list and zero tokens immediately.
jina-embeddings-v3 defaults to task='text-matching' with truncate=true.

description: Retries only the embedding indices the API failed to return, then fills gaps safely.
heuristics: Missing indices are retried up to MAX_RETRIES=3.
indexMap remaps retried positions back to the original batch order.
After final failure, zero vectors of the target dimension are inserted as placeholders.

description: Reranks documents against a query using Jina's multilingual reranker.
heuristics: Documents are processed in parallel batches of up to 2000 items.
Each batch asks for top_n equal to the batch length and remaps scores to original indices.
On error, rerankDocuments returns an empty results array instead of throwing.

description: Computes vector cosine similarity for query, citation, and image matching.
heuristics: It throws when the two vectors have different lengths.
If either vector has zero magnitude, it returns 0.
The score is the dot product divided by the product of both norms.

description: Falls back to token-overlap ranking when embeddings cannot be trusted.
heuristics: Both query and docs are lowercased and split on non-word characters.
Scores use intersection size divided by union size of token sets.
Results are returned sorted by descending relevance_score.

description: Asks the coder model for a self-contained JavaScript return statement.
heuristics: The prompt forbids third-party libraries and requires a return statement.
Available variables are described from live context via analyzeStructure().
Previous failed code and errors are fed back into the next generation attempt.

description: Executes generated JavaScript against live context and retries on errors.
heuristics: Code runs in new Function('context', ...) with a with(context) wrapper.
Returning undefined is treated as a failure with an explicit error message.
solve retries up to maxAttempts=3 before throwing.

description: Compresses runtime data into a typed shape summary for code generation.
heuristics: Strings include cleaned example values up to 50 characters.
Arrays are summarized from their first item as Array<...>.
Functions are labeled 'Function' and nested objects are recursively expanded.

description: Downloads, validates, resizes, and embeds candidate images before ranking.
heuristics: downloadFile rejects files over 100MB and any non-image content type.
Images must be at least 256x256 and are resized to fit inside a square box.
processImage embeds the result with jina-clip-v2 at 512 dimensions.

description: Matches answer chunks to image embeddings so only relevant images survive.
heuristics: Answer chunks shorter than 80 chars are ignored before image matching.
Answer text is embedded with jina-clip-v2 using 512 dimensions.
Only matches above minRelScore=0.35 are kept, with uniqueness across chunks and images.

description: Drops near-duplicate images by embedding similarity before response assembly.
heuristics: The similarity threshold defaults to 0.86.
Each new image is checked against existing images and already accepted new images.
If deduplication errors, the function returns all new images unchanged.

description: Keeps only deduped, high-scoring image URLs in the final response payload.
heuristics: Aggregated answers sort imageReferences by descending relevanceScore before filtering.
filterImages maps deduped image URLs back to their original reference objects.
Final aggregated responses cap returned images at 10.

description: Validates chat requests before research starts and rejects malformed state.
heuristics: messages must be an array with at least one element.
The last message must have role='user' or the request is rejected.
express.json limits request bodies to 10mb.

description: Normalizes prior messages so reasoning tags and images don't corrupt prompts.
heuristics: Assistant <think>...</think> blocks are stripped before messages are reused.
User image_url parts are rewritten to {type:'image', image:url} objects.
System message content arrays are flattened into a plain string.

description: Streams think text in human-like chunks with delays for CJK, URLs, and punctuation.
heuristics: URLs are emitted as whole chunks once https?:// is detected.
Each CJK character is split into its own streamed chunk.
Sentence-ending punctuation adds up to about 200ms of extra delay.

description: Publishes search queries, visited URLs, and thought updates as SSE progress.
heuristics: Search actions emit each searchRequests entry as delta.query.
Visit actions emit each URLTargets entry as delta.url.
Think updates are queued, suffixed with a space, and serialized through processQueue().

description: Serves a fixed OpenAI-compatible model list for clients that enumerate models.
heuristics: /v1/models returns jina-deepsearch-v1 and jina-deepsearch-v2.
Unknown /v1/models/:model requests return 404 with code 'model_not_found'.
Both advertised models are owned_by 'jina-ai'.

description: Re-extracts the final answer into a caller-supplied JSON schema when asked.
heuristics: It only runs when response_format.json_schema is present on the request.
jsonSchema conversion failures return HTTP 400 before research continues.
Successful extraction replaces finalAnswer with a pretty-printed JSON string.

description: Packages citations, usage, URL lists, and related images onto responses.
heuristics: Annotations only include references that have url, title, exactQuote, and dateTime.
Both streaming and non-streaming responses include usage, visitedURLs, readURLs, and numURLs.
relatedImages is only included when with_images is true.

description: Accumulates tool token usage and exposes OpenAI-style and internal totals.
heuristics: Every trackUsage call emits a 'usage' event.
If asyncLocalContext is available, chargeAmount is updated from totalTokens on each usage event.
getUsageBreakdown sums totalTokens by tool name.

description: Tracks the current step state and emits progress events for the UI layer.
heuristics: trackAction merges partial state and emits the current thisStep object.
trackThink can localize named status keys through getI18nText when a language is provided.
reset restores an empty answer action with totalStep=0 and no gaps.

description: Formats logs as structured JSON with severity, component, trace, and source fields.
heuristics: Every log entry includes component='deepsearch' and an ISO timestamp.
Error objects are expanded into message, stack, and name.
On Cloud Run, traces are emitted as projects/$GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT/traces/$K_REVISION.

description: Uses a shared Axios client with timeouts, keep-alive pools, size caps, and error rewriting.
heuristics: The shared client defaults to 30000ms timeout, 10MB maxContentLength, and 5 redirects.
HTTP and HTTPS agents keep 200 sockets, 50 free sockets, and keepAlive enabled.
402 responses are rewritten to an 'Insufficient balance' error.

description: Starts a per-request async context for billing, traces, and saved prompt artifacts.
heuristics: traceId is taken from x-request-id, request-id, x-cloud-trace-context, or randomUUID().
The wrapper stores traceT0 and client ip before calling next().
Later billing and persistence read chargeAmount and promptContext from process.asyncLocalContext.

description: Requires a Bearer token and resolves it to a cached Jina account and UID.
heuristics: The middleware rejects requests without Authorization: Bearer ... before reaching the app.
The auth DTO extracts the token from Authorization or from an input _token field.
401 validation failures are rewritten to 'Invalid API key, please get a new one from https://jina.ai'.

description: Caches token-account profiles in Firestore so requests can skip repeat billing lookups.
heuristics: Cached accounts are keyed by the bearer token document id in Firestore.
Profiles younger than 180000ms are reused unless ignoreCache is requested.
Fresh validateToken results are merged back with an updated lastSyncedAt timestamp.

description: Applies rolling per-UID or per-IP limits before a research request is processed.
heuristics: Authenticated users default to 500 RPM when speed_level>=2, else 50 RPM.
Anonymous callers get a zero-quota policy over 120 seconds.
Limit checks count SUCCESS and PENDING calls since pointInTime and return Retry-After on overflow.

description: Stores prompt context and grounded knowledge so hosted runs can be audited later.
heuristics: Authenticated knowledge items are saved to Firestore with uid and traceId.
promptContext is uploaded to Cloud Storage under promptContext/{traceId}.json.
Before serialization, each context.result field is blanked out.

description: Confirms the request is research-ready and emits a start message when it is.
heuristics: Formats the clarification prompt from the buffered message history plus today's date
If clarification is not needed, appends the model's verification message
Routes straight to write_research_brief after verification

description: Turns the conversation into a structured research brief for downstream agents.
heuristics: Uses structured ResearchQuestion output with retries
Builds the prompt from get_buffer_string(state messages) and get_today_str()
Stores response.research_brief into state before supervisor starts

description: Initializes supervisor state with a system prompt and the generated brief.
heuristics: Creates lead_researcher_prompt with date, max_concurrent_research_units, and max_researcher_iterations
Overrides supervisor_messages with a SystemMessage plus HumanMessage(research_brief)
Uses override semantics instead of appending old supervisor state

description: Generates web queries to gather context for planning report sections.
heuristics: Uses number_of_queries from legacy Configuration
Formats report_planner_query_writer_instructions with topic, report structure, and today
Calls the structured Queries tool before any planning search

description: Generates section-specific search queries from a section description.
heuristics: Uses section.description, not the section name, as the query-writing topic
Formats query_writer_instructions with topic, section_topic, number_of_queries, and today
Returns queries in structured Queries format

description: Builds a concise report plan with section metadata from search context.
heuristics: Feeds planner the search-derived source_str plus any accumulated feedback
For claude-3-7-sonnet-latest, allocates max_tokens=20000 and thinking budget 16000
Requires Sections structured output with name, description, research, and content fields

description: Interrupts for human approval or revision notes on the proposed section plan.
heuristics: Formats each section as name, description, and research-needed flag
Treats boolean true as approval and any string as regeneration feedback
Uses interrupt() to pause the graph until review input arrives

description: Replans sections when the reviewer supplies textual feedback.
heuristics: Appends feedback strings to feedback_on_report_plan instead of replacing history
Joins prior feedback with ' /// ' before re-planning
Routes back to generate_report_plan on string feedback

description: Lets the legacy supervisor ask a follow-up scope question and stop for user input.
heuristics: Adds the Question tool only when ask_for_clarification is true
When Question is called, appends the question as an assistant message and ends
Question text comes from a structured Pydantic tool output

description: Formats the current date for prompts and report-generation context.
heuristics: New utils format dates like 'Mon Jan 15, 2024' via manual f-string
Legacy utils format dates with strftime('%a %b %-d, %Y')
Prompts throughout planning, summarization, and evaluation inject this formatted date

description: Hands a detailed research topic from the supervisor to a researcher subgraph.
heuristics: Supervisor exposes ConductResearch as a tool-bound structured output
ConductResearch.research_topic is documented as a single detailed paragraph topic
Supervisor_tools invokes researcher_subgraph with HumanMessage(research_topic)

description: Records explicit reflection steps so search loops pause and reassess progress.
heuristics: think_tool returns 'Reflection recorded: {reflection}'
Supervisor_tools converts think_tool calls into ToolMessages without extra execution
The tool is available to both supervisor and researcher toolkits

description: Stops a research loop when completion signals or exit conditions are met.
heuristics: Supervisor exits on no tool calls, ResearchComplete, or too many iterations
Researcher exits to compression when there are no tool calls and no native web search
Researcher also compresses when ResearchComplete appears in tool calls

description: Enforces a hard ceiling on supervisor reflection and delegation cycles.
heuristics: Supervisor increments research_iterations on every model turn
supervisor_tools ends research when research_iterations > max_researcher_iterations
On forced exit it still preserves notes extracted from prior tool messages

description: Limits how many research tasks the supervisor can run at once.
heuristics: Slices ConductResearch calls to configurable.max_concurrent_research_units
Executes only the allowed slice in parallel
Returns explicit ToolMessage errors for overflow research calls

description: Runs multiple researcher subgraphs concurrently and collects their outputs.
heuristics: Builds one researcher_subgraph.ainvoke task per allowed ConductResearch call
Uses asyncio.gather to run all researcher tasks together
Concatenates returned raw_notes across researcher results into one state field

description: Builds the researcher toolset from core tools, search, and MCP integrations.
heuristics: Starts with ResearchComplete and think_tool as core tools
Extends with exactly one configured search backend via get_search_tool
Raises a ValueError in researcher() if the assembled tool list is empty

description: Builds the supervisor toolset for planning, delegation, and completion.
heuristics: New supervisor binds [ConductResearch, ResearchComplete, think_tool]
Legacy multi-agent supervisor binds Sections, Introduction, Conclusion, and FinishReport
Legacy optionally adds Question and search tools based on configuration

description: Builds the legacy section-research toolset for writing and search.
heuristics: Legacy get_research_tools starts with Section and FinishResearch tools
It appends a search tool unless search_api is 'none'
It then appends non-conflicting MCP tools filtered by inclusion config

description: Executes tool calls and converts observations back into tool messages.
heuristics: New researcher_tools launches all tool invocations with asyncio.gather
Legacy supervisor_tools processes every tool call before deciding the next state
Both paths preserve the original tool_call_id on returned tool messages

description: Wraps async tool execution so failures become observations instead of crashes.
heuristics: Calls await tool.ainvoke(args, config) inside a try/except
On exception returns the string 'Error executing tool: ...'
Used by researcher_tools before building ToolMessage outputs

description: Decides whether the supervisor should keep looping after each turn.
heuristics: Legacy supervisor_should_continue returns END when the last message has no tool_calls
Otherwise it routes to supervisor_tools
New supervisor_tools routes back to supervisor after processing tool results

description: Keeps a researcher iterating until it can finish or compress findings.
heuristics: Legacy research_agent_should_continue ends only when the first tool is FinishResearch
New researcher_tools returns to researcher unless max_react_tool_calls is hit
If a late exit triggers, new researcher_tools sends collected tool outputs to compression

description: Fans out approved sections into parallel section-writing or research tasks.
heuristics: human_feedback sends one build_section_with_web_research task per section with research=True
legacy multi_agent supervisor sends one research_team task per planned section string
Both use LangGraph Send() to parallelize downstream work

description: Publishes a finished section back into shared report state.
heuristics: legacy write_section updates completed_sections with the finished Section object
If include_source_str is enabled it also stores source_str alongside the section
legacy research_agent_tools writes completed_sections when the Section tool returns

description: Chooses the configured search provider and exposes it in tool form.
heuristics: New get_search_tool maps ANTHROPIC to web_search_20250305 with max_uses=5
OPENAI maps to web_search_preview and TAVILY maps to tavily_search metadata name='web_search'
Legacy select_and_execute_search dispatches across tavily, duckduckgo, perplexity, exa, arxiv, pubmed, linkup, googlesearch, and azureaisearch

description: Runs one or more Tavily searches and formats the results for research.
heuristics: Uses AsyncTavilyClient.search(query,max_results,include_raw_content,topic) per query
New tavily_search deduplicates results by URL before summarization
Legacy tavily_search can optionally summarize or split-and-rerank raw_content

description: Queries Azure AI Search with semantic plus vector retrieval settings.
heuristics: Requires AZURE_AI_SEARCH_ENDPOINT, AZURE_AI_SEARCH_INDEX_NAME, and AZURE_AI_SEARCH_API_KEY
Uses query_type='semantic' and semantic_configuration_name='fraunhofer-rag-semantic-config'
Selects url, title, chunk, creationTime, and lastModifiedTime fields

description: Calls the Perplexity chat API and normalizes citations into search results.
heuristics: Posts to https://api.perplexity.ai/chat/completions with model 'sonar-pro'
Stores the first citation with full content and raw_content score 1.0
Stores later citations as secondary sources with placeholder content and score 0.5

description: Runs Exa search and returns result summaries plus optional full text.
heuristics: Raises ValueError if include_domains and exclude_domains are both set
Uses summary=True and text=True unless max_characters is provided
Adds a 0.25s delay between queries and a 1.0s extra delay after 429 errors

description: Adds Exa subpages without duplicating URLs already seen in main results.
heuristics: Processes subpages only when the subpages parameter is not None
Tracks seen_urls across main results and subpages
Collects images only from main results to avoid duplicate image entries

description: Queries arXiv and formats paper metadata and optional full text.
heuristics: Creates a fresh ArxivRetriever per query with configurable load flags
Scores results by rank using a decreasing base_score scheme
Sleeps 3 seconds between queries to respect arXiv rate limits

description: Queries PubMed and formats summaries into normalized result records.
heuristics: Uses PubMedAPIWrapper.lazy_load in a thread pool instead of load()
Defaults email to 'your_email@example.com' when none is supplied
Builds each paper URL as https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/{uid}/

description: Adjusts PubMed pacing based on recent success or failure.
heuristics: Starts with a 1.0 second delay between queries
On successful results reduces delay toward a 0.5 second floor
On exceptions increases delay up to a 5.0 second ceiling

description: Runs Linkup searches and formats returned searchResults records.
heuristics: Calls LinkupClient.async_search(query, depth, output_type='searchResults')
Normalizes each result to title, url, and content only
Gathers multiple Linkup queries concurrently with asyncio.gather

description: Searches Google via Custom Search API or a scraping fallback.
heuristics: Uses the API only when both GOOGLE_API_KEY and GOOGLE_CX are set
API mode paginates in batches of up to 10 results with a 0.2s delay
Scraping mode uses a ThreadPoolExecutor and lower concurrency than API mode

description: Scrapes Google result cards into normalized records when API keys are absent.
heuristics: Sends requests with a randomized Lynx-style User-Agent string
Bypasses consent with CONSENT and SOCS cookies
Parses div.ezO2md blocks and decodes /url?q= links into clean URLs

description: Fetches full page text for search results and handles binary content safely.
heuristics: Limits concurrent content fetches with a semaphore of 3
Marks PDFs and octet-stream responses as unsupported binary content
Parses HTML text with BeautifulSoup and stores it back into raw_content

description: Downloads a list of result pages and emits a markdown-formatted source bundle.
heuristics: Uses httpx.AsyncClient with follow_redirects=True and timeout=30.0
Converts text/html pages to markdown with markdownify
Formats each page as '--- SOURCE N: title ---' with URL and full content

description: Runs DuckDuckGo searches with retries and returns scraped page bundles.
heuristics: Calls DDGS().text(modified_query,max_results=5) inside a thread pool
Builds simple descending scores 1.0, 0.9, 0.8 for returned results
If any URLs were found, it passes titles and URLs to scrape_pages

description: Uses exponential backoff and query mutation to survive DuckDuckGo rate limits.
heuristics: Retries up to 3 times with delay backoff_factor**retry_count plus randomness
Adds a random modifier like 'about' or 'overview' on retries
Stops retrying early for non-rate-limit errors

description: Loads MCP tools from configured servers with optional auth headers.
heuristics: Fetches MCP tokens first when mcp_config.auth_required is true
Builds server URL as mcp_config.url.rstrip('/') + '/mcp'
Returns [] when config is incomplete or the MCP client connection fails

description: Filters out MCP tools that clash with existing names or are not allowed.
heuristics: Skips any MCP tool whose name already exists in the active toolkit
Warns when a conflicting tool name is ignored
Keeps only tool names listed in mcp_config.tools or mcp_tools_to_include

description: Deduplicates search results by URL before formatting or summarizing them.
heuristics: legacy deduplicate_and_format_sources supports keep_first and keep_last strategies
Invalid deduplication_strategy values raise ValueError
New Tavily formatting also keeps only the first result seen for each URL

description: Formats normalized search results into a readable source bundle string.
heuristics: Legacy formatter truncates raw_content using roughly 4 characters per token
Each source block includes title, URL, relevant content, and optional raw content
New formatters emit numbered 'SOURCE N' sections separated by long dashed rules

description: Summarizes raw webpage text into compact findings plus key excerpts.
heuristics: New implementation wraps summarization in asyncio.wait_for(..., timeout=60.0)
On timeout or exception it falls back to the original webpage content
Legacy Anthropic requests use ephemeral cache_control ttl='1h' in the user message

description: Applies webpage summarization across retrieved search results when configured.
heuristics: Legacy Tavily post-processing only summarizes results that have raw_content
Legacy summarization path caps input at 30000 characters per result
New Tavily path caps raw_content with configurable.max_content_length

description: Splits pages into chunks and retrieves the most query-relevant ones.
