heuristics: Uses RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter with chunk_size=1500 and chunk_overlap=200
Indexes splits in an InMemoryVectorStore
Runs similarity_search(query,k=max_chunks) over embedded chunks

description: Reassembles retrieved chunks into one stitched document per URL.
heuristics: Hashes each chunk with sha256(page_content) to dedupe per URL
Keeps URL-grouped chunks in insertion order
Wraps each stitched chunk as '...chunk...' before joining them

description: Detects whether the model used built-in provider web search instead of tools.
heuristics: OpenAI detection scans additional_kwargs.tool_outputs for type='web_search_call'
Anthropic detection reads usage.server_tool_use.web_search_requests
Unexpected metadata shapes return False instead of raising

description: Pulls plain-text notes out of prior tool messages for synthesis.
heuristics: Uses filter_messages(messages, include_types='tool')
Returns only tool_msg.content values
Supervisor exit paths use this helper to populate notes

description: Aggregates raw researcher and tool outputs into a single notes stream.
heuristics: Supervisor concatenates every observation.raw_notes with newline separators
compress_research concatenates both tool and AI message contents
Aggregated raw notes are stored as a single-item list in state

description: Compresses a researcher's message trace into a concise research summary.
heuristics: Appends compress_research_simple_human_message before synthesis
Runs up to 3 synthesis attempts with a dedicated compression model
Returns both compressed_research text and raw_notes content

description: Shrinks context when models hit prompt or context-length limits.
heuristics: compress_research trims message history to before the last AIMessage
final_report_generation first truncates findings to model_token_limit*4 chars
Later report retries reduce the current findings cap by 10 percent

description: Formats finished sections into reusable context for later writing steps.
heuristics: legacy format_sections prints section number, description, research flag, and content
Missing section content is rendered as '[Not yet written]'
gather_completed_sections uses this formatted string to seed final sections

description: Writes a researched body section from gathered source material.
heuristics: Uses section_writer_instructions plus formatted inputs containing existing section content
Prompt requires a strict 150-200 word limit and a '##' Markdown title
Writes the returned text back into section.content before grading

description: Grades whether a drafted section covers its assigned scope well enough.
heuristics: Uses structured Feedback output with grade and follow_up_queries
For claude-3-7-sonnet-latest, reflection uses max_tokens=20000 and thinking budget 16000
A passing grade or hitting max_search_depth immediately publishes the section

description: Launches another search round when section grading reports gaps.
heuristics: On fail, write_section updates search_queries with feedback.follow_up_queries
The grader prompt asks for empty follow-up queries when grade='pass'
Control returns to search_web for another search iteration

description: Writes intro or summary sections from completed report sections instead of fresh search.
heuristics: Uses final_section_writer_instructions with completed report sections as context
Introduction guidance is 50-100 words with no structural elements
Conclusion guidance is 100-150 words with at most one list or table

description: Drafts the report introduction after body research is complete.
heuristics: Legacy supervisor asks for introduction and conclusion only after completed_sections exist
If the Introduction tool output lacks '# ', code prepends an H1 heading
Introduction content is stored in final_report temporarily while conclusion is pending

description: Drafts the conclusion and stitches it after the introduction and body.
heuristics: If the Conclusion tool output lacks '## ', code prepends an H2 heading
After intro is stored, the supervisor is prompted to write a conclusion next
Conclusion is appended after all completed body sections

description: Finalizes the report state once all major writing stages are done.
heuristics: Legacy multi_agent assembles intro, body sections, and conclusion into final_report
New final_report_generation clears notes using an override update
Failure paths still return a final_report field with an error message

description: Compiles completed sections back into the original planned order.
heuristics: Maps completed section content by section.name
Rewrites each planned section's content from that mapping before joining
Returns source_str too when include_source_str is enabled

description: Synthesizes the final long-form report from the brief, chat, and findings.
heuristics: Builds the final prompt from research_brief, buffered messages, findings, and date
Uses the configurable final_report_model and final_report_model_max_tokens
On success stores the model response both as final_report and as the latest message

description: Progressively truncates findings so final report synthesis can fit model limits.
heuristics: Uses lookup_model_token_limit to estimate a first character cap at token_limit*4
If the model limit is unknown, returns an explicit error asking to update the map
Each retry trims the current findings cap to 90 percent of its prior value

description: Exchanges a Supabase access token for an MCP access token.
heuristics: POSTs form-encoded data to base_mcp_url.rstrip('/') + '/oauth/token'
Sets resource to base_mcp_url.rstrip('/') + '/mcp' in the exchange request
Logs non-200 response text and returns None on failure

description: Loads previously stored MCP tokens from the LangGraph store.
heuristics: Requires both configurable.thread_id and metadata.owner to proceed
Reads from namespace (user_id,'tokens') with key 'data'
Returns None when no cached tokens exist

description: Rejects cached tokens whose stored lifetime has already elapsed.
heuristics: Computes expiration_time as created_at + timedelta(seconds=expires_in)
Compares token age against datetime.now(timezone.utc)
Deletes expired token records from the store before returning None

description: Persists refreshed MCP tokens into the per-user token namespace.
heuristics: Requires configurable.thread_id and metadata.owner before writing
Stores tokens at namespace (user_id,'tokens') with key 'data'
Uses store.aput for async persistence

description: Refreshes or re-fetches MCP tokens when no valid cached token exists.
heuristics: Returns cached tokens immediately when get_tokens succeeds
Needs configurable x-supabase-access-token and mcp_config.url to mint new tokens
Stores newly exchanged MCP tokens before returning them

description: Rewrites MCP auth failures into user-facing ToolException messages.
heuristics: Recursively scans exception chains and ExceptionGroup-like .exceptions for McpError
Special-cases MCP error code -32003 as an interaction-required auth error
Appends the provided URL to the ToolException message when available

description: Lets selected state fields be replaced outright instead of always appended.
heuristics: override_reducer checks for updates shaped like {'type':'override','value':...}
Non-override updates fall back to operator.add semantics
Used for supervisor_messages, raw_notes, and notes in graph state

description: Authenticates bearer tokens against Supabase and returns the user identity.
heuristics: Rejects missing or malformed Authorization headers with HTTP 401
Uses asyncio.to_thread(supabase.auth.get_user, token) to avoid blocking
Returns {'identity': user.id} when Supabase returns a valid user

description: Writes thread ownership metadata when a user creates a thread.
heuristics: Skips owner stamping for StudioUser requests
Uses value.setdefault('metadata', {}) before writing
Stores ctx.user.identity under metadata['owner']

description: Restricts thread reads and mutations to the owning user.
heuristics: Applies to thread read, delete, update, and search handlers
Returns {'owner': ctx.user.identity} as the access filter
Bypasses filtering for StudioUser

description: Writes assistant ownership metadata when a user creates an assistant.
heuristics: Skips owner stamping for StudioUser requests
Uses value.setdefault('metadata', {}) before writing owner
Stores ctx.user.identity under assistant metadata['owner']

description: Restricts assistant reads and mutations to the owning user.
heuristics: Applies to assistant read, delete, update, and search handlers
Returns {'owner': ctx.user.identity} as the filter
Bypasses filtering for StudioUser

description: Authorizes store access only inside the caller's namespace.
heuristics: Reads the tuple namespace from the store request value
Asserts namespace[0] == ctx.user.identity
Skips the assertion for StudioUser

description: Builds the main runtime config from env vars and runnable overrides.
heuristics: Configuration.from_runnable_config iterates over all Pydantic model fields
Each value prefers os.environ.FIELD then config['configurable'][field]
Drops only fields whose resolved value is None

description: Builds the legacy workflow or multi-agent config from env and run config.
heuristics: Legacy dataclass config readers iterate over dataclass fields(f)
Each value prefers env var uppercase field name over configurable[field]
Legacy config constructors drop falsy values, not just None

description: Normalizes enums and passthrough values into plain config values.
heuristics: New get_config_value returns None unchanged
Strings and dicts are returned as-is
Enum-like values fall back to .value

description: Strips search config down to parameters accepted by the chosen backend.
heuristics: Uses an allowlist keyed by backend name in SEARCH_API_PARAMS
Returns {} when no search_api_config was supplied
Example allowlists include Exa domains and PubMed email/api_key fields

description: Resolves the correct provider API key for a configured model name.
heuristics: Switches between config-sourced keys and env vars via GET_API_KEYS_FROM_CONFIG
Uses model prefixes openai:, anthropic:, and google to choose keys
Returns None when config-based apiKeys are missing

description: Resolves the Tavily API key from config or environment.
heuristics: Also follows the GET_API_KEYS_FROM_CONFIG flag
Reads config['configurable']['apiKeys']['TAVILY_API_KEY'] when config-driven
Falls back to the TAVILY_API_KEY environment variable otherwise

description: Looks up a model's known context limit from a built-in map.
heuristics: Scans MODEL_TOKEN_LIMITS by substring containment rather than exact equality
Covers OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Cohere, Mistral, Ollama, and Bedrock entries
Returns None when no entry matches the model string

description: Identifies provider-specific token-limit failures from thrown exceptions.
heuristics: Infers provider from model prefixes openai:, anthropic:, gemini:/google:
OpenAI checks BadRequest/InvalidRequest errors plus token or context keywords
Anthropic checks 'prompt is too long' and Gemini checks ResourceExhausted patterns

description: Loads legacy MCP server JSON config from disk without blocking the event loop.
heuristics: Reads the JSON file inside a helper run by asyncio.to_thread
Uses json.load on the provided path
Returns the parsed dict unchanged

description: Formats benchmark input messages into a stable string for graders.
heuristics: Returns the only message's content directly for single-message inputs
Wraps user messages in <user_input> tags
Wraps assistant follow-ups in <assistant_follow_up> tags

description: Scores overall report quality across six benchmark dimensions.
heuristics: Uses OverallQualityScore structured output with six 1-5 fields
Normalizes each returned score by dividing by 5
Passes today's date into OVERALL_QUALITY_PROMPT

description: Scores whether the report stays tightly relevant to the user query.
heuristics: Uses RelevanceScore with reasoning plus integer score 1-5
Returns a metric key named relevance_score
Includes the grader's reasoning as the metric comment

description: Scores report structure and flow against the user's requested format.
heuristics: Feeds STRUCTURE_PROMPT as a single user message rather than system+user
Returns metric key structure_and_cohesiveness_score
Normalizes the integer structure score by dividing by 5

description: Scores correctness by comparing the report to a reference answer.
heuristics: Reads the reference answer from reference_outputs['answer']
Builds CORRECTNESS_PROMPT with question, report, answer, and today
Returns correctness_score normalized to the 0-1 range

description: Scores how many extracted report claims are supported by retrieved notes.
heuristics: Uses outputs['raw_notes'] as the grounding context string
Retries groundedness extraction up to 3 times
Computes score as grounded_claim_count divided by total_claim_count

description: Extracts report claims and labels each one as grounded or ungrounded.
heuristics: GroundednessClaim contains a claim string plus a grounded boolean
GroundednessScore wraps a list of GroundednessClaim objects
The evaluator serializes the full claim list into the metric comment

description: Scores whether the report fully answers the brief implied by the query.
heuristics: Uses research_brief from outputs alongside the final report
Builds COMPLETENESS_PROMPT with user question, research_brief, and report
Returns completeness_score normalized by dividing by 5

description: Runs the benchmark target across a LangSmith dataset with attached evaluators.
heuristics: tests/run_evaluate.py calls client.aevaluate on dataset 'Deep Research Bench'
The target compiles deep_researcher_builder with a MemorySaver checkpointer
Evaluation metadata logs the configured models, limits, and search backend

description: Extracts finished LangSmith runs into Deep Research Bench JSONL format.
heuristics: Reads project.reference_dataset_id to recover example metadata
Keeps only root runs whose outputs contain final_report
Writes tests/expt_results/{dataset_name}_{model_name}.jsonl in JSONL format

description: Checks whether the supervisor chose the expected number of parallel tasks.
heuristics: Compares len(last supervisor message tool_calls) to reference_outputs['parallel']
Uses graph.get_state(config, subgraphs=True).tasks[0].state as the evaluation output
Runs this evaluator with max_concurrency=1 in tests/supervisor_parallel_evaluation.py

description: Runs a head-to-head LLM judge over two report outputs.
heuristics: Uses Claude Opus 4 with thinking budget 16000 as the grader
Returns [1,0] when answer 1 wins and [0,1] when answer 2 wins
Compares outputs via each candidate's final_report field

description: Ranks three candidate reports and assigns winner/runner-up scores.
heuristics: Uses structured Rankings output with preferred, second_best, and worst answers
Assigns scores of 1, 0.5, and 0 based on the ranking
Compares each candidate via outputs[i].get('final_report','N/A')

description: Runs the legacy pytest matrix across one or both agent implementations.
heuristics: Supports --agent multi_agent or graph, or --all to run both
Sets LANGSMITH_PROJECT and LANGSMITH_TEST_SUITE before each run
Invokes pytest with -v -s --disable-warnings --langsmith-output

description: Injects model and search overrides into the legacy pytest command line.
heuristics: add_model_configs appends --supervisor-model and --researcher-model when present
It also forwards planner, writer, eval, search_api, and max_search_depth flags
Only provided arguments are added to the spawned pytest command

description: Store user, system, assistant, and tool messages with bounded chat history.
heuristics: update_memory only accepts user/system/assistant/tool roles.
Memory keeps at most 100 messages and trims the oldest entries.

description: Run think/act steps until the agent finishes or hits its step budget.
heuristics: run() refuses to start unless the agent state is IDLE.
Each loop increments current_step and stops when state becomes FINISHED or max_steps is reached.
When max_steps is hit, current_step resets to 0 and state is set back to IDLE.

description: Detect repeated assistant outputs so the agent can avoid looping.
heuristics: is_stuck compares the latest message content only against earlier assistant messages.
duplicate_threshold defaults to 2 matching assistant responses.

description: Rewrite the next-step prompt when duplicate responses show the agent is stuck.
heuristics: handle_stuck_state prepends a duplicate-response warning to next_step_prompt.
The injected prompt tells the model to try new strategies and avoid repeated paths.

description: Normalize assistant tool calls and tool outputs into memory messages.
heuristics: Message.from_tool_calls stores tool decisions as an assistant-role message.
Tool messages preserve tool_call_id, tool name, and optional base64_image.

description: Execute the current tool-call list in order and record each observation.
heuristics: ToolCallAgent.act runs tool_calls sequentially in list order.
Each observation is truncated by max_observe when that limit is set.
A tool message is appended to memory after every tool execution.

description: Release sandbox and tool resources after a run ends or fails.
heuristics: BaseAgent.run always awaits SANDBOX_CLIENT.cleanup() after the loop.
ToolCallAgent.run calls cleanup() in a finally block.
cleanup() only awaits tool.cleanup methods that are coroutine functions.

description: Convert Message objects into API-ready text or multimodal chat payloads.
heuristics: format_messages turns base64_image into an image_url data URI only when supports_images is true.
If images are unsupported, it drops base64_image and keeps the text content.
Dict messages without a role field raise ValueError.

description: Estimate chat token usage for text, tool calls, and images before sending.
heuristics: TokenCounter starts with 2 format tokens and 4 base tokens per message.
It counts role, content, tool call name and arguments, name, and tool_call_id.
Low-detail images cost 85 tokens; high detail is computed from 512px tiles.

description: Block requests that would exceed the configured cumulative input-token budget.
heuristics: check_token_limit compares cumulative input tokens plus new input against max_input_tokens.
ask_tool adds serialized tool-schema tokens before the limit check.
Overages raise TokenLimitExceeded instead of being retried.

description: Request a plain chat completion when the agent needs free-form model output.
heuristics: ask() retries up to 6 times with exponential backoff on OpenAIError, Exception, or ValueError.
Streaming is enabled by default and prints chunks as they arrive.
Reasoning models use max_completion_tokens; other models use max_tokens plus temperature.

description: Request a multimodal completion when the model must read attached images.
heuristics: ask_with_images rejects models outside MULTIMODAL_MODELS.
The last formatted message must be from the user so images can be attached.
String image inputs are appended as image_url items on the last user message.

description: Request a non-streaming model response that can choose or call tools.
heuristics: ask_tool validates tool_choice against none, auto, and required.
Tool requests always run with stream=false.
Each tool definition must be a dict containing a top-level type field.

description: Translate OpenAI-style chat and tool payloads to Amazon Bedrock and back.
heuristics: OpenAI function specs become Bedrock toolSpec.inputSchema.json objects.
Assistant tool_calls become toolUse blocks and tool replies become user toolResult blocks.
Bedrock responses are converted back into OpenAI-style message and usage objects.

description: Create a step plan for complex work and seed it with the active plan id.
heuristics: _create_initial_plan injects active_plan_id into planning tool arguments before execution.
If the LLM does not call the planning tool, a default 3-step plan is created.
When multiple executors exist, the prompt asks for [agent_name] tags in step text.

description: Persist step statuses, notes, and plan progress as execution changes.
heuristics: New plans initialize step_statuses to not_started and step_notes to empty strings.
Plan updates preserve status and notes only when the step text stays at the same index.
mark_step accepts only not_started, in_progress, completed, and blocked.

description: Choose the best executor for a plan step from the available agents.
heuristics: get_executor first matches step_type directly to an agent key.
If there is no match, it uses the first configured executor_key.
If no executor_key resolves, it falls back to primary_agent.

description: Run the next active plan step with its assigned executor and mark progress.
heuristics: _get_current_step_info selects the first step still not_started or in_progress.
Selected steps are marked in_progress before execution.
_execute_step passes the full plan status and current step text to executor.run().

description: Render the current plan into a progress summary the agent can reuse.
heuristics: _generate_plan_text_from_storage pads statuses and notes to the number of steps.
Status marks are rendered as [✓], [→], [!], and [ ].
Progress is shown as completed/total with a percentage.

description: Summarize what a completed plan accomplished for the final response.
heuristics: _finalize_plan asks the LLM to summarize the final plan text.
If the LLM call fails, it falls back to primary_agent.run() with the same plan text.

description: Run short Python snippets when the agent needs computation or scripting.
heuristics: Python executes in a separate multiprocessing.Process.
Only stdout captured via print() is returned; function return values are not.
Processes still alive after the timeout are terminated and reported as failures.

description: Run shell commands in a persistent bash session when state must carry over.
heuristics: Bash commands run inside one persistent /bin/bash session.
Each command appends a <<exit>> sentinel so output can be read without EOF.
Timed-out sessions are marked unusable until restarted.

description: Inspect files or directories before editing or debugging local workspace state.
heuristics: StrReplaceEditor requires absolute paths.
Directory views use find with maxdepth 2 and exclude hidden paths.
File view ranges are 1-based and allow [start, -1] to read to EOF.

description: Create a new text file when the target path does not already exist.
heuristics: The create command fails if the target path already exists.
Created file contents are stored in _file_history for undo support.

description: Replace one exact text block inside a file when a targeted edit is enough.
heuristics: old_str and new_str are tab-expanded before matching.
Replacement only runs when old_str appears exactly once.
On success the tool returns a snippet around the edited lines.

description: Insert new text at a validated line boundary without rewriting the whole file.
heuristics: insert_line must be between 0 and the file line count.
Inserted edits save the previous file text for undo.
The response previews nearby lines around the insertion.

description: Undo the most recent tracked edit to a file when a change was wrong.
heuristics: undo_edit restores the latest saved version from _file_history.
It errors if no edit history exists for the target path.

description: Ask the user a direct question when the agent truly needs human input.
heuristics: The tool prompts on stdin as `Bot: {inquire}` followed by `You:`.
Returned input is stripped before being passed back.

description: Emit schema-shaped output when the caller needs a typed response payload.
heuristics: CreateChatCompletion builds JSON schema from str, BaseModel, list, dict, or Union types.
Single required fields return a scalar, while multiple required fields return a dict.
BaseModel outputs reuse model_json_schema().

description: Emit the final success or failure status when work is complete.
heuristics: Terminate only accepts status values success or failure.
Its output is a fixed completion string containing the chosen status.

description: Start and reuse a browser context when the task needs live web interaction.
heuristics: Browser sessions default to headless=false and disable_security=true.
Proxy settings are loaded from config.browser_config.proxy when present.
A BrowserContext is created once and reused across calls.

description: Capture the live browser URL, tabs, DOM summary, and screenshot for prompting.
heuristics: get_current_state captures a full-page JPEG screenshot with animations disabled.
State includes url, title, tabs, interactive element text, and scroll metrics.
total_height is computed from pixels_above + pixels_below + viewport_height.

description: Open a URL in the browser when the agent needs to move to a page.
heuristics: go_to_url requires a url parameter.
After page.goto(url), the tool waits for the page load state.

description: Search the web from the browser tool and jump straight to the top result.
heuristics: browser_use web_search calls WebSearch with fetch_content=true and num_results=1.
It navigates to the first returned result URL after the search.

description: Fetch and sanitize webpage text when snippets are not enough.
heuristics: WebContentFetcher strips script, style, header, footer, and nav elements.
Fetched text is whitespace-normalized and truncated to 10,000 characters.

description: Click a known indexed page element when the next step is visible in the DOM.
heuristics: click_element resolves targets through get_dom_element_by_index(index).
If the click triggers a download, the saved download path is included in the output.

description: Type into an indexed browser field when filling forms or search boxes.
heuristics: input_text requires both index and text.
It refuses to type when the indexed DOM element cannot be found.

description: Move the browser viewport when needed content is off screen.
heuristics: scroll_down and scroll_up default to the browser window height when no amount is passed.
scroll_to_text uses page.get_by_text(text, exact=false).

description: Send keyboard shortcuts to the current page when direct typing is not enough.
heuristics: send_keys calls page.keyboard.press(keys).
Missing keys produce an explicit tool error response.

description: Read the available select options before choosing one in the browser.
heuristics: Dropdown options are read by evaluating the select element XPath in the page.
Returned option objects include text, value, and index.

description: Choose a dropdown value by label when the page exposes a select control.
heuristics: select_dropdown_option requires both index and text.
It selects by label using page.select_option(element.xpath, label=text).

description: Open, switch, or close tabs while keeping browser task state intact.
heuristics: switch_tab waits for the selected tab to finish loading.
open_tab requires a URL, and close_tab closes the current tab.

description: Extract goal-focused page content when the agent needs more than raw HTML.
heuristics: Page HTML is converted to Markdown before extraction.
The prompt truncates content to browser_config.max_content_length or 2000 chars.
Extraction uses a required function call that returns extracted_content.

description: Run a Google query when Google is the preferred search backend.
heuristics: Google uses googlesearch.search(query, advanced=true).
String-only hits are wrapped with fallback titles like Google Result N.

description: Run a DuckDuckGo query when a lightweight privacy-friendly backend is needed.
heuristics: DuckDuckGo uses DDGS().text(query, max_results=num_results).
Dict hits map href to url and body to description.
Unknown result types fall back to str(item).

description: Run a Bing query and paginate until enough organic results are collected.
heuristics: Bing follows the Next page link until enough results are collected.
Snippets are truncated to 300 characters.
Results are parsed from li.b_algo entries under ol#b_results.

description: Run a Baidu query when Chinese-language search coverage is needed.
heuristics: Baidu uses baidusearch.search(query, num_results=num_results).
Dict hits map url and abstract, while unknown types fall back to attribute access or str(item).

description: Connect to an MCP server over SSE and expose its tools locally.
heuristics: connect_sse disconnects any existing session with the same server_id first.
Connections are stored in an AsyncExitStack and initialized before listing tools.

description: Connect to an MCP server over stdio when it runs as a local process.
heuristics: stdio connections wrap StdioServerParameters(command, args).
Existing sessions for the same server_id are disconnected before reconnecting.

description: Proxy a local tool call to a remote MCP tool implementation.
heuristics: MCPClientTool refuses execution when no session is attached.
Tool calls are forwarded as session.call_tool(original_name, kwargs).
Only TextContent parts are joined into the returned output string.

description: Refresh live MCP tool schemas so the agent notices added or removed tools.
heuristics: _refresh_tools compares live tool input schemas against cached tool_schemas.
Added and removed tools are appended to memory as system messages.

description: Close one or all MCP connections and remove their proxied tools.
heuristics: disconnect(server_id) closes the stored AsyncExitStack.
Disconnecting all servers iterates in sorted server_id order.
Disconnected servers are removed from tool_map and sessions.

description: Register OpenManus tools on the built-in MCP server for external callers.
heuristics: MCPServer pre-registers bash, browser, editor, and terminate tools.
Registered method signatures are derived from each tool JSON schema.
Dict and Pydantic results are JSON-serialized before returning.

description: Expose a Manus agent over the A2A service protocol for remote task execution.
heuristics: The A2A AgentCard advertises streaming=false and pushNotifications=true.
A2AManus.invoke always returns is_task_complete=true and require_user_input=false.
ManusExecutor emits a completed_task artifact with the agent text content.

description: Create a Daytona sandbox when work needs an isolated remote workspace.
heuristics: Daytona sandboxes use the configured browser-use image with CPU=2, memory=4, and disk=5.
They set VNC_PASSWORD plus Chrome debugging environment variables.
New sandboxes start supervisord immediately after creation.

description: Restart an archived or stopped Daytona sandbox before reusing it.
heuristics: get_or_start_sandbox restarts sandboxes only when state is ARCHIVED or STOPPED.
Restarting also launches a supervisord session.

description: Launch supervisord inside a sandbox so background services stay available.
heuristics: Supervisord always runs in session_id `supervisord-session`.
It executes `/usr/bin/supervisord -n -c /etc/supervisor/conf.d/supervisord.conf`.
The launcher waits 25 seconds after starting it.

description: Run shell commands in the sandbox when remote CLI work is required.
heuristics: Commands run inside tmux sessions rooted at /workspace by default.
Non-blocking execution is the default behavior.
Blocking mode polls until timeout or prompt-like completion markers appear.

description: Inspect or stop long-running sandbox commands without losing their session state.
heuristics: check_command_output captures the full tmux pane for a named session.
list_commands parses tmux list-sessions output into session names.
terminate_command kills the named tmux session.

description: Drive a browser inside the sandbox when remote web automation is safer.
heuristics: Sandbox browser actions call localhost:8003/api/automation/* via curl inside the sandbox.
Returned screenshot_base64 is validated before reuse.
The last browser state is cached in browser_message for get_current_state().

description: Create or modify sandbox files when remote code or assets must change.
heuristics: Sandbox file paths are cleaned to stay under /workspace.
str_replace only succeeds when old_str appears exactly once.
Creating or rewriting root index.html appends the preview URL for port 8080.

description: Load an image from the sandbox when the model needs visual file context.
heuristics: Only the see_image action is supported.
Original images over 10MB and compressed images over 5MB are rejected.
Images are resized to at most 1920x1080 before base64 encoding.

description: Automate the sandbox desktop when browser-only actions are not enough.
heuristics: Desktop automation talks to the sandbox preview API on port 8000.
Mouse positions are tracked so click and drag can reuse the last coordinates.
Scroll amounts are clamped to the range -10..10.

description: Create, track, and clean Docker sandboxes for isolated local execution.
heuristics: DockerSandbox containers run with `tail -f /dev/null` as the base command.
Network mode is `none` unless network_enabled is true.
SandboxManager auto-pulls missing images before creation.

description: Move files into or out of a sandbox when local and remote state must sync.
heuristics: copy_from extracts container archives to the host filesystem.
copy_to uploads tar archives and verifies the destination exists afterward.

description: Read a file from a sandbox when the agent needs remote file contents.
heuristics: Container reads resolve relative paths against the sandbox work_dir.
Path segments containing `..` are rejected.

description: Write a file into a sandbox when remote code or data must be persisted.
heuristics: Writes create parent directories with `mkdir -p` before uploading tar data.
Relative paths are resolved under the sandbox work_dir.

description: Crawl one or more pages when the agent needs AI-ready web content.
heuristics: crawl4ai accepts one URL or a list and skips invalid URLs before crawling.
The crawler runs with JavaScript enabled, iframes processed, and overlays removed.
Markdown previews are limited to the first 300 characters in the summary.

description: Validate crawl targets before fetching them to avoid bad or unsupported URLs.
heuristics: _is_valid_url requires both scheme and netloc.
Only http and https URLs are accepted.

description: Prepare chart-ready CSV and JSON metadata before rendering visualizations.
heuristics: VisualizationPrepare expects Python code to print the saved JSON path.
Visualization mode outputs [{csvFilePath, chartTitle}] while insight mode outputs [{chartPath, insights_id}].

description: Render charts from prepared data when analysis needs visual output.
heuristics: CSV inputs are loaded with pandas and converted to JSON records.
Charts are rendered by spawning `npx ts-node src/chartVisualize.ts`.
output_type supports png and html.

description: Add selected insight annotations to an existing chart when findings matter.
heuristics: Insight runs require chartPath plus insights_id selections.
Insights are added by calling invoke_vmind with task_type='insight'.

description: Generate a saved text report when the task needs conclusions, metrics, and findings.
heuristics: NormalPythonExecute asks for printed, content-rich reports saved in the workspace.
Its prompt explicitly asks for dataset overview, stats, outliers, and key insights.

description: Create the FastAPI app before registering middleware and routes.
heuristics: app = FastAPI() is instantiated once at module load
The root, health, and search handlers are all registered on that single app object

description: Open CORS for browser clients when exposing the search API cross-origin.
heuristics: Adds CORSMiddleware to the FastAPI app
Allows all origins and headers with credentials enabled
Restricts methods to GET and POST

description: Return a simple root payload when clients probe the service entrypoint.
heuristics: Registers a GET handler at /
Returns the message hello world openperplex v1

description: Return a lightweight OK status when deployment health checks hit the app.
heuristics: Registers a GET handler at /up_test
Returns status ok for the deploy health check

description: Reject empty search requests before the pipeline spends provider calls.
heuristics: ask() checks if not query before doing any retrieval
Raises HTTPException with status_code 400 and detail Query cannot be empty

description: Run retrieval, optional crawling, context build, answering, and follow-ups for one query.
heuristics: Calls get_sources() first and populate_sources(..., 2) only in pro mode
Builds context with build_context() before streaming get_answer()
Generates follow-ups with get_relevant_questions() after answer streaming

description: Emit staged SSE events while the search answer is being assembled.
heuristics: Wraps the generator in StreamingResponse with media_type text/event-stream
Streams sources, llm, relevant, finished, and error payload types
Ends with an explicit end-of-stream event

description: Normalize the requested geo for Serper, falling back to US when absent.
heuristics: Uses stored_location or DEFAULT_LOCATION and lowercases the result
DEFAULT_LOCATION is set to us

description: Reduce Serper result count in pro mode before downstream crawling and reranking.
heuristics: Sets num_results to 10 in pro mode and 20 otherwise
Passes that value to Serper as the num payload field

description: Call Serper search with query, result count, and geo when web retrieval is needed.
heuristics: POSTs to https://google.serper.dev/search
Sends q, num, and gl in the JSON payload
Uses the X-API-KEY header and a 10 second timeout

description: Project raw provider items down to only the fields the pipeline consumes.
heuristics: Creates a new dict per item using only the requested field list
Skips any field that is missing from the source item

description: Keep normalized organic hits when snippet-based evidence is needed.
heuristics: Reads organic results from the Serper response
Keeps only title, link, snippet, and date fields

description: Keep normalized top stories when news titles may improve context.
heuristics: Reads topStories from the Serper response
Keeps only title and imageUrl

description: Keep a small normalized image set when visual search signals are useful.
heuristics: Slices the images array to the first 6 results
Keeps only title and imageUrl for each image

description: Carry direct-answer objects forward when Serper returns graph or answer-box data.
heuristics: Maps knowledgeGraph from Serper to the graph field
Maps answerBox from Serper to the answerBox field

description: Choose only the first organic links for crawling when pro mode asks for deeper evidence.
heuristics: populate_sources() iterates only over sources[:num_elements]
The main pipeline calls crawling only when pro_mode is True
The current crawl budget is fixed at 2 links

description: Fetch page text from selected links when snippets alone are not enough.
heuristics: Uses WebBaseLoader(url) to load page documents
Runs extraction from each selected source link

description: Strip newlines and ignore empty page fragments before adding crawled text.
heuristics: Appends only documents whose page_content is truthy
Replaces newline characters with empty strings before joining

description: Cap crawled page text before context assembly to keep prompts bounded.
heuristics: Returns only the first 4000 characters of cleaned text
Returns an empty string when combined page text is 200 characters or shorter

description: Attach extracted page text onto each crawled organic source as html.
heuristics: Writes source html from extract_website_content(source['link'])
Stores the enriched source back into the original list slot

description: Start the context with organic snippets when search results already summarize pages.
heuristics: Builds snippet strings from organic results first
Extends the combined evidence list with snippets before other signals

description: Append each snippet's date so time-sensitive answers can use recency cues.
heuristics: Formats each snippet as snippet plus item.get('date', '')
Falls back to an empty date string when a result has no date

description: Merge crawled page text into one body before chunking it for context.
heuristics: Joins every available html field from organic results with spaces
Only sends the merged text to chunking when it exceeds 200 characters

description: Split long crawled text into semantic chunks before retrieval-time reranking.
heuristics: Uses StatisticalChunker with max_split_tokens set to 200
Embeds with Cohere model embed-multilingual-v3.0 using input_type search_document
Flattens chunk objects into plain content strings

description: Add top-story titles when news headlines can sharpen answer context.
heuristics: Reads titles from topStories if that field is present
Appends only items that contain a title

description: Add knowledge-graph descriptions when Serper returns entity summary text.
heuristics: Checks sources_result for a graph object
Appends graph.description only when it is truthy

description: Add answer-box answer and snippet text when search already surfaced a direct answer.
heuristics: If answerBox exists it iterates over answer and snippet keys
Appends each present answerBox value to the combined context list

description: Join all collected evidence blocks into the final prompt context string.
heuristics: Uses a double newline join across the final evidence list
Returns an empty string if context building throws an exception

description: Append date context to the rerank query when time awareness should affect relevance.
heuristics: In pro mode the reranker query is query plus date_context
Non-pro mode skips reranking so no date-expanded rerank query is built

description: Reorder candidate context blocks with Jina when pro mode wants tighter evidence.
heuristics: Pro mode calls get_reranking_jina(combined_list, query + date_context, 15)
Posts to Jina with model jina-reranker-v2-base-multilingual and top_n
Returns document text fields from the reranked results

description: Reorder context blocks with Cohere when swapping in the alternate reranker.
heuristics: Uses Cohere model rerank-multilingual-v3.0
Calls co.rerank with return_documents=True and top_n=top_res
Returns item.document.text for each reranked result

description: Format the answer system prompt with the current date before LLM generation.
heuristics: Builds a PromptTemplate with the search_prompt_system template
Formats the system prompt with date_context before the chat call
The prompt requires English Markdown output and says to return information missing on weak context

description: Stream answer tokens from Groq when the user should see incremental generation.
heuristics: Uses Groq model llama3-70b-8192
Calls client.chat.completions.create with stream=True
Yields only non-null delta content from each streamed chunk

description: Ask Groq for three related follow-up questions after the main answer is ready.
heuristics: Calls a second Groq completion with the relevant prompt
The user message includes both the original query and the full contexts
The main pipeline emits an empty list if follow-up generation fails

description: Force the follow-up generator to return a JSON object instead of free text.
heuristics: Passes response_format with type json_object to Groq
The prompt schema requires a followUp array with three strings
The prompt says the JSON object must not include special characters

description: Chooses the active search group and downgrades restricted modes to web when the user lacks access.
heuristics: Memory and buddy fall back to web when no authenticated user exists
Connectors, MCP, canvas, and multi-agent fall back to web for non-Pro users
Returns both the tool list and the mode-specific system instructions for the resolved group

description: Lazily imports only the tools needed for the active mode, user state, and file context.
heuristics: Deduplicates activeToolNames before loading any modules
Skips file_query_search when no context files are attached
Adds dynamic MCP tools with Object.assign only when includeMcpTools is enabled

description: Blocks requests that exceed auth, subscription, or usage limits before the model starts streaming.
heuristics: Unauthenticated users are limited by IP with an Upstash sliding window of 25 requests per 7 days
Extreme and MCP modes require authentication, and MCP also requires Pro
Non-Pro users are blocked at 100 daily messages, Max Anthropic at 60 weekly uses, and Max Gemini at 80 monthly uses

description: Creates or validates a chat, allocates a stream id, and starts title generation without blocking the stream.
heuristics: Temporary chats skip chat creation and ownership validation
Existing chats create the stream id early in parallel with access checks
New chats use a single saveNewChatWithStream write, then kick off title generation asynchronously

description: Streams model output, tools, reasoning, and sources over a resumable UI message stream.
heuristics: Adds dynamic MCP tools for MCP and extreme groups before tool loading
Injects xai_web_search and xai_x_search only for Pro multi-agent mode
Repairs invalid tool arguments against the tool schema with scira-default before retrying

description: Writes only the valid final assistant messages for the latest stream and latest user turn.
heuristics: Skips persistence when a newer stream id exists for the same chat
Skips persistence when a newer user message id exists for the same chat
Aborted streams persist only assistant parts that contain text, reasoning, tool calls, files, or sources

description: Resumes the latest SSE stream for a chat and can replay a just-finished assistant message if needed.
heuristics: Uses the newest stream id from getStreamIdsByChatId with ai-resumable-stream
If resumeStream returns null, it replays the last assistant message only when it is 15 seconds old or newer
Client auto-resume only fires when the last initial message role is user

description: Stops the current resumable stream for a chat when the owner explicitly cancels generation.
heuristics: Returns 204 when Redis resumable-stream clients are unavailable
Authorizes the caller against the chat owner before stopping a stream
Stops only the latest stream id for the chat via context.stopStream

description: Prevents stale writes and restores fresh assistant output when replicas lag behind stream state.
heuristics: Uses maindb for latest stream and latest user message guards to avoid replica lag
Skips stale stream persistence when latestStreamId differs from the request streamId
Replays the last assistant message when the resumable stream is gone but the message is still fresh

description: Runs multi-query web search through Exa, Parallel, or Firecrawl with result and image deduping.
heuristics: Fills missing maxResults, topics, and quality arrays to the query count
Falls back to Firecrawl when the selected provider returns zero results for every query
Deduplicates both results and images by exact URL and by domain

description: Searches research papers and PDFs with Firecrawl and streams per-query progress updates.
heuristics: Accepts 1 to 5 queries and defaults each query to 20 results
Restricts Firecrawl search categories to research and pdf
Emits started, completed, and error data-query_completion events per query

description: Finds YouTube videos and enriches them with metadata, timestamps, and native transcript chunks.
heuristics: Caps resolved videos at SEARCH_LIMIT 12 after deduping by video id
Fetches transcripts in native mode and polls async jobs up to 5 times every 1.5 seconds
Uses chapter markers from the video description before generating transcript-based chapters

description: Searches Spotify tracks, artists, albums, and playlists and reranks them for relevance.
heuristics: Caches the client-credentials token until 60 seconds before expiry
On HTTP 429 it waits Retry-After seconds and retries recursively
Reranks each result type with Cohere rerank-v4.0-pro

description: Searches Reddit via Parallel, date-filters queries, and extracts post text plus subreddit metadata.
heuristics: Restricts Parallel source_policy to reddit.com and optional after_date
Forces max_results to at least 10 even if the caller asks for less
Parses subreddit names from URLs and joins excerpts into content and comments

description: Searches GitHub repositories with Firecrawl and returns repo metadata, markdown, and parsed stats.
heuristics: Uses Firecrawl github search with markdown plus a repo metadata JSON extraction schema
Filters out non-repo paths like /topics, /trending, and profile-only pages
Normalizes star counts from strings like 1.2k into integers when possible

description: Searches recent X posts across multiple queries and dedupes tweet sources by id or canonical link.
heuristics: Defaults the date window to today and 15 days ago when dates are omitted
Rejects requests that provide both includeXHandles and excludeXHandles
Sanitizes handle inputs by stripping leading @ symbols before calling xai.tools.xSearch

description: Searches Polymarket and Kalshi together to surface live probabilities and market metadata.
heuristics: Always queries both valyu-polymarket and valyu-kalshi sources together
Defaults maxResults to 15 and caps it at 30
Maps Polymarket active and closed flags differently from Kalshi status and result fields

description: Retrieves full content for supplied URLs and switches to Supadata for supported social platforms.
heuristics: Auto-detects twitter, youtube, tiktok, and instagram from URL patterns
Uses Supadata metadata plus transcript extraction for non-general social URLs
Falls back to general retrieval only when the URL is not recognized as a supported social type

description: Pulls technical library and framework context from Exa Context for short development queries.
heuristics: Rejects queries shorter than 1 char or longer than 100 chars via schema
POSTs to api.exa.ai/context with tokensNum set to dynamic
Returns a structured response with response text, resultsCount, searchTime, and outputTokens

description: Builds stock price charts with company resolution, news, earnings, and optional financial extras.
heuristics: Non-Pro users only get the base stock chart path while extras stay gated
Runs each news query twice in Tavily with finance and news topics over the last 7 days
Uses short SEC filing responses when the request is large, such as 3 or more extra data sources or more than 2 companies

description: Fetches Pro-gated balance-sheet, income, cash-flow, dividend, insider, and mover datasets for stocks.
heuristics: Statistics, balance sheet, income statement, cash flow, dividends, insider trades, and movers require Pro
Stores financial extras keyed by requested company name in separate result maps
Treats 3 or more enabled extras as a large request when tuning upstream response sizes

description: Collects stock news from Tavily and Exa to accompany charted price and fundamentals output.
heuristics: Tavily runs each query once against finance and once against news
Each Tavily call uses days 7, maxResults 3, and advanced searchDepth
Exa also searches each company for financial report analysis with category financial report and 10 results

description: Fetches SEC filings or filing sections for companies through Valyu when Pro access is available.
heuristics: Only Pro users can pass filing_types through to the SEC path
Switches from a full filing query to a section-specific query when sections are supplied
Uses the valyu-sec-filings source with responseLength shortened for large requests

description: Converts currencies by querying Valyu for forward and reverse rates in parallel.
heuristics: Searches both valyu-forex and valyu-crypto sources for each pair
Fetches from-to and to-from rates concurrently
Computes reverseRate as 1 forwardRate when the reverse lookup is missing

description: Fetches full CoinGecko coin metadata and market data for a coin id.
heuristics: Defaults localization, tickers, market_data, community_data, and developer_data to true
Always disables sparkline in the CoinGecko request
Returns a success false object with the error message instead of throwing on API failure

description: Looks up a token by platform id and contract address and returns CoinGecko metadata for it.
heuristics: Calls the CoinGecko platform contract endpoint with the same rich metadata flags as coin lookup
Sends the x-cg-demo-api-key header on every request
Uses the token homepage when present, otherwise falls back to a generic CoinGecko URL

description: Fetches coin OHLC candles plus full coin metadata for candlestick charts.
heuristics: Loads OHLC candles and full coin data in parallel
Formats each candle with timestamp and ISO date fields
Hardcodes the chart output as candlestick with datetime x scale and linear y scale

description: Resolves a place to coordinates and returns forecast, air quality, and 16-day weather data.
heuristics: Geocodes names with Open-Meteo and reverse-geocodes coordinates with OpenWeather when needed
Fetches weather forecast, current air pollution, and Open-Meteo daily forecast in parallel
Makes a separate OpenWeather air pollution forecast request after the first batch completes

description: Tracks a flight by carrier, number, and departure date and normalizes status and timing fields.
heuristics: Prefers actual timings over estimated and estimated over scheduled
Derives status from arrival point, departure point, matching segment, leg, then flight
Looks up airline names and airport names from Amadeus reference endpoints after fetching the schedule

description: Performs forward or reverse geocoding through Google Maps and returns normalized place records.
heuristics: Uses address geocoding when query is provided and reverse geocoding when lat and lng are provided
Rejects requests that provide neither query nor coordinates
Maps Google results into place_id, formatted address, location, types, and viewport fields

description: Finds nearby places around a location or coordinate pair and enriches them with place details.
heuristics: Geocodes the location first when latitude and longitude are missing
Clamps radius to 50000 meters and slices nearby results to 20 places
Fetches Place Details per result and computes distance with a haversine calculation

description: Looks up a movie or TV show in TMDB and returns details plus trimmed cast and crew info.
heuristics: Searches TMDB multi search and picks the first movie or tv result
Fetches details and credits in separate follow-up requests
Limits cast to 8 people and expands poster and backdrop paths to original image URLs

description: Lists the current TMDB trending movies with full poster and backdrop URLs.
heuristics: Calls the TMDB trending movie day endpoint
Rewrites poster_path and backdrop_path to image.tmdb.org original URLs
Takes no input parameters

description: Lists the current TMDB trending TV shows with full poster and backdrop URLs.
heuristics: Calls the TMDB trending tv day endpoint
Rewrites poster_path and backdrop_path to image.tmdb.org original URLs
Takes no input parameters

description: Translates plain text or image text, auto-detecting the source language when needed.
heuristics: Detects the source language with scira-default unless from is explicitly provided
Routes image translation through scira-gemini-3-flash with the images attached directly
Routes text-only translation through Cohere command-a-translate-08-2025 at temperature 0

description: Returns the current UTC timestamp in several formatted date and time layouts.
heuristics: Always formats output in UTC even though the description mentions user timezone
Returns timestamp, ISO, and full, short, and split date/time strings
Builds iso_local with a sv-SE formatter and then replaces the space with T

description: Generates presigned upload URLs for supported images and documents with auth-aware storage keys.
heuristics: Allows JPEG, PNG, and GIF up to 5 MB and documents up to 50 MB
Unauthenticated uploads are rate-limited by IP before a presigned URL is issued
Stores authenticated uploads under scira/users/{userId} and public uploads under scira/public-...

description: Lists a user's uploaded files by merging live storage data with message-derived metadata.
heuristics: GET requires an authenticated session and scopes R2 listing to scira/users/{userId}/
On the first page it also recovers legacy R2 and Vercel Blob files from message parts
Returns pagination via nextCursor and isTruncated with private cache headers

description: Deletes a stored file only after validating ownership against its key or chat message usage.
heuristics: Rejects non-R2 and non-Vercel-Blob URLs before any delete attempt
Allows direct deletion when the R2 key is under the caller's scira/users/{userId}/ prefix
Otherwise requires the file URL to appear in one of the caller's user message parts

description: Semantically searches uploaded docs and optionally reranks the matching chunks.
heuristics: Supports PDF, CSV, DOCX, XLSX, and XLS files only
Builds a VectorStoreIndex per file with Cohere embed-v4.0 and similarityTopK 10
Optional rerank uses Cohere rerank-v4.0-pro, otherwise results are sorted by retriever score

description: Searches a user's Supermemory memory store inside the user-scoped container tag.
heuristics: createMemoryTools scopes Supermemory tools with containerTags set to the user id
Server-side memory search returns an empty result immediately for blank queries
All memory search entrypoints require an authenticated user

description: Stores user memory entries in Supermemory using the user's container tag as the scope key.
heuristics: The add memory tool is created by supermemoryTools with containerTags set to the user id
Memory CRUD helpers require authentication before create, list, or delete work
Stored memory is scoped by user container tags rather than by chat ids

description: Starts an OAuth connector link for Google Drive or Notion with provider-specific sync limits.
heuristics: Rejects OneDrive with a coming soon error instead of opening a connection
Sends redirectUrl /connectors/{provider}/callback and containerTags [userId, syncTag]
Applies the provider's configured documentLimit and source metadata during connection creation

description: Triggers a connector resync and checks imported document counts per provider.
heuristics: Manual sync calls client.connections.import with the user id container tag
Google Drive document count comes from metadata.pageToken when available
Other providers use listDocuments and accept array, object, or numeric count responses

description: Searches synced docs from user connectors and enriches valid matches with provider links.
heuristics: When provider is all, it fans out across selected connectors or all configured connectors in parallel
Each search uses containerTags [userId, syncTag], limit 15, rerank true, and includeSummary true
Drops docs whose chunks, summary, and content are empty or equal to Empty Chunk

description: Plans a multi-step research run that can search, scrape, code, and package findings into one result.
heuristics: Extreme search supports the scira-ext-1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 model ids
Code runner instructions enforce one chart per execution and forbid savefig
Final research output later aggregates searches, files, charts, and canvas parts from tool results

description: Runs chained source searches so extreme research can cover multiple angles before synthesis.
heuristics: Multi-agent instructions explicitly tell the model to search broadly first and then narrow
The research prompt tells the agent to cross-check important claims across multiple sources
Mode instructions prefer using both web and X when both published sources and social signals matter

description: Scrapes webpage bodies during research and falls back to metadata extraction when needed.
heuristics: Uses scrapeWebpageWithNotte for main-content extraction when rich scraping is needed
Falls back to metadata.scira.app when direct extraction fails
Filters candidate URLs down to valid http and https links before scraping

description: Indexes uploaded research files into retrievers so later extreme-search steps can query them semantically.
heuristics: Loads PDFs, CSVs, DOCX files, and Excel sheets with dedicated reader classes
Builds a VectorStoreIndex per file with Cohere embed-v4.0 via vercelEmbedding
Logs indexing failures per file and continues building other retrievers

description: Reranks retrieved research chunks so the final answer uses the strongest file evidence first.
heuristics: Uses Cohere rerank-v4.0-pro when shouldRerank is true
Limits reranked output with topN set to maxResults
Without rerank, sorts chunks by retriever score descending

description: Executes Python research code in a Daytona sandbox and returns the run result.
heuristics: Creates sandboxes from SNAPSHOT_NAME in the us Daytona target
Installs missing Python libraries with pip before codeRun when requested
Deletes the sandbox asynchronously after execution so cleanup does not block the result

description: Turns code-run chart output into downloadable chart artifacts for research results.
heuristics: Code runner instructions allow only one chart per execution
Chart PNG data URIs are stripped to base64 and uploaded as scira/charts/{id}.png
Missing chart titles fall back to the code title or to Chart N

description: Uploads research artifacts to public R2 URLs so the frontend can render them after the run.
heuristics: Stores chart files in Cloudflare R2 using the configured public bucket URL prefix
Passes through existing chart.url values without re-uploading them
Filters out per-chart upload failures instead of failing the whole research run

description: Renders structured canvas research specs into an interactive report with state-backed components.
heuristics: CanvasRenderer sanitizes singleton children into arrays before rendering
Rendering is wrapped in StateProvider, VisibilityProvider, and ActionProvider
Extreme search references the canvas catalog prompt when asking for canvas output

description: Creates or reconnects a cloud build box with the requested runtime and warm startup steps.
heuristics: box_init must be the first sandbox action and selects one of node, python, golang, ruby, or rust
BoxManager reconnects to an existing box id before creating a new box
Initialization warms the box, installs Bun, and writes uploaded files into /workspace/home

description: Attaches enabled user MCP servers to the build box so the coding agent can call them.
heuristics: Loads only enabled user MCP servers and resolves auth headers before Box.create
Always includes the anthropic web-search MCP alongside the user's servers
Flags Vercel OAuth MCP so the agent can later extract the token from mcp-config.json

description: Runs shell commands, file writes, and autonomous agent work inside the persisted build box.
heuristics: box_exec streams running, completed, or error states into data-build_search events
box_write_file writes arbitrary absolute paths before the agent runs
box_agent streams tool calls, free text, and cost while warning about available MCP servers and uploaded files

description: Registers a user MCP server with validated transport, auth mode, and encrypted credentials.
heuristics: Creation restricts name to 80 chars and transportType to http or sse
validateMcpServerUrl rejects non-https URLs except localhost http in development
Bearer, header, and OAuth secrets are encrypted before insertion

description: Updates MCP server config, disabled tools, and stored credentials without recreating the record.
heuristics: PATCH can explicitly clear credentials or OAuth tokens
Switching away from oauth wipes access token, refresh token, expiry, connectedAt, and oauthError
disabledTools is persisted so dynamic tool loading can skip selected MCP tools later

description: Deletes a user's MCP server record after ownership and Pro checks pass.
heuristics: DELETE is scoped by both server id and user id
Missing servers return a not_found API error
Successful deletions return a simple ok true response

description: Opens an MCP client against saved or ad hoc config and records the last test result.
heuristics: Accepts either a stored serverId or a direct transportType and url pair
Normalizes one common HTTP not initialized failure into an SSE or session-endpoint hint
Writes lastTestedAt and lastError back to the saved server on both success and failure

description: Lists the tools exposed by a saved MCP server after resolving auth headers.
heuristics: Builds a real MCP client from the server's saved transport and resolved headers
Returns only name, title, and description for each listed tool
Always closes the MCP client in a finally block

description: Builds the HTTP headers required to call an MCP server for bearer, header, or OAuth auth.
heuristics: Non-oauth auth returns decrypted bearer or custom header credentials from encryptedCredentials
OAuth auth first resolves a usable access token and then returns Authorization Bearer
Auth type none returns an empty headers object

description: Starts an MCP OAuth authorization flow with PKCE, signed state, and provider-specific handling.
heuristics: Uses PKCE with a SHA-256 code challenge and HMAC-signed state payloads
GitHub, Slack, Vercel, and Canva flows require explicit or managed client credentials
Vercel automatically adds offline_access and Slack user OAuth sends both scope and user_scope

description: Refreshes stored MCP OAuth tokens when the access token is missing or near expiry.
heuristics: Keeps an access token only if it expires more than 60 seconds from now
If no refresh token exists, clears stored tokens and marks the server disconnected with an oauthError
Refresh success rewrites encrypted access and refresh tokens, expiry, connectedAt, and clears oauthError

description: Loads enabled MCP tools into the search runtime and namespaces them to avoid collisions.
heuristics: Skips OAuth servers that are not yet connected when enabledOnly is true
Namespaces tools as mcp_{serverSlug}_{toolName} and appends numeric suffixes when needed
Omits any tool listed in the server's disabledTools array

description: Waits for user responses to MCP elicitation prompts across server instances via Redis.
heuristics: Persists pending and response state in Redis so another instance can resolve the request
Polls Redis every 500 ms while also supporting an in-process resolver map
Times out after 5 minutes and emits both start and done data-stream events

description: Bridges MCP app tool and resource calls from the web app to the user's saved server.
heuristics: Allows only tools/call, resources/list, resources/read, resources/templates/list, and prompts/list
Tool calls look up the named tool from client.tools and execute it with parsed arguments
resources/read rejects requests that omit the uri parameter

description: Exports chat content to PDF or DOCX after normalizing markdown citations and math blocks.
heuristics: Both exporters reject empty content payloads before rendering
PDF and DOCX preprocessors turn footnotes into inline numbered notes and normalize LaTeX
PDF embeds custom Geist fonts while DOCX builds structured paragraphs, lists, and tables

description: Renders a public share page for a chat with OG metadata and converted UI messages.
heuristics: Non-public chats return notFound instead of rendering a share view
OpenGraph and Twitter metadata point to /api/og/chat/{id}
Messages are converted with convertToUIMessages before ShareViewer receives them

description: Builds chat and X Wrapped OG images from sanitized text and bundled fonts.
heuristics: Chat OG strips markdown, code fences, file names, and citation markers before display
Non-public chats get a generic Scira OG card instead of message previews
Font files are loaded from app/api/og/chat/[id]/fonts at request time

description: Fetches remote images through a same-origin proxy with long-lived cache headers.
heuristics: Rejects missing or invalid url query parameters before fetch
Sends a desktop Chrome-style User-Agent on upstream image requests
Successful responses set immutable one-year cache headers and Access-Control-Allow-Origin *

description: Runs uploaded images through a guard model to classify risky visual content before use.
heuristics: Wraps each image as a separate user message containing an image part
Uses groq meta-llama/llama-guard-4-12b for classification
Returns the raw moderation model text for caller-side allow or deny handling

description: Maps streamed tool parts to specialized React components for each tool result type.
heuristics: Treats any part type starting with tool- or equal to dynamic-tool as a tool invocation
Loads browser-only map and chart renderers with ssr false
Routes stock, retrieve, extreme, Reddit, GitHub, prediction, and other tools to dedicated result components

description: Renders model reasoning with debounced state changes, auto-scroll, and markdown formatting.
heuristics: Debounces the complete state by 150 ms so the Thinking label does not flicker
Auto-scrolls the reasoning container while new text arrives
Auto-collapses only after reasoning stays complete for a short delay

description: Renders canvas catalog components through a registry-backed JSON renderer with safe spec cleanup.
heuristics: Sanitizes non-array children values into arrays before rendering the spec
Wraps rendering in StateProvider, VisibilityProvider, and ActionProvider
Unknown component types fall back to a registry fallback component

description: Renders interactive stock, research, and generic charts with lazy browser-only chart libraries.
heuristics: interactive-charts lazy-loads echarts-for-react with ssr false and a Skeleton loader
interactive-stock-chart swaps chart palettes based on the current theme
Extreme search chart cards support fullscreen Cambio previews and PNG downloads through blob or proxy fallback

description: Displays retrieved and searched sources as compact cards with favicons, metadata, and mobile panels.
heuristics: Retrieve result thumbnails proxy known social-media hosts through /api/proxy-image
Card favicons come from explicit metadata first and then Google s2 fallback URLs
Single-result and multi-result views switch between Sheet and Drawer depending on mobile state

description: Paginates, deletes, and bulk-deletes chats while keeping pinned chats ordered ahead of the rest.
heuristics: Chat listings fetch limit plus one row to compute hasMore
Ordering is by isPinned desc, updatedAt desc, then id desc
Bulk delete runs per-chat deletions with allSettled and throws if any deletion fails

description: Toggles visibility and pin state so chats can stay private or be shared publicly.
heuristics: updateChatVisibility returns a plain serializable object with rowCount and the new visibility
Pinned state is updated through updateChatPinnedById without changing visibility
Public share pages and OG cards render only when chat.visibility is public

description: Generates a short plain-text chat title from the first user message.
heuristics: Only the first text part of the user message is used as the title prompt
The system prompt enforces 3 to 4 words, plain text, and no quotes or colons
Provider options disable thinking and force low verbosity across OpenAI, Google, and Vertex paths

description: Suggests 3 to 5 next questions that stay specific to the conversation and available tools.
heuristics: Structured output requires between 3 and 5 follow-up questions
Prompt forbids pronouns and requires specific nouns or entities from context
It remaps some topics, such as weather or location chat, into more useful next-query categories

description: Persists merged user preferences and clears caches so later requests see the new settings.
heuristics: POST /api/preferences rejects payloads that are missing, arrays, or non-objects
upsertUserPreferences merges incoming keys over existing JSON inside a transaction
Preference caches are cleared after save and cached values are served on GET

description: Creates one-off or recurring scheduled searches with per-user caps and QStash delivery.
heuristics: Only Pro users can create lookouts, with a cap of 10 total and 5 active daily lookouts
One-time runs use qstash publish with a minimum 5 second delay, recurring runs use qstash schedules.create
nextRunAt is computed from cron-parser for recurring jobs or a one-time local date calculation

description: Executes a lookout by creating a private chat, streaming the answer, and recording run metrics.
heuristics: Retries lookout lookup up to 3 times with 500, 1000, and 2000 ms backoff
Creates the chat, initial user message, stream id, and running status before streaming tools
Success writes duration, token count, and tool-call count into the lookout run history

description: Emails the completed lookout summary to the user after truncating it at a safe markdown boundary.
heuristics: Sends email only when the user record has an email address
Uses event.text first and falls back to assistant message parts when the text is empty
truncateMarkdown cuts at a clean paragraph or sentence boundary and caps email content at 2000 chars

description: Runs a real-time voice session with mic capture, server VAD, and tool-enabled assistant replies.
heuristics: Creates a 48 kHz AudioContext and starts microphone capture before network awaits
Fetches an ephemeral session secret from /session and opens wss://api.x.ai/v1/realtime
session.update enables only web_search and x_search and uses server_vad with threshold 0.4 and 100 ms silence

description: Transcribes an uploaded audio blob into text with the ElevenLabs transcription model.
heuristics: Requires multipart formData with an audio field that is a Blob
Uses experimental_transcribe with elevenlabs transcription scribe_v2
Returns HTTP 400 for missing audio and HTTP 500 for processing failures

description: Runs a filtered X query with optional date and handle filters through a forced tool step.
heuristics: Defaults the search window to the last 15 days when startDate or endDate is blank
Strips @ from handles and forbids includeXHandles together with excludeXHandles
The route forces step 0 to call the xql tool and the tool then calls xai.tools.xSearch once

description: Calls Bing Web Search when you need top web results from the configured Microsoft API key.
heuristics: Sends GET to BING_SEARCH_V7_ENDPOINT with q and mkt=en-US.
Passes the key in Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key.
Returns json_content["webPages"]["value"][:8] or [] on missing keys.

description: Calls Google CSE when you need Programmable Search results for a query.
heuristics: GETs GOOGLE_SEARCH_ENDPOINT with key, cx, q, and num=8.
Uses the shared 5 second search timeout.
Returns json_content["items"][:8] or [] when items is absent.

description: Calls Serper when you want Google-style results through the Serper API.
heuristics: POSTs JSON with q and num rounded up to the next multiple of 10.
Uses X-API-KEY plus application/json headers.
Applies the shared 5 second timeout to the request.

description: Normalizes Serper payloads when you need Bing-like source objects for downstream RAG.
heuristics: Prepends knowledgeGraph and answerBox entries only when both url and snippet exist.
Maps each organic result to name, url, and snippet fields.
Truncates the merged contexts list to 8 entries.

description: Calls SearchApi when you want Google search results through SearchApi.io.
heuristics: GETs SEARCHAPI_SEARCH_ENDPOINT with q, engine=google, and num rounded to a multiple of 10.
Uses Authorization: Bearer <key> for auth.
Uses a 30 second timeout instead of the shared 5 second timeout.

description: Normalizes SearchApi payloads when you need answer boxes and organic hits in one context list.
heuristics: Adds answer_box only when url and answer/snippet are both present.
Appends organic_results, then related_questions entries with source.link plus answer text.
Truncates the merged contexts list to 8 entries.

description: Delegates search and RAG remotely when BACKEND is LEPTON.
heuristics: Creates Client("https://search-api.lepton.run/", stream=True) during init.
Forwards query, search_uuid, and generate_related_questions unchanged.
Wraps the remote iterator in StreamingResponse with media_type="text/html".

description: Builds the Lepton-backed OpenAI client when answer or follow-up generation needs an LLM.
heuristics: Uses base_url https://{self.model}.lepton.run/api/v1/.
Takes the API key from LEPTON_WORKSPACE_TOKEN or the current workspace token.
Sets httpx timeouts to connect=10, read=120, write=120, pool=10.

description: Logs into the Lepton workspace at startup when backend or LLM calls need workspace auth.
heuristics: Calls leptonai.api.v0.workspace.login() at the start of init().
Workspace token fallback comes from WorkspaceInfoLocalRecord.get_current_workspace_token().

description: Strips simple prompt-injection tags when a local backend is about to search and answer.
heuristics: Removes [INST] and [/INST] with re.sub(r"\[/?INST\]", "", query).
Runs after defaulting the query and before search_function(query).

description: Falls back to a canned question when the local backend receives an empty query.
heuristics: Uses "Who said 'live long and prosper'?" as the fallback query.
Applies only on the local search path after the LEPTON early return.

description: Formats snippets as numbered citation context when preparing the answer prompt.
heuristics: Joins snippets as [[citation:{i+1}]] {snippet} separated by blank lines.
System prompt requires [citation:x] references and answer language matching the question.

description: Streams the answer tokens when the app has search context and an LLM client ready.
heuristics: Calls chat.completions.create with stream=True using system prompt plus user query.
Uses max_tokens=1024, temperature=0.9, and stop=stop_words.
Emits chunk.choices[0].delta.content or an empty string for each token chunk.

description: Generates follow-up questions when RELATED_QUESTIONS is enabled and context is available.
heuristics: Uses a separate prompt built from context snippets and the original query.
Requests a function tool call matching ask_related_questions(List[str]).
Parses tool arguments as JSON and returns at most 5 questions, else [].

description: Serves the POST /query API when the UI needs a streaming search-and-answer response.
heuristics: Decorated with @Photon.handler(method="POST", path="/query").
Accepts query, search_uuid, and generate_related_questions=True.
Raises HTTP 400 when search_uuid is missing.

description: Creates the shared KV store at init when results must be replayable by search UUID.
heuristics: Instantiates KV(os.environ["KV_NAME"], create_if_not_exists=True, error_if_exists=False).
Logs that first-time KV creation may take a while.

description: Replays a stored response when the incoming search_uuid already exists in KV.
heuristics: Checks self.kv.get(search_uuid) before any new search or LLM call.
Wraps the cached string in a one-yield generator for StreamingResponse.
Logs misses and KV errors, then regenerates instead of failing.

description: Sends raw source context first when beginning the multiplexed response stream.
heuristics: Yields json.dumps(contexts) as the first stream section.

Immediately follows with 

__LLM_RESPONSE__

 as the section marker.

description: Streams answer text after the source block when the LLM response iterator yields chunks.
heuristics: Prepends a grain-of-salt warning when contexts is empty.
