Only emits token text from chunks that have a non-empty choices list.

description: Appends related questions last when follow-up generation was scheduled.
heuristics: Waits for related_questions_future.result() after answer streaming finishes.

Emits 

__RELATED_QUESTIONS__

 before the JSON payload.
Falls back to "[]" if JSON serialization of the result fails.

description: Warns the user when search returns no sources and the answer may be weakly grounded.
heuristics: Runs only when not contexts before any answer tokens are emitted.
Uses the exact message that the answer should be taken with a grain of salt.

description: Caches the final streamed payload in the background when a new response completes.
heuristics: Accumulates every yielded chunk in all_yielded_results while streaming.
Submits self.kv.put(search_uuid, "".join(all_yielded_results)) to the executor after streaming.
Does not await the KV write before returning to the client.

description: Builds the search page URL when the UI needs to carry both query text and search UUID.
heuristics: Uses /search.html in production and /search in development.
Appends q via encodeURIComponent and rid via the provided search_uuid.

description: Submits a typed question when the user presses enter or clicks the search button.
heuristics: Prevents the default form submit behavior.
Ignores empty input values.
Clears the field and pushes getSearchUrl(encodeURIComponent(value), nanoid()).

description: Reads the current query and request id when the search results page loads.
heuristics: Uses useSearchParams() inside the client SearchPage component.
Reads q and rid, defaults missing values to an empty string, and decodes both.

description: Reruns the same question with a fresh cache key when the user clicks Rewrite.
heuristics: The Rewrite button calls router.push with getSearchUrl(encodeURIComponent(query), nanoid()).
Keeps the displayed query text unchanged while replacing rid.

description: Starts the backend stream when the results view requests /query.
heuristics: POSTs to /query with JSON body containing query and search_uuid.
Sends controller.signal so unmounting can abort the request.
Calls onError(status) and returns early for non-200 responses.

description: Recursively forwards response chunks when the client wraps fetch body reading in a stream.
heuristics: Reads from response.body.getReader() until done is true.
Invokes onChunk(value) before controller.enqueue(value).
Calls onDone() and controller.close() when the reader finishes.

description: Splits the multiplexed stream when raw sources, answer text, and related questions arrive.
heuristics: Buffers bytes into one Uint8Array and decodes them incrementally with TextDecoder.
Treats __LLM_RESPONSE__ as the source/answer boundary.
Treats __RELATED_QUESTIONS__ as the answer/related boundary.

description: Normalizes streamed citation markup when backend text uses several citation bracket variants.
heuristics: Rewrites [[citation:x]]-style forms into markdown links [citation](x).
Normalizes both Citation and citation spellings before rendering.

description: Routes parsed stream sections into React state when new sources, answer text, or relates appear.
heuristics: Parses the pre-LLM chunk as JSON sources exactly once using sourcesEmitted.
Sends normalized markdown to onMarkdown as answer text grows.
Parses related questions at stream end and falls back to an empty list on JSON errors.

description: Renders the streamed answer as markdown when text has arrived from the backend.
heuristics: Uses react-markdown inside a prose prose-sm container.
Shows five Skeleton rows while markdown is still empty.
Overrides markdown links with a custom citation renderer.

description: Shows a citation popover when the reader hovers or taps an inline source marker.
heuristics: Resolves citation href N to sources[N-1].
Popover content shows source name, snippet, optional thumbnail, URL, and favicon.
Missing href or unresolved source returns an empty fragment.

description: Shows fetched sources as clickable cards when the backend has returned context items.
heuristics: Derives the display domain with new URL(url).hostname.
Renders a 2-column grid that becomes 4 columns on small screens.
Displays the 1-based index and a Google favicon for each source.

description: Renders suggested follow-up searches when related questions are available.
heuristics: Maps each related question to a PresetQuery link.
Shows "No related questions." for an empty relates array.
Shows three Skeleton rows while relates is still null.

description: Displays placeholder skeletons while answer, sources, or related questions are still loading.
heuristics: Skeleton uses animate-pulse with rounded corners.
Answer, Sources, and Relates each render skeleton placeholders before data arrives.

description: Shows a blocking overlay when the query request fails.
heuristics: The overlay appears whenever error state is truthy.
HTTP 429 shows a too-many-requests message.
All other statuses show an overloaded-service message.

description: Mounts the built UI bundle when Photon serves the frontend from the backend process.
heuristics: Uses @Photon.handler(mount=True) for the UI handler.
Returns StaticFiles(directory="ui") as the mounted app.

description: Redirects the site root when the deployed app should open the built UI entry page.
heuristics: Uses @Photon.handler(method="GET", path="/").
Returns RedirectResponse(url="/ui/index.html").

description: Calls Search1API when you want Google-backed web results through one hosted endpoint.
heuristics: Posts JSON to `https://api.search1api.com/search/` with `max_results: 10` and `search_service: google`.
Sends `Authorization: Bearer <SEARCH1API_KEY>` and raises `HTTPException` on non-OK responses.
Normalizes each hit by copying `title -> name` and `link -> url`, then keeps `REFERENCE_COUNT` items.

description: Queries Bing Web Search when the deployment is configured around Microsoft search.
heuristics: GETs `BING_SEARCH_V7_ENDPOINT` with params `q` and `mkt=en-US`.
Uses header `Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key` and a `DEFAULT_SEARCH_ENGINE_TIMEOUT` timeout.
Reads `webPages.value[:REFERENCE_COUNT]` and returns `[]` if that shape is missing.

description: Uses Google Programmable Search when you have a CX and API key configured.
heuristics: GETs `GOOGLE_SEARCH_ENDPOINT` with `key`, `cx`, `q`, and `num=REFERENCE_COUNT`.
Maps each item's `title` to `name` and `link` to `url` for downstream code.
Applies the shared search timeout and raises `HTTPException` on failed responses.

description: Runs a SerpApi Google search when you want hosted Google results without managing CX.
heuristics: Builds `GoogleSearch` params with `engine: google`, `q`, and `api_key`.
Transforms `organic_results` into `{name,url,snippet}` records and defaults missing snippets to `""`.
Returns only the first `REFERENCE_COUNT` normalized results.

description: Queries a self-hosted SearXNG instance when you want low-cost multi-engine search.
heuristics: Prefixes queries with `:auto `, URL-encodes them, and requests `category=general&format=json&engines=bing%2Cgoogle`.
Iterates over at most the first 9 SearXNG results and builds `conv_links` records.
Falls back to `conv_links` because the threaded full-page extraction block is commented out.

description: Converts provider-specific hits into a common source shape before prompting and rendering.
heuristics: Search1API and Google adapters rewrite provider fields to shared `name` and `url` keys.
SerpApi emits fresh `{name,url,snippet}` dicts from `organic_results` instead of raw API objects.
All adapters cap result lists with `REFERENCE_COUNT` or an explicit top-N slice.

description: Adds display metadata like site names and icons so sources render as recognizable cards.
heuristics: The SearXNG adapter derives `site_name` with `tldextract.extract(url).domain`.
It also builds `icon_url` as `scheme://netloc/favicon.ico` from `urlparse(url)`.
The UI derives `hostname` with `new URL(url).hostname` and fetches favicons from Google's `s2` endpoint.

description: Fetches a page and extracts readable text when richer snippets are needed.
heuristics: `extract_url_content` calls `trafilatura.fetch_url(url)` and then `trafilatura.extract(downloaded)`.
It returns only `{url, content}` so callers can merge extracted text back into search results.
The SearXNG executor path that would batch this extraction is present but commented out.

description: Creates the right async LLM client from the selected model and configured keys.
heuristics: Models containing `claude-3` use `AsyncAnthropic` with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.
All other models use `AsyncOpenAI` with `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `GROQ_API_KEY`.
The OpenAI-compatible client also receives `OPENAI_BASE_URL` and the shared `httpx.AsyncClient`.

description: Strips simple prompt-control tokens from user input before search and synthesis.
heuristics: `query_function` removes both `[INST]` and `[/INST]` with one regex before searching.
Sanitization happens after cache checks but before any new backend query runs.
No broader prompt-cleaning is applied beyond this targeted replacement.

description: Builds the answer system prompt by numbering snippets and embedding citation markers.
heuristics: `system_prompt` formats `_rag_query_text` with one block per context: `[[citation:n]] snippet`.
The template instructs the model to stay in the user's language and limit answers to 1024 tokens.
It explicitly asks for `[citation:x]` tags at sentence ends and a fallback `information is missing on ...` phrase.

description: Generates short follow-up questions from the query and retrieved snippets.
heuristics: The prompt asks for follow-ups in the original language and no longer than 20 words each.
It starts as a future before answer streaming finishes when related questions are enabled.
Returned questions are normalized to `[{question: ...}]` and trimmed to at most 5 items.

description: Uses Claude tool calling to request structured related-question output.
heuristics: Calls `client.beta.tools.messages.create` with a tool named `ask_related_questions`.
The tool schema requires an object containing a `questions` string array.
The parser scans `response.content` for a `tool_use` block with that exact tool name.

description: Uses OpenAI function calling to force structured related-question generation.
heuristics: Sends a `tools` array with function `ask_related_questions` and forces it via `tool_choice`.
Primary parsing path reads `message.tool_calls[0].function.arguments` as JSON.
Fallback parsing accepts plain text lines starting with `1.`, `2.`, or `3.` and strips quotes.

description: Streams Claude text tokens while interleaving sources and follow-up sections.
heuristics: It sends `json.dumps(contexts)` first, then the sentinel `__LLM_RESPONSE__`.
Claude output is read from `client.messages.stream(...).text_stream` and forwarded chunk by chunk.
Related questions run concurrently via `asyncio.create_task` and are appended after `__RELATED_QUESTIONS__`.

description: Streams OpenAI-compatible chat output through the shared sentinel protocol.
heuristics: Creates chat completions with `stream=True`, `max_tokens=1024`, and `temperature=0.9`.
`_raw_stream_response` yields contexts, `__LLM_RESPONSE__`, answer deltas, and optional related questions.
Each streamed chunk contributes `chunk.choices[0].delta.content or ""` to the response body.

description: Bootstraps runtime state, credentials, concurrency, and storage before requests arrive.
heuristics: `server_init` reads backend and model env vars and stores them on `_app.ctx`.
It creates a thread pool with `max_workers = handler_max_concurrency * 2` and opens `KVWrapper` on `KV_NAME` or `search.db`.
`RELATED_QUESTIONS` and `CHAT_HISTORY` are enabled only when env values are `1`, `yes`, or `true`.

description: Merges query args, form data, and JSON into one request params object.
heuristics: `get_query_object` starts from `request.args` and keeps the first value for each key.
If the content type contains `form`, it overlays `request.form`; otherwise it tries `request.json`.
Invalid JSON bodies are ignored by catching `InvalidUsage` instead of failing the request.

description: Returns a stored streamed response when the same search session is requested again.
heuristics: When chat history is off, cached dict results are reused only if `result["query"] == query`.
When chat history is on and the latest history query matches, the handler returns `result["txt"]` immediately.
`KeyError` or KV failures log and fall back to regenerating instead of aborting the request.

description: Loads prior turns and stored results so a search session can continue coherently.
heuristics: History loading only runs when `CHAT_HISTORY` is enabled and a `search_uuid` is provided.
The handler fetches both `${search_uuid}_history` and `search_uuid` from SQLite via the executor.
Only history entries containing both `query` and `llm_response` are turned into chat messages.

description: Reuses the last turn's search results when the user asks a follow-up in the same session.
heuristics: If `old_query != query`, the handler reuses `history[-1]["search_results"]` as `contexts`.
It rebuilds `chat_history` as alternating user and assistant messages from stored entries.
A new web search is skipped whenever reused `contexts` are already populated.

description: Splits a stored raw response into sources, answer text, and related questions.
heuristics: `extract_all_sections` uses one DOTALL regex around `__LLM_RESPONSE__` and optional `__RELATED_QUESTIONS__`.
Matched groups are `.strip()`ed so cached sections can be parsed back cleanly.
If the sentinels are missing, it returns `None` for all three sections.

description: Appends a completed turn to persistent history with bounded session length.
heuristics: `KVWrapper.append` initializes missing history keys to `[]` and commits after every write.
Before appending, it trims stored turns to the last `MAX_HISTORY_LEN` entries.
The saved history item contains `query`, `search_results`, `llm_response`, and `related_questions`.

description: Stores the full streamed payload under the session id for later replay.
heuristics: After `response.eof()`, the handler submits `kv.put(search_uuid, ...)` on the executor.
The cached value is a dict with both `query` and `txt`, where `txt` is the joined raw stream.
The raw cache preserves sentinel markers so the client can replay sources, answer text, and follow-ups.

description: Orchestrates search, prompting, streaming, and caching behind the `/query` API.
heuristics: `/query` only accepts POST and requires both `query` and `search_uuid`, otherwise it raises `HTTPException`.
It conditionally searches, builds the prompt, and selects Claude or OpenAI streaming by model name.
Unexpected failures return `{"message": "Internal server error."}` with HTTP 503.

description: Recursively reads response bytes so the UI can react to each arriving chunk.
heuristics: `fetchStream` grabs `response.body!.getReader()` and wraps it in a new `ReadableStream`.
`pump` calls `reader.read()` repeatedly, forwards each `Uint8Array` to `onChunk`, and enqueues it.
When `done` is true, it runs `onDone` and closes the stream controller.

description: Parses the sentinel stream into source cards, answer markdown, and related queries.
heuristics: The client POSTs `{query, search_uuid, lang}` to `/query` and aborts with `onError` if status is not 200.
It accumulates bytes in one `Uint8Array`, decodes them, and splits on `__LLM_RESPONSE__` first.
Sources are emitted once via `sourcesEmitted`, and related questions are parsed only after `__RELATED_QUESTIONS__` on completion.

description: Rewrites citation tokens into markdown links the answer renderer can intercept.
heuristics: The parser normalizes case variants like `[[Citation:x]]` to the lowercase `citation` form.
A regex chain converts bracketed citations into link syntax like `[citation](3)`.
Normalization happens incrementally on the streaming answer text before state updates.

description: Renders the streamed answer as markdown and swaps in skeletons while loading.
heuristics: `Answer` shows five skeleton lines until the `markdown` prop becomes non-empty.
It uses `react-markdown` and overrides anchor rendering instead of using default links.
Citation anchors resolve sources by numeric `href - 1`, and missing hrefs render nothing.

description: Shows a source preview popover when the user clicks a citation badge.
heuristics: Each citation badge opens a Radix `Popover` with `align="start"` content.
The popover shows source name, optional `primaryImageOfPage.thumbnailUrl`, snippet, URL, and favicon.
Badges are numeric pills derived from the citation link target, not raw markdown text.

description: Builds search URLs and session ids so users can start new searches or continue one.
heuristics: New searches generate a fresh `nanoid()` rid, while continue mode reuses the current `rid` from the URL.
`getSearchUrl` targets `/search` in dev and `/search.html` in production.
`SearchPage` decodes `q` and `rid` from query params and keys `Result` by `rid`.

description: Displays retrieved sources as clickable cards with rank and domain metadata.
heuristics: The grid uses `grid-cols-2` on small screens and `sm:grid-cols-4` on larger screens.
Each card overlays an absolute `<a>` across the whole tile and shows `index + 1 - hostname`.
When sources are empty, four skeleton source tiles are rendered instead of cards.

description: Renders suggested follow-up queries as reusable search links below the answer.
heuristics: `Relates` treats `null` as loading, `[]` as an empty state, and non-empty arrays as buttons.
Each question is rendered through `PresetQuery`, which builds a fresh rid-backed link.
The empty state text comes from `Locale.Relates.no_related_questions`.

description: Shows a blocking overlay when the streaming request fails.
heuristics: `parseStreaming` calls `onError(status)` whenever `/query` returns a non-200 response.
`Result` renders an absolute blurred overlay only when `error` state is set.
HTTP 429 gets `Locale.Err[429]`; every other failure falls back to `Locale.Err[500]`.

description: Chooses the UI language from saved preference or the browser's language.
heuristics: `getLang` first reads `localStorage['lang']` and accepts it only if it matches `AllLangs`.
If nothing valid is saved, it lowercases `navigator.language` and checks whether it includes `en`, `cn`, or `jp`.
The fallback locale is hardcoded to `en`.

description: Persists a new language choice and reloads the app so locale strings update.
heuristics: `changeLang` writes the selected language to `localStorage` under the `lang` key.
It immediately calls `location.reload()` instead of updating locale state in place.
The home page `<select>` binds `value={getLang()}` and forwards changes into `changeLang`.

description: Serves the exported frontend and API from the same Sanic process.
heuristics: `app.static('/ui', os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'ui/'))` exposes the exported asset directory.
`app.static('/', os.path.join(BASE_DIR, 'ui/index.html'))` makes the homepage a static file.
The production frontend is built around the `/ui` path, including assets like `/ui/bg.svg`.

description: Exports the Next.js app as static files so Python can ship the whole product.
heuristics: In production, `next.config.mjs` sets `output: 'export'`, `assetPrefix: '/ui/'`, and `basePath: '/ui'`.
Production builds write into `../ui`, aligning the frontend output with Sanic's static mount.
The Docker build sets `NODE_ENV=production`, runs `npm run build`, and copies the result to `/app/ui`.

description: Merge user YAML into defaults, including engine keep/remove rules.
heuristics: Non-engine mappings are deep-merged with `update_dict()`, except `use_default_settings`.
`use_default_settings.engines.remove` filters defaults before user engine overrides apply.
User engines update matching names in place or append new engine definitions.

description: Configure debug or production logging from `general.debug` settings.
heuristics: `general.debug` enables `coloredlogs` when available and `TERM` is not `dumb` or `unknown`.
Production mode sets root and `werkzeug` loggers to `WARNING` with `LOG_FORMAT_PROD`.
`SEARXNG_DEBUG_LOG_LEVEL` overrides the debug log level name.

description: Start locales, Valkey, plugins, search, limiter, and favicons.
heuristics: `webapp.init()` aborts in production if `server.secret_key` is still `ultrasecretkey`.
Initialization order is locales, Valkey, plugins, `search.initialize`, limiter, then favicons.
`search.initialize()` loads engines, initializes networks, metrics, and the processor map.

description: Split the raw query into user text and control tokens.
heuristics: `RawTextQuery` splits on whitespace and keeps separator tokens instead of dropping them.
Only the last non-space token enables autocomplete suggestions in parsers.
Special tokens go to `query_parts`, while ordinary words go to `user_query_parts`.

description: Interpret `:language` tokens and normalize them to locale tags.
heuristics: `LanguageParser` lowercases input and converts underscores to hyphens before matching.
Known locales from `sxng_locales` are stored with uppercased territory like `en-US`.
`VALID_LANGUAGE_CODE` or `auto` is accepted even if it is not in selectable languages.

description: Read `<timeout` tokens and convert them into seconds.
heuristics: `TimeoutParser` only accepts digits after `<`.
Values below `100` mean seconds, while `100+` means milliseconds divided by `1000`.
Empty timeout tokens suggest `<3` and `<850` in autocomplete mode.

description: Map `!bangs` to engine shortcuts, engines, or categories.
heuristics: Single-bang tokens are normalized by replacing `-` and `_` with spaces and lowercasing.
Shortcut matches are resolved through `engine_shortcuts` before engine lookup.
Category bangs expand to `EngineRef` entries except disabled `(engine, category)` pairs and mark the query as specific.

description: Recognize `!!bang` redirects and attach ranked autocomplete choices.
heuristics: `ExternalBangParser` only matches tokens starting with `!!` and longer than two chars.
Trie lookup comes from `EXTERNAL_BANGS` and stores the bang name on `raw_text_query.external_bang`.
Autocomplete candidates are recursively expanded and sorted by negative rank, then bang name.

description: Choose categories from form data, cookies, or the general fallback.
heuristics: `parse_category_form` accepts `categories` CSV and `category_*` toggles only for known categories.
If no request categories are present, `preferences.get_value('categories')` is reused.
If selection is still empty, `get_selected_categories()` falls back to `['general']`.

description: Build a `SearchQuery` from form fields, prefs, and parsed modifiers.
heuristics: Missing `q` raises `SearxParameterException` before any parsing happens.
`language=auto` resolves to `preferences.client.locale_tag` or `all` in the final `SearchQuery`.
Engine refs are deduplicated, then split into valid, unknown, and missing-token lists.

description: Import an engine module and bind it to one engine definition.
heuristics: Engine names with underscores are rejected, and mixed-case names are lowercased in place.
The `engine` field is required and loaded as `<module>.py` from `ENGINE_DIR`.
Engines failing setup or activity checks return `None` and are marked inactive by `load_engines()`.

description: Overlay engine YAML onto module attributes and fill missing defaults.
heuristics: String categories are split on commas before assignment.
`about` dictionaries are merged with `module.about` instead of replaced wholesale.
Missing attributes are deep-copied from `ENGINE_DEFAULT_ARGS` to avoid shared state.

description: Reject malformed engine modules and incomplete public attributes.
heuristics: `check_engine_module()` raises if `engine.network` is an imported module instead of config data.
`is_missing_required_attributes()` treats any public attribute set to `None` as a config error.
`is_engine_active()` disables engines in `onions` unless Tor is configured.

description: Register engines globally by name, shortcut, and category.
heuristics: `register_engine()` exits on ambiguous engine names.
`register_engine()` also exits on ambiguous shortcut collisions.
Every engine category appends the engine object into the global `categories` map.

description: Swap engines to onion endpoints and longer timeouts when Tor is used.
heuristics: `using_tor_proxy()` is true if `outgoing.using_tor_proxy` or `engine.using_tor_proxy` is set.
Engines with `onion_url` get `search_url = onion_url + search_path` and `extra_proxy_timeout` added.
Engines in the `onions` category are skipped entirely when Tor is unavailable.

description: Fetch or reuse engine trait maps for languages and regions.
heuristics: `EngineTraits.fetch_traits()` only runs when the engine defines `fetch_traits()`.
`EngineTraitsMap.fetch_traits()` reuses persisted `ENGINE_TRAITS` data if live fetching raises.
Fetched trait maps log language and region counts per engine before storage.

description: Apply persisted traits to each engine and narrow fixed locales.
heuristics: If settings pin `engine.language` or `engine.region`, traits are reduced to that single mapping.
Unsupported pinned language or region raises `ValueError` during trait injection.
Trait lookup first uses `engine.name`, then falls back to `engine.engine` for shared modules.

description: Create one processor per active `engine_type` and register it.
heuristics: `PROCESSORS.init()` skips inactive engine specs and engines missing from the registry.
`processor_types` maps `online`, `offline`, `online_dictionary`, `online_currency`, and `online_url_search`.
If an engine defines `init()`, processor registration happens later in a daemon thread.

description: Ask built-in answerers first and skip provider search if they answer.
heuristics: `Search.search_answerers()` calls `searx.answerers.STORAGE.ask()` with the stripped query text.
Returned answers are extended into the result container with `engine_name=None`.
`Search.search()` only runs standard engine search when no answerer produced results.

description: Turn a valid `!!bang` into an immediate redirect target.
heuristics: `Search.search_external_bang()` stores `redirect_url` from `get_bang_url(search_query)`.
The search stops only when `redirect_url` is a string, so invalid bangs fall through.
`resolve_bang_definition()` substitutes the encoded query into the bang target URL.

description: Assemble per-engine request params and skip unsupported searches.
heuristics: Abstract `get_params()` skips engines when paging, `max_page`, or `time_range` requirements are unsupported.
Engine-specific `engine_data` is copied from `search_query.engine_data[engine.name]`.
Online processors add `Accept-Encoding`, `Cache-Control`, `DNT`, `Connection`, `User-Agent`, and `Accept-Language` headers.

description: Cap search timeout using engine defaults, user input, and instance max.
heuristics: `default_timeout` is the maximum timeout across all selected processors.
`actual_timeout` chooses the minimum of `default_timeout`, query `timeout_limit`, and `outgoing.max_request_timeout`.
The chosen timeout is logged with all three contributing values.

description: Skip engines that are temporarily suspended after repeated failures.
heuristics: Suspension state is shared by network identity when available, otherwise by engine name.
`extend_container_if_suspended()` records the engine as unresponsive with `suspended=True`.
Access-denied and transport exceptions can set `suspend_end_time` through `SuspendedStatus.suspend()`.

description: Run selected engine searches concurrently and join until timeout.
heuristics: Each request is wrapped with `copy_current_request_context` before spawning a thread.
Threads share one UUID `search_id` as their name so the join loop can find them.
Timed-out threads get `_timeout=True`, `unresponsive_engine('timeout')`, and an engine timeout log.

description: Call engine request/response hooks and merge results or failures.
heuristics: `OnlineProcessor.search()` initializes thread-local timeout, HTTP time, and network context first.
The `engine.request()` hook mutates params before the network call, then `engine.response()` parses the reply.
SSL, timeout, HTTP, CAPTCHA, and access-denied failures can suspend the engine.

description: Cache `AsyncClient` instances by TLS, redirects, IP, and proxy tuple.
heuristics: `Network.get_client()` keys clients by `verify`, `max_redirects`, `local_address`, and `proxies`.
`new_client()` builds per-pattern proxy mounts plus a base transport with `httpx.Limits`.
Tor-marked networks validate the proxy with `check.torproject.org` before caching the client.

description: Shuffle most TLS ciphers to vary the client fingerprint.
heuristics: `shuffle_ciphers()` preserves the first three ciphers and randomizes the rest.
SSL contexts are cached in `SSLCONTEXTS` by `proxy_url`, `cert`, `verify`, and `trust_env`.
Every `get_sslcontexts()` call reshuffles the cached `SSLContext` before reuse.

description: Cycle through configured source IPs or every host in a CIDR.
heuristics: CIDR entries expand with `ip_network(...).hosts()` before cycling.
If no source address is configured, `get_ipaddress_cycle()` yields `None` forever.
Each new client key consumes the next `local_address` from the cycle.

description: Round-robin proxy URLs per mount pattern across client creation.
heuristics: `iter_proxies()` normalizes requests-style schemes like `socks5:` to `socks5://`.
A single proxy string is mounted as `all://` for every protocol.
`get_proxy_cycles()` cycles each proxy list independently and yields the next tuple each time.

description: Retry failed HTTP calls and selected bad status responses.
heuristics: `RemoteProtocolError` gets one immediate retry with a fresh client without decrementing retries.
`retry_on_http_error` can be `True`, a list of status codes, or a single status code.
HTTP request and status exceptions are only raised once retries are exhausted.

description: Expose streamed responses as a response object plus raw byte iterator.
heuristics: `stream_chunk_to_queue()` yields the response first, then raw `65536`-byte chunks from `aiter_raw()`.
The patched `response.close()` drains the generator to avoid leaking the background future.
Exceptions during stream setup are queued so `stream()` can raise them synchronously.

description: Canonicalize URLs, text, dates, and engine tags on results.
heuristics: Missing URL schemes default to `http` when `parsed_url` is normalized.
Title and content whitespace collapse to single spaces, and duplicate content equal to title is cleared.
Valid engine names are added into the `result.engines` set during normalization.

description: Apply one URL filter across result, infobox, and media fields.
heuristics: `filter_urls()` visits `url`, `iframe_src`, `audio_src`, `img_src`, `thumbnail_src`, and `thumbnail`.
A filter return value of `False` removes that URL field or infobox entry, while a string rewrites it.
After URL filtering, `normalize_result_fields()` runs again to resync `parsed_url` and text state.

description: Deduplicate main results by normalized URL hash and merge fields.
heuristics: `MainResult` hashes ignore URL scheme and use template, netloc, path, query, fragment, and `img_src`.
Duplicate merges keep the longer title/content, copy missing fields, and append new positions.
If one duplicate uses a secure scheme ending in `s`, the merged URL is upgraded to it.

description: Combine infoboxes with the same id and keep richer values.
heuristics: Infoboxes only merge when their `id` fields match exactly after normalization.
URL entries are deduplicated by shared `entity` or identical `url` before appending.
A higher engine weight can replace the chosen engine, image, or content fields.

description: Collect answers, suggestions, corrections, counts, and `engine_data`.
heuristics: Legacy result dicts with `suggestion`, `answer`, `correction`, `infobox`, `number_of_results`, or `engine_data` are split out.
`engine_data` is stored under `result_container.engine_data[result.engine][result['key']]`.
`ResultContainer.paging` flips to true if any contributing engine has paging enabled.

description: Score merged results and interleave grouped categories for display.
heuristics: `calculate_score()` multiplies engine weights together and scales by the number of positions.
`priority='high'` adds full weight per position, while `priority='low'` zeroes the score.
Grouping reinserts similar template/category blocks only within `max_count=8` and `max_distance=20`.

description: Import plugin classes from settings and keep only active ones.
heuristics: `PluginStorage.load_settings()` imports each configured FQN and instantiates `PluginCfg(**plg_settings)`.
Plugin IDs must match the lowercase ASCII regex `[a-z][a-z0-9].*`.
`PluginStorage.init()` removes any plugin whose `init(app)` method returns `False`.

description: Run enabled `pre_search` hooks and stop on the first false result.
heuristics: Only plugins whose `id` is in `search.user_plugins` are executed.
Exceptions in `pre_search` are logged and ignored so later plugins still run.
The search is skipped as soon as one plugin returns a falsy value.

description: Let enabled plugins rewrite or drop each result as it arrives.
heuristics: `SearchWithPlugins` wires `result_container.on_result` to `PluginStorage.on_result()`.
Plugins run in order only when their `id` is enabled for the request.
The first falsy `on_result` return drops that result from further processing.

description: Append plugin results after search, with keyword gating when defined.
heuristics: `post_search()` inspects the first non-empty query token before running keyworded plugins.
Plugins with keywords are skipped unless that first token matches one of them.
Returned results are extended under an engine name prefixed with `plugin: `.

description: Register built-in answerer classes and legacy answerer modules.
heuristics: `AnswerStorage.load_builtins()` loads every non-underscore `.py` file as `searx.answerers.<name>.SXNGAnswerer`.
Legacy directories with `answerer.py` still load through `ModuleAnswerer` with a deprecation warning.
Each registered keyword maps to a list of answerer instances in the storage dict.

description: Dispatch answerers by the first non-empty word in the query.
heuristics: `AnswerStorage.ask()` ignores queries whose first token has no registered answerer.
Only answerers attached to that exact keyword are invoked.
Every returned answer gets `engine='answerer: <keyword>'` before being returned.

description: Evaluate math expressions client-side when the calculator plugin is enabled.
heuristics: The results-page router lazy-loads `Calculator` only if `client_settings.plugins` includes `calculator`.
`Calculator` uses a curated `mathjs/number` dependency set rather than the full library.
It appends an answer only when parsing and `node.evaluate()` both succeed.

description: Return a named hash digest for page-one queries like `sha256 text`.
heuristics: `hash_plugin` only runs on page `1` and matches `/(<algo>) (.*)/i` against the full query.
Supported algorithms are `md5`, `sha1`, `sha224`, `sha256`, `sha384`, and `sha512`.
Blank input after the algorithm short-circuits without producing an answer.

description: Rewrite, remove, or reprioritize results by hostname regexes.
heuristics: The `hostnames` plugin disables itself entirely when `settings['hostnames']` is missing.
Regex lists and mappings may be loaded from external YAML files via `get_yaml_cfg()`.
`REMOVE` patterns can drop whole results, while `HIGH` and `LOW` set `result.priority`.

description: Rewrite DOI-like result URLs to the selected open DOI resolver.
heuristics: Only the main result `url` field is rewritten; all other URL fields are left unchanged.
`extract_doi()` scans path first, then query values, with regex `10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s]+`.
Suffixes like `.pdf`, `.xml`, `/full`, `/meta`, and `/abstract` are stripped before rewriting.

description: Answer page-one `ip` or `user-agent` queries from the current request.
heuristics: `self_info` only returns answers on the first results page.
Queries matching `/^ip/i` return `ip_address(request.remote_addr).compressed`.
Queries matching `/^user-agent/i` return the `request.user_agent` string verbatim.

description: Convert locale-aware measured values between compatible unit symbols.
heuristics: The plugin searches for separators `in`, `to`, or `as` and only runs on page `1`.
Input numbers are parsed with Babel using the current UI locale, including scientific notation.
Ambiguous unit symbols are resolved by matching source and target entries with the same SI name.

description: Show the current local time for a queried place or the server zone.
heuristics: The plugin strips time-related keywords from the query before geolocating the remainder.
An empty remainder returns `DateTime(datetime.now()).l10n()` immediately.
`GeoLocation.by_query()` drives timezone lookup, and the answer includes the zone abbreviation.

description: Check whether the caller IP appears in Tor's exit node list.
heuristics: `tor_check` only runs on page `1` and only when the lowercased query exactly matches a keyword.
It downloads `https://check.torproject.org/exit-addresses` and extracts `ExitAddress` lines via regex.
HTTP download failures produce an answer instead of throwing an error.

description: Collapse client IPs into configured IPv4 or IPv6 network buckets.
heuristics: `get_network()` uses `botdetection.ipv4_prefix` for IPv4 and `botdetection.ipv6_prefix` for IPv6.
The network is built with `ip_network(f'{real_ip}/{prefix}', strict=False)`.
Limiter bypasses link-local networks and the `/healthz` path before stricter checks.

description: Derive `REMOTE_ADDR` from trusted `X-Forwarded-For` proxy chains.
heuristics: `ProxyFix` validates and normalizes `REMOTE_ADDR`, `X-Real-IP`, and every `X-Forwarded-For` entry.
Trusted proxies are matched right-to-left until the first untrusted address is found.
If no usable address remains, `REMOTE_ADDR` falls back to `100::` as a black-hole address.

description: Allow or block client networks against configurable pass and block lists.
heuristics: Limiter checks `pass_ip` before `block_ip`, so passlist matches always win.
`pass_ip` can include hardcoded `SEARXNG_ORG` networks when `pass_searxng_org` is true.
Invalid IP or CIDR entries in list config are logged and skipped instead of crashing.

description: Block search requests that look unlike real browser traffic.
heuristics: All requests run `http_user_agent` matching against a large known-bot regex.
`/search` also requires `Accept` to include `text/html`, `Accept-Language` to be non-empty, and `gzip` or `deflate` in `Accept-Encoding`.
Supported browsers on HTTPS must present valid `Sec-Fetch-Mode`, `Sec-Fetch-Site`, and `Sec-Fetch-Dest` values.

description: Enforce sliding-window limits per client network and API format.
heuristics: Non-HTML format requests increment `API_WINDOW` and block after `API_MAX=4` per hour.
Vanilla limits use `BURST_WINDOW=20s/BURST_MAX=15` and `LONG_WINDOW=600s/LONG_MAX=150`.
When `link_token` is enabled, suspicious requests use much lower burst and long thresholds.

description: Issue rotating CSS tokens and track browser pings per network fingerprint.
heuristics: `get_token()` creates a random 16-char lowercase-alnum token with a 600-second TTL in Valkey.
Without Valkey, `get_token()` returns the fixed fallback token `12345678`.
Ping keys hash `network.compressed` plus `Accept-Language` and `User-Agent` and live for `3600` seconds.

description: Return HTML, JSON, CSV, or RSS search responses from one route.
heuristics: `/search` rejects formats outside `OUTPUT_FORMATS` or those disabled in `settings['search']['formats']`.
Valid external bangs redirect before any template rendering or serialization.
HTML rendering highlights result title/content text and marks template group boundaries with `open_group/close_group`.

description: Serve query suggestions from answerers, modifiers, and remote backends.
heuristics: The autocompleter first parses `RawTextQuery` with disabled engines and collects `Answer` objects from answerers.
Backend autocomplete only runs when there are no inline autocomplete tokens and the stripped prefix is non-empty.
`XMLHttpRequest` callers get a flat JSON list, while browser omnibox callers get `[prefix, results]`.

description: Read, encode, and save preferences through cookies and shareable blobs.
heuristics: Preference blobs are zlib-compressed urlencoded settings, then base64url-encoded.
POST form parsing injects missing `BooleanSetting` fields as `False` before saving.
`preferences_preview_only=true` shows shared prefs without writing cookies.

description: Proxy remote images through a signed, size-limited streaming endpoint.
heuristics: `/image_proxy` requires both a `url` parameter and a valid HMAC over that URL.
Responses larger than `5 MiB` by `Content-Length` or with non-image content types are rejected.
The proxy uses a dedicated network context and forwards only a small header subset to the client.

description: Publish instance capabilities, engines, plugins, and limits as JSON.
heuristics: `/config` only includes engines that pass `preferences.validate_token(engine)`.
Each engine entry exposes categories, shortcut, language/region traits, paging, safesearch, time range, and timeout.
The payload also includes limiter flags, DOI resolvers, branding URLs, and `public_instance`.

description: Registers search, results, auth, RAG, and admin routes under both app prefixes.
heuristics: `swirl/urls.py` registers the same viewsets for normal routes and `/sapi/*` aliases on the DRF router.
`swirl_server/urls.py` mounts the app at both `/swirl/` and `/api/swirl/` and redirects `/` to `/galaxy/`.
Explicit paths cover login, logout, OIDC auth, Microsoft callback, detail RAG, and test-only QueryTransform endpoints.

description: Rejects tokenless `/sapi/` calls and binds the request user from DRF tokens.
heuristics: `TokenMiddleware` skips branding, login, OIDC, and non-`/sapi/` paths before enforcing auth.
It requires an `Authorization` header, splits out the bearer token, and loads `request.user` from `rest_framework.authtoken.Token`.
Missing or unknown tokens return `HttpResponseForbidden()` immediately.

description: Applies optional timeouts to RAG searches and returns HTTP 504 on overrun.
heuristics: `TimeoutMiddleware` only activates when `rag_timeout` is present and the request is a RAG endpoint or a search with `rag`/`do_rag=true`.
Accepted timeout values are integers from 1 to 180 seconds; invalid or out-of-range values return `HttpResponseBadRequest`.
It wraps the request with `func_timeout()` and returns `HttpResponse('Rag timed out', status=504)` on `FunctionTimedOut`.

description: Shapes API payloads for searches, results, providers, auth, and RAG responses.
heuristics: `SearchProviderNoCredentialsSerializer` omits `credentials` and `eval_credentials` while the full serializer includes them.
`ResultSerializer` exposes `result_processor_json_feedback`, processor lists, messages, and `json_results`.
`DetailSearchRagSerializer` is a minimal serializer with a single required `message` field.

description: Creates Search rows from GET or POST inputs with owner, tags, and status.
heuristics: `SearchViewSet.list()` creates a `Search` from `?q=` or `?qs=` with `owner=self.request.user`, provider list, and optional tags.
GET `?q=` runs the search and redirects to `/swirl/results`, while `?qs=` runs it and returns mixed results directly.
`SearchViewSet.create()` persists POSTed searches, then immediately runs `run_search()` when the caller has provider permissions.

description: Chooses active providers by explicit IDs, names, defaults, and tag matches.
heuristics: `get_query_selected_provider_list()` matches `searchprovider_list` entries against provider id, provider name, or provider tags.
Without an explicit list, `select_providers()` includes active default providers and also pulls in non-default providers whose tags match query tags.
If a leading tag matches nothing, `select_providers()` falls back to the default provider set instead of returning none.

description: Extracts leading and embedded `tag:value` selectors from the user query.
heuristics: The leading provider selector comes from the first token before `:` in `search.query_string.strip().split()[0]`.
Embedded tags are any query tokens containing `:`; trailing-colon tokens keep only the tag name, others keep the prefix before `:`.
The parsed tag list feeds both provider selection and adaptive query rewriting later in the pipeline.

description: Runs pre-query processors or saved transforms in order and stores rewrites.
heuristics: If `search.pre_query_processors` is empty, `query_string_processed` is copied directly from `query_string`.
Each processor is resolved through `get_pre_query_processor_or_transform()`, so missing Python classes can fall back to `QueryTransform` records.
A rewrite only appends a search message when the returned query differs from the previous `query_temp`.

description: Fans federation out as one Celery task per provider and joins with a timeout.
heuristics: `search()` builds a Celery `group()` of `federate_task.s(search.id, provider.id, provider.connector, update, session, request_id)`.
When running inside a Celery task it wraps the join in `allow_join_result()`; otherwise it calls `results.get()` directly.
Both join paths use `interval=0.05` and `timeout=settings.SWIRL_TIMEOUT`.

description: Runs whole-search post processors to rescore, dedupe, redact, or RAG-enrich results.
heuristics: If `search.post_result_processors` is present, the search status becomes `POST_RESULT_PROCESSING` for the duration of the loop.
Negative processor return values are logged as deleted-result messages; non-negative values are logged as updated-result messages.
The code avoids appending the same message twice in a row by comparing it against `search.messages[-1:]`.

description: Converts in-flight search statuses to ready states and records elapsed time.
heuristics: `PARTIAL_RESULTS` becomes `PARTIAL_RESULTS_READY` or `PARTIAL_UPDATE_READY`; `FULL_RESULTS` becomes `FULL_RESULTS_READY` or `FULL_UPDATE_READY`.
`search.time` is stored as a one-decimal string built from `end_time - start_time`.
After status finalization, `run_processor_if_tag_in_request()` can invoke `RAGPostResultProcessor` when the request has a `rag` query key.

description: Builds rerun, filter, mixed-result, and paging URLs from the current request host.
heuristics: `search.result_url` includes `search_id` and the active `result_mixer` using `get_url_details(request)`.
`Mixer.__init__()` builds per-provider `filter_url` links and a search-level `rerun_url` in the `mix_wrapper` info block.
`Mixer.finalize()` adds `next_page` and `prev_page` links, preserving an on-the-fly mixer override when one was used.

description: Resolves provider connector names to concrete classes before federation starts.
heuristics: `alloc_connector()` simply returns `globals()[connector]` from `swirl/connectors/__init__.py`.
`federate_task()` instantiates the class as `alloc_connector(connector)(provider_id, search_id, update, request_id=request_id)`.
Disabled connectors like PostgreSQL remain commented out in the registry, so they are unavailable until imported there.

description: Substitutes `{url}` and mapped placeholders into provider query templates.
heuristics: `bind_query_mappings()` ignores mapping keys listed in `QUERY_MAPPING_VARIABLES`, such as `RESULT_INDEX` and `PAGE_INDEX`.
Credential mappings are appended to the mapping list unless `credentials` starts with `HTTP`.
A placeholder is only replaced when the bound template still contains the matching `{SWIRL_KEY}` token.

description: Rejects blank or half-bound provider queries before any external call is made.
heuristics: Base `Connector.validate_query()` fails when `query_to_provider` is the empty string.
`Requests.validate_query()` returns false if `{` or `}` remain in the bound request string.
`DBConnector.validate_query()` separately checks both `count_query` and `query_to_provider` and warns when braces remain after binding.

description: Runs provider result processors in order, with skip tags and feedback carryover.
heuristics: Search tags of the form `SW_RESULT_PROCESSOR_SKIP:ProcessorName` cause `Connector.process_results()` to skip that processor.
If the last returned item contains `result_processor_feedback`, it is popped off and merged into `self.result_processor_json_feedback` for later stages.
After processing, `retrieved` is reset to `len(self.processed_results)` so deletions affect counts.

description: Creates or updates `Result` rows, appending update batches as unread items.
heuristics: Update mode requires exactly one existing `Result` for the search/provider pair; otherwise it sets an error status and aborts.
Updated items are marked with `new=True`, appended to the existing `json_results`, and the existing `found` count is kept as the max of old/new.
Fresh result creation stores the combined pre-query and provider query processor list, provider result processors, and `result_processor_json_feedback`.

description: Executes paged HTTP GET/POST searches with multiple auth and payload formats.
heuristics: When `PAGE` mapping exists and `results_per_query > 10`, the connector pages in steps of 10 using `RESULT_INDEX`, `RESULT_ZERO_INDEX`, or `PAGE_INDEX`.
Auth handling supports `HTTPBasicAuth`-style strings, `bearer=...`, and `X-Api-Key=...` credentials.
Response parsing handles XML via `xmltodict`, list-of-lists tables, and ThoughtSpot `contents` blocks before mapping results.

description: Pulls FOUND, RETRIEVED, RESULTS, or nested RESULT objects via JSONPath.
heuristics: Response control mappings are evaluated as `$.<mapped_path>` with `jsonpath_ng.parse()`.
If `RESULTS` is missing but the payload is already a top-level list or dict, the connector treats that payload as the result set.
A `RESULT` control mapping drills one level deeper into each mapped result and appends the extracted match objects individually.

description: Runs SQL count and data queries against a local SQLite file source.
heuristics: `Sqlite3.execute_search()` requires `provider.url` to exist as a filesystem path and enables `sqlite3.Row` mode.
It executes `self.count_query` first and short-circuits to READY with zero counts when the count is 0.
A special case suppresses the zero-count exit when the count query text contains `json`.

description: Queries BigQuery using provider-supplied service-account credentials.
heuristics: `BigQuery.execute_search()` exports `provider.credentials` as `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` before creating the client.
The total hit count comes from the first cell of the count-query result: `int(list_results[0][0])`.
Returned column names are inferred from `dict(self.response[0]).keys()` after the main query finishes.

description: Runs count and data SQL against PostgreSQL using a colon-delimited DSN.
heuristics: `provider.url` must split into exactly 5 parts: host, port, database, user, and password.
The connector executes `self.count_query` first and exits READY with zero counts when no rows match.
Main-query column names are read from `cursor.description` and stored for later row serialization.

description: Builds Mongo `$text` searches, then sorts and limits the matching documents.
heuristics: Unless `MATCH_ANY` appears in search tags or provider query mappings, non-negated terms are wrapped in double quotes before binding into `$text.$search`.
`provider.query_template_json['$text']['$search']` must contain `{query_string}` or the connector errors out.
Date sorting uses `collection.find(...).sort(self.query_mappings['sort_by_date'], -1).limit(results_per_query)`.

description: Uses account, warehouse, and database settings to query Snowflake sources.
heuristics: Credentials are parsed as `username:password:database:warehouse`, while `provider.url` supplies the Snowflake account.
The connector explicitly runs `USE WAREHOUSE ...` and `USE DATABASE ...` before executing either query.
`retrieved` is set to `provider.results_per_query` after the query, not to the actual fetched row count.

description: Executes Oracle SQL and converts cursor rows into JSON-serializable records.
heuristics: Credentials are parsed as `username:password` and `provider.url` is treated as the Oracle DSN.
Result rows are zipped to lowercased column names from `cursor.description`.
The connector serializes the rows to JSON in `self.results` and sets `self.response = None` so base DB normalization is skipped.

description: Parses Elastic query strings for index, body, and size before searching.
heuristics: `Elastic.construct_query()` builds a Python-like string containing `index='...'`, `query={...}`, and `size=n`.
`execute_search()` extracts the index with regex `index='([^']+)'` and parses the query dict with `ast.literal_eval()`.
Date sorting appends `sort=[{sort_field: 'desc'}]` when `search.sort` is `date` and `sort_by_date` is mapped.

description: Loads OpenSearch JSON bodies, injects sort, and searches the mapped index.
heuristics: `OpenSearch.construct_query()` JSON-decodes the bound query string into a dict before execution.
Date sorting injects a `sort` array into the request body using `query_mappings['sort_by_date']`.
Searches always target `query_mappings['index_name']`, and credentialed mode enables SSL with optional CA certs.

description: Posts Microsoft Graph search requests with bearer auth and entity defaults.
heuristics: M365 connectors force `provider.credentials` to `bearer=<session token>` and validate auth with `Microsoft.is_authenticated(session)`.
Graph search bodies are posted to `https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/search/query` with `'(query) AND (NOT contenttype:folder)'`.
Date sort uses `query_mappings['DATE_SORT']` when present, otherwise `createdDateTime desc`.

description: Embeds provider queries with a transformer model tag and mean-pooled vectors.
heuristics: `VectorDBConnector` reads the embedding model name from the provider tag `model`.
It loads `AutoTokenizer` and `AutoModel` for that model on every query execution.
The query vector is `outputs.last_hidden_state.mean(dim=1)[0].numpy().tolist()`.

description: Searches Qdrant with the query vector and returns payloads plus similarity.
heuristics: `provider.url` is split once on `-` into the Qdrant base URL and collection name.
The search uses `with_payload=True` and `with_vectors=False` and limits to `results_per_query`.
Each returned point contributes its payload plus stringified `id` and `similarity=point.score`.

description: Queries Pinecone with vector search and emits metadata with IDs and scores.
heuristics: The connector runs `pc.Index(index_name).query(vector=..., top_k=results_per_query, include_metadata=True, include_values=False)`.
Every kept match copies `match['metadata']`, adds `id`, and stores the score as `similarity`.
If `matches` is missing or empty, it sets `found=0`, `retrieved=0`, and leaves the result READY.

description: Sends a provider query to an LLM and stores the reply as one synthetic result.
heuristics: `GenAI.execute_search()` passes `provider.credentials` as an explicit OpenAI key override when present.
If the query does not end with `?`, it formats the prompt with `query_mappings['PROMPT']`; otherwise it uses the raw query.
The connector stores a single result whose `title` is the original query and whose payload includes the chosen model name.

description: Chooses Azure OpenAI first when fully configured, otherwise falls back to OpenAI.
heuristics: An explicit key always forces the `OPENAI` provider path, even if Azure is globally configured.
Azure is selected only when `AZURE_OPENAI_KEY`, `AZURE_MODEL`, and `AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT` are all present.
For OpenAI, `get_model()` requires a usage-specific `SWIRL_REWRITE_MODEL`, `SWIRL_QUERY_MODEL`, or `SWIRL_RAG_MODEL`.

description: Normalizes query text by stripping tags and unsupported punctuation.
heuristics: `GenericQueryProcessor` returns `clean_string(self.query_string).strip()`.
`clean_string()` removes HTML tags, scripts, and styles before scanning characters.
Only letters, numbers, and a small punctuation allowlist survive; `!`, `;`, `/`, `_`, `|`, and `.` become spaces.

description: Rewrites tagged queries to keep only values for tags the provider owns.
heuristics: `AdaptiveQueryProcessor` builds `dict_tags` only for tags that match the provider's tag set, case-insensitively.
If the adapted tagged query does not contain `not`, it returns only the tagged values and drops the rest of the query.
Negation is rewritten with `NOT` or a prefix character from provider `query_mappings` when those features are supported.

description: Leaves unknown tag terms in place so tagless providers still see the user intent.
heuristics: When a query tag is not in the provider's canonical tag set, the original `tag:value` token is pushed into `query_wot_list`.
If a provider has no tags at all, `AdaptiveQueryProcessor` falls back to the query with tags removed down to plain terms.
If a provider supports neither `NOT` nor `NOT_CHAR`, negated terms are stripped and the positive terms are preserved.

description: Runs TextBlob spelling correction when spellcheck processing is enabled.
heuristics: `SpellcheckQueryProcessor()` returns the original query unchanged when its length is 0.
Otherwise it computes `TextBlob(query_string).correct()` and formats the result as a plain string.
The function only catches `NameError` and `TypeError` before returning the corrected query string.

description: Uses Presidio to remove detected PII from a query before provider search.
heuristics: `remove_pii()` strips HTML tags before calling `AnalyzerEngine.analyze(text=..., language='en')`.
The default anonymizer operator is `OperatorConfig('redact')`, which removes the detected PII text entirely.
`RemovePIIQueryProcessor.process()` simply returns `remove_pii(self.query_string)`.

description: Uses chat completions plus tag-driven prompts and guides to rewrite queries.
heuristics: Prompt, system guide, and filtering behavior come from tags `CHAT_QUERY_REWRITE_PROMPT`, `CHAT_QUERY_REWRITE_GUIDE`, and `CHAT_QUERY_DO_FILTER`.
If a prompt omits `{query_string}`, the processor appends it automatically, usually after a colon.
Filtered mode rejects very long replies and can convert numbered or blank-line answer lists into `OR` queries.

description: Loads CSV-backed rewrite or synonym transforms from `QueryTransform` records.
heuristics: When processor allocation fails, the code splits the processor string as `name.type` and looks up `QueryTransform.objects.get(name=name, qrx_type=type)`.
`TransformQueryProcessorFactory` supports `rewrite`, `synonym`, and `bag` transform types.
Transform configs are parsed as CSV, skipping blank lines and lines whose first cell starts with `#`.

description: Builds the canonical Swirl result object with rank, score, highlights, and payload.
heuristics: `create_result_dictionary()` predefines `swirl_rank`, `swirl_score`, `searchprovider_rank`, date fields, author, hit highlights, and `payload`.
Generic and mapping processors stamp each result with `date_retrieved = str(datetime.now())`.
Fallback title logic uses `url` first, then `author`, when no explicit title was mapped.

description: Maps provider JSON fields into Swirl result fields, templates, and payload sidecars.
heuristics: `MappingResultProcessor` supports `swirl_key=source_key`, multi-source fallbacks with `|`, and quoted templates like `'https://...{field}'`.
Each source field is resolved with JSONPath, and `ResultMapConverter` can apply `sw_btcconvert(...)` or `sw_urlencode(...)` directives.
If a Swirl field is already filled, extra mapped values are pushed into payload keys like `title_sourceField` instead of overwriting it.

description: Profiles payload keys to promote likely title, body, URL, and date fields.
heuristics: Title/body candidates come from payload population and length profiling, with tag overrides like `MAX_TITLE_LEN` and `TITLE_MEDIAN_LEN`.
Before using profile guesses, the processor copies exact or prefix/suffix payload-name matches into empty top-level fields.
When `DATASET` appears in `result_mappings`, all payload rows are collapsed into one item with `payload.dataset`.

description: Backfills unknown publish dates by regexing common date formats from result bodies.
heuristics: Only items whose `date_published` is exactly `unknown` are inspected by `DateFinderResultProcessor`.
The regex accepts `MM/DD/YYYY`, `MM.DD.YYYY`, `MM-DD-YYYY`, and short or full month-name forms like `Jan 2, 2024`.
Parsed dates are normalized to `%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S`.

description: Trims oversized title/body text and preserves the full value in payload.
heuristics: The max length comes from a provider tag `max_length`; otherwise it falls back to `SWIRL_MAX_FIELD_LEN`.
The original long value is saved to `payload[field + '_full']` before truncation.
If any query word appears in the field, truncation starts at the first matching word; otherwise the processor takes the first `max_length-3` characters.

description: Normalizes noisy result text by collapsing odd punctuation runs.
heuristics: `CleanTextResultProcessor` only edits the `title` and `body` fields.
Its regex converts three or more dots to `...` and two or more hyphens to `--`.
All other disallowed characters are replaced with spaces.

description: Drops results whose title does not contain all cleaned query terms.
heuristics: Both the query string and the title are run through `remove_tags()` and `remove_non_alphanumeric()` before token matching.
The processor keeps a result only when `match_all()` finds every cleaned query token in the title token stream.
Results with no title are dropped and counted as deletions.

description: Uses Presidio to replace PII in result titles, bodies, and string payload fields.
heuristics: `RedactPIIResultProcessor` and `RedactPIIPostResultProcessor` walk `title`, `body`, and only string-valued payload entries.
Redaction mode switches Presidio to `OperatorConfig('replace')`, then converts angle brackets like `<PERSON>` to `[PERSON]`.
When a query string is supplied, the anonymized text is re-highlighted with `highlight_list()`.

description: Parses queries into positive, NOT, stemmed, and n-gram targets for scoring.
heuristics: `parse_query()` removes quotes, `AND`, `OR`, and punctuation, then parses negation from `NOT` or leading `-` tokens.
Provider query terms collected from earlier highlight feedback are appended to the live query token list.
For queries of length 3 or more, it builds full-phrase, bigram, and unigram target lists on both raw and stemmed terms.

description: Scores result fields with stemming, term windows, spaCy similarity, and highlights.
heuristics: Field weights default to `title=1.5`, `body=1.0`, and `author=1.0` from `SWIRL_RELEVANCY_CONFIG`.
Zero-vector cases use fallback scores like `0.3 + 1/3`, `0.31 + 1/3`, and `0.32 + 1/3` instead of dropping the match.
Pass 1 stores `dict_score`, `dict_len`, hit positions, and feedback about provider query terms and result field lengths.

description: Recomputes cross-provider scores using length medians and provider-rank boosts.
heuristics: `CosineRelevancyPostResultProcessor` merges `dict_result_lens` and `list_query_lens` feedback from every provider result set before scoring.
Pass 2 multiplies similarity by field weight, `len(key)^2`, result-length adjustment, query-length adjustment, and `1 + 1/sqrt(searchprovider_rank)`.
Items marked with `_relevancy_model = 'FILE_SYSTEM'` can reuse title weight for body matches when the title has no hits.

description: Removes results whose `swirl_score` does not clear the minimum threshold.
heuristics: `DropIrrelevantPostResultProcessor` keeps an item only when `item['swirl_score'] > settings.MIN_SWIRL_SCORE`.
Results missing `swirl_score` are also dropped.
The processor returns a negative count equal to the number of removed results.

description: Deduplicates results inside one provider using a configured grouping field.
heuristics: `DedupeByFieldResultProcessor` is a no-op unless `provider.result_grouping_field` is set.
Deduping checks both the top-level record and `payload[field]` via `_get_field_value_top_level_or_payload()`.
Duplicates are skipped in order and reported as `-dupe_count`.

description: Deduplicates across providers by the global Swirl dedupe field, usually URL.
heuristics: `DedupeByFieldPostResultProcessor` uses one shared `dedupe_key_dict` across all provider result rows for the search.
The grouping field is `SWIRL_DEDUPE_FIELD`, which defaults to `url`.
Each `Result` row is saved after its `json_results` list has been filtered.

description: Drops near-duplicate results by spaCy similarity over title and body content.
heuristics: `DedupeBySimilarityPostResultProcessor` concatenates the configured fields in `SWIRL_DEDUPE_SIMILARITY_FIELDS`, defaulting to `title` and `body`.
Each candidate is compared against previously kept documents with `nlp(content).similarity(n)`.
Any similarity above `SWIRL_DEDUPE_SIMILARITY_MINIMUM`, default `0.95`, is treated as a duplicate.

description: Orders mixed results by score, then date, then provider rank.
heuristics: `RelevancyMixer` applies nested stable sorts: `searchprovider_rank` ascending, `date_published` descending, then `swirl_score` descending.
`Mixer.finalize()` removes `explain` and `swirl_score` unless explain mode is enabled.
The final mix wrapper includes provider info, search info, and total retrieved/found counts.

description: Shows only unread update batches and can clear their `new` markers.
heuristics: `RelevancyNewItemsMixer` filters `self.all_results` down to items carrying a `new` key.
When `mark_all_read` is true, it deletes the `new` key from stored `json_results` and saves each changed `Result`.
It appends messages describing either zero new results or how many old results were hidden.

description: Hides undated items and sorts the remaining mixed results newest-first.
heuristics: `DateMixer` drops any item whose `date_published` is exactly `unknown`.
It reduces `retrieved_total` by the number of hidden unknown-date items.
Remaining items are sorted descending by `date_published`.

description: Builds a date-sorted unread view and can clear `new` flags after reading.
heuristics: `DateNewItemsMixer` clears `new` flags exactly like the relevancy variant when `mark_all_read` is requested.
It reports how many unknown-date items were hidden before sorting by `date_published`.
The implementation computes `dated_results` from `all_results`, not from `new_results`, so its unread filter is only partially applied.

description: Interleaves one result at a time from each ranked provider.
heuristics: `RoundRobinMixer` sets `stack = 1` and reuses `StackNMixer.order()`.
Providers are ranked by the score of each provider's top result before interleaving begins.
The mixer stops once it fills the requested page budget or all provider lists are exhausted.

description: Stacks top N results per provider based on each provider's best hit.
heuristics: `StackNMixer` groups all ranked results by `searchprovider` after sorting by score and provider rank.
When `stack` is 0, it defaults to `results_requested / len(dict_ranked_providers)`.
The mixer takes `stack` results per provider per pass and warns when result lists are exhausted.

description: Builds page fetchers only when fetch config is explicitly enabled.
heuristics: `PageFetcherFactory` returns no fetcher unless the options include `cache` set to either `'true'` or `'false'`.
`PageFetcherOptions` merges default headers and timeout with configured values, removing keys whose override is the empty string.
Domain-specific timeout overrides are looked up by URL netloc inside the options object.

description: Fetches pages with GET or POST and only accepts successful HTTP responses.
heuristics: `PageFetcher._get_page()` uses `requests.get()` with configured headers and timeout and accepts only HTTP 200.
`post_page()` accepts HTTP 200, 201, 202, 204, and reset-content responses before wrapping the page.
Connection, timeout, and invalid-URL failures are logged and returned as `None` rather than raised.

description: Turns fetched HTML into cleaner readable text for later prompt packing.
heuristics: `DocumentWebPage.get_text_for_query()` runs the page through Readability `Document(summary())` before extracting text with BeautifulSoup.
The extracted text is normalized with `clean_string_keep_punct()` so punctuation useful to RAG is preserved.
`WebPage.html_to_text(skip_summary=True)` can bypass Readability and strip raw HTML directly when needed.

description: Chooses RAG inputs by explicit IDs or score, then ranks the shortlist.
heuristics: If `rag_items` are supplied, `RAGPostResultProcessor` keeps only items whose `swirl_id` matches the requested list.
Without explicit IDs, only items with `swirl_score > 50.0` are considered for RAG.
Candidates are sorted by `searchprovider_rank` ascending, `date_published` descending, and `swirl_score` descending, then truncated to `RAG_MAX_TO_CONSIDER`.

description: Packs supporting text into a token-budgeted RAG prompt and rejects weak chunks.
heuristics: `RagPrompt` rejects chunks shorter than `len(query_terms) + 5`, PDF-like file types, or chunks with no token overlap with the query.
Chunk token counts are measured with `tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model)` and trimmed until they fit the remaining token budget.
The final prompt always appends HTML-format and citation instructions in `_prompt_footer`.

description: Fetches candidate pages, falls back to summaries, asks the chat model, and stores a result.
heuristics: `page_fetcher_task` falls back to a cleaned `body : ...` summary when page fetch fails and `SWIRL_ALWAYS_FALL_BACK_TO_SUMMARY` is true.
If the assembled prompt is too short, `RAGPostResultProcessor` retries with concatenated result bodies before giving up.
Successful answers are stored as a `Result` with `searchprovider='ChatGPT'`, `rag_query_items`, and optional `result_block` from `SWIRL_DEFAULT_RESULT_BLOCK`.

description: Stores access, refresh, and expiry data in session and persistent token tables.
heuristics: `Authenticator.set_session_data()` writes provider-specific access, refresh, and expiry fields under `request.session['user']`.
`Microsoft.update_oauth_token_in_db()` uses `OauthToken.objects.get_or_create(owner=owner, defaults={...})` and updates existing rows in place.
`LoginView` issues reusable API tokens with `Token.objects.get_or_create(user=user)`.

description: Starts Microsoft auth-code flow for logged-in users and saves the auth state.
heuristics: Anonymous callers are redirected to `/swirl/api-auth/login?next=/swirl/authenticators.html` before any Microsoft flow is started.
`Microsoft.login()` calls `initiate_auth_code_flow()` with the fixed Graph scopes list and `MICROSOFT_REDIRECT_URI`.
The returned flow object is saved in `request.session['auth_flow']` before redirecting to `auth_uri`.

description: Exchanges the callback code for tokens, hydrates session state, and persists OAuth tokens.
heuristics: `Microsoft.callback()` resolves the code with `acquire_token_by_auth_code_flow(flow, request.GET)` after popping `auth_flow` from session.
It fetches the signed-in user profile from `https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me` using the returned access token.
Session expiry is stored as `int(now.timestamp()) + result['expires_in']`, and both access and refresh tokens are persisted to `OauthToken`.

description: Refreshes Microsoft access tokens when sessions or subscriber runs need one.
heuristics: `Microsoft.update_token()` refreshes from the session refresh token and rewrites both session state and the persistent `OauthToken` row.
`subscriber._get_session_for_oauth_providers()` decodes JWT `exp` without signature verification to decide whether a stored token is expired.
`update_access_from_refresh_token()` persists the newly returned access and refresh tokens back to the owner's `OauthToken` record.

description: Re-executes subscribed searches on the beat schedule and disables failing jobs.
heuristics: `subscriber()` iterates `Search.objects.filter(subscribe=True)` on every scheduled run.
Before rerunning, it refreshes OAuth-backed provider sessions and sets `search.status = 'UPDATE_SEARCH'`.
Permission errors or failed updates append timestamped messages and flip `search.subscribe` to `False`.

description: Rewrites a follow-up into a standalone query when prior context matters.
heuristics: standaloneFollowUp must be self-contained and concise
The prompt resolves pronouns from chat history, like turning 'How do they work' into 'How do cars work?'
The rewritten follow-up is injected into the researcher conversation block

description: Decides when Vane can skip research and answer from knowledge or widgets alone.
heuristics: skipSearch is true for greetings, writing tasks, general knowledge, or widget-only answers
The prompt says to set skipSearch=false whenever the query is uncertain or ambiguous
SearchAgent only creates a Researcher when skipSearch is false

description: Flags turns that should search uploaded files when the user refers to documents.
heuristics: personalSearch is true when the user explicitly references uploaded documents
The prompt says if unsure, set personalSearch=false and skipSearch=false
uploads_search becomes available whenever fileIds exist, with personalSearch as the main intent signal

description: Flags turns that should use scholarly sources when papers or studies are requested.
heuristics: academicSearch is true for requests for research papers, academic articles, studies, or citations
academic_search only enables when source 'academic' is selected and skipSearch is false
Academic lookup uses arxiv, google scholar, and pubmed engines

description: Flags turns that should use discussion sources for opinions, reviews, or advice.
heuristics: discussionSearch is true for opinions, reviews, personal experiences, and common-issue queries
social_search only enables when source 'discussions' is selected and skipSearch is false
Discussion lookup runs through SearXNG with engines=['reddit']

description: Flags weather requests so the app can show a forecast widget when it fits.
heuristics: showWeatherWidget is true for current weather or forecast questions about a place
The prompt treats 'Show me the weather' as current-location weather
If the weather widget fully answers the turn, skipSearch should also be true

description: Flags stock price requests so the app can show a market widget when it fits.
heuristics: showStockWidget is true for current stock prices or simple stock lookups
The prompt explicitly excludes broader market analysis or news from stock widget use
If the stock widget fully answers the turn, skipSearch should also be true

description: Flags math or conversion turns so the app can answer with a calculation widget.
heuristics: showCalculationWidget is true for math expressions, conversions, or computation tasks
The prompt requires skipSearch=true when the calculation widget can fully answer
The extracted expression later has to be valid for MathJS

description: Writes the next research step before tools run when non-speed modes allow planning.
heuristics: __reasoning_preamble is enabled only when mode is not speed
Balanced and quality prompts require it before every tool call, including done
The plan must open with a short intent phrase and avoid naming tools

description: Runs the LLM tool loop until enough evidence is gathered or the agent stops.
heuristics: Researcher streams tool calls, merges partial tool arguments, then executes them
Assistant tool calls and tool outputs are appended back into agentMessageHistory
The loop stops when no tool calls arrive or the last tool call is done

description: Caps research depth by mode so the tool loop cannot wander indefinitely.
heuristics: speed mode sets maxIteration=2
balanced mode sets maxIteration=6
quality mode sets maxIteration=25

description: Builds the active tool list from enabled actions before each research turn.
heuristics: ActionRegistry only exposes actions whose enabled(config) returns true
Tool definitions include name, description, and a Zod-derived schema
Registered actions include web, academic, social, scrape_url, uploads, reasoning, and done

description: Executes tool calls and feeds their results back into the research conversation.
heuristics: executeAll runs tool calls in Promise.all
Each tool result is pushed back as a tool message with the matching call id
Unknown action names throw 'Action with name ... not found'

description: Streams planning and retrieval progress as live research substeps in the UI.
heuristics: Research always starts by emitting a research block with subSteps=[]
Tools append reasoning, searching, reading, or upload substeps and patch them live
A researchComplete event is emitted before final answer synthesis starts

description: Builds the final cited answer prompt after research and widgets have finished.
heuristics: Search findings are wrapped in <search_results> and marked citable
Widget outputs are wrapped separately and explicitly marked do not cite
Writer prompt also receives systemInstructions and optimization mode

description: Builds and sends SearXNG queries when Vane needs web-style search results.
heuristics: Requests target `${searxngURL}/search?format=json` and always append q
Array options like engines are joined with commas in the query string
Requests abort after 10 seconds and raise 'SearXNG search timed out'

description: Runs general web lookup through SearXNG when web research is enabled.
heuristics: web_search truncates to at most 3 queries per call
It only enables when sources include web and skipSearch is false
Each query result is emitted into the research block as search_results

description: Runs scholarly lookup when academic search is requested and enabled.
heuristics: academic_search truncates to at most 3 queries per call
It only enables when source academic is on and academicSearch=true
Queries run through SearXNG with arxiv, google scholar, and pubmed

description: Runs discussion lookup when community opinions or reviews are needed.
heuristics: social_search truncates to at most 3 queries per call
It only enables when source discussions is on and discussionSearch=true
Queries run through SearXNG with engines=['reddit']

description: Fetches page contents when the user explicitly asks about specific URLs.
heuristics: scrape_url accepts only the first 3 URLs
Its description says never call it unless the user explicitly asked about those pages
Fetched pages are added as reading substeps before being returned

description: Converts fetched HTML into markdown so page content can enter the research loop.
heuristics: scrape_url converts fetched HTML with Turndown before returning chunks
Chunk metadata keeps the page title from the HTML <title> tag when present

description: Merges duplicate hits so repeated URLs do not bloat the final evidence set.
heuristics: Researcher merges results that share the same metadata.url
Duplicate content is appended to the first seen result instead of emitted twice
uploads_search applies the same URL-based merge before returning results

description: Extracts a weather location or coordinates when the turn should show weather.
