heuristics: The extractor returns either location text or lat/lon, never both
The prompt expands short forms like NYC to New York City
If no valid place is found, notPresent=true and the widget is skipped

description: Turns a named place into coordinates before fetching weather data.
heuristics: Named places are geocoded with Nominatim search and limit=1
Requests send User-Agent: Vane and Content-Type: application/json
Missing matches throw 'Could not find coordinates for location'

description: Turns coordinates into a readable place name for weather widget display.
heuristics: Coordinate queries call Nominatim /reverse with lat, lon, and format=json
display_name from reverse geocoding becomes the widget location label

description: Fetches current, hourly, and daily weather when the widget has a location.
heuristics: Weather calls request current, hourly, and daily fields from Open-Meteo
Hourly results are sliced to the first 24 entries and daily results cover 7 days
Forecast requests set timezone=auto

description: Maps raw weather codes into user-facing labels, icons, and units.
heuristics: The home weather route maps Open-Meteo codes to condition strings and SVG icon names
Icons switch between day and night variants using current.is_day
Imperial mode appends fahrenheit and mph query params

description: Extracts a base stock and optional comparison names for the stock widget.
heuristics: The extractor returns one base name plus up to 3 comparisonNames
Names or tickers are both allowed in the prompt
notPresent=true skips stock lookup entirely

description: Fetches the live quote once the stock widget resolves a ticker.
heuristics: The widget resolves user input through yf.search before calling yf.quote
It uses the first search quote symbol as the ticker
If no quote is found, the widget returns an error payload instead of throwing

description: Fetches multiple chart windows so the stock widget can render price history.
heuristics: Charts are fetched for 1D, 5D, 1M, 3M, 6M, 1Y, and MAX
Intraday windows use 5m or 15m intervals; longer windows use 1d or 1wk
Failed chart calls are caught and stored as null

description: Builds comparison series when the user asks to compare several stocks.
heuristics: comparisonNames are sliced to at most 3 symbols
Each comparison ticker gets its own quote and the same 7 chart ranges
Comparison data is returned beside the base stock for overlay charts

description: Extracts a MathJS-ready expression when the turn is really a calculation.
heuristics: The LLM must return a MathJS-compatible expression string
The prompt says invalid or unclear expressions should set notPresent=true

description: Evaluates the extracted math expression and emits a calculation widget.
heuristics: The widget evaluates the expression with mathjs evaluate()
The result and original expression are returned as a calculation_result widget

description: Stores uploaded files and prepares them for later semantic search.
heuristics: POST /api/uploads expects files plus embedding_model_key and provider_id in form-data
Each upload gets a random file id and a randomized stored filename
The response returns fileName, fileExtension, and fileId

description: Rejects uploads outside the small set of document formats Vane can process.
heuristics: Only application/pdf, DOCX, and text/plain are accepted
Unsupported MIME types throw before files are written

description: Reads plain text uploads into chunkable content for semantic indexing.
heuristics: Plain text files are read with fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')
Text is split into 512-token chunks with 128-token overlap before embedding

description: Parses PDF uploads into text so they can be embedded and searched later.
heuristics: PDF extraction uses pdf-parse with CanvasFactory
Only the parsed text field is chunked and embedded

description: Parses DOCX uploads into text so they can be embedded and searched later.
heuristics: DOCX extraction uses officeParser.parseOfficeAsync on the file buffer
Parsed text is chunked and embedded like other formats

description: Splits long document text into overlapping token-bounded chunks for embeddings.
heuristics: splitText breaks on sentence, newline, punctuation, and list boundaries via a regex
Chunks are capped at 512 tokens by default
Chunks carry overlap from earlier segments up to 64 or 128 tokens

description: Embeds document chunks with the chosen embedding model during upload.
heuristics: UploadManager calls embeddingModel.embedText on every chunk batch
It throws if embedding count does not match chunk count
Embeddings are stored in a sibling .content.json file

description: Scores uploaded chunks against one or more semantic queries and returns the best.
heuristics: Query embeddings are compared with cosine similarity
Scores are rank-fused with score/(rank+1+60) across multiple queries
Results are deduped by hashed chunk identity before topK slicing

description: Validates provider config before a model provider becomes active.
heuristics: Every provider is instantiated through createProviderInstance(), which calls parseAndValidate
OpenAI requires apiKey and baseURL; Ollama, LM Studio, and Lemonade require baseURL
Invalid configs throw before the provider is activated

description: Bootstraps model providers from env-backed settings on startup.
heuristics: ConfigManager scans provider UI fields for env-backed values on startup
A provider is auto-added only if all required env fields are present
Provider hashes prevent the same env config from being inserted twice

description: Discovers provider model lists so the UI and APIs can pick valid models.
heuristics: OpenAI returns built-in defaults only for the official https://api.openai.com/v1 base URL
Ollama hits /api/tags; LM Studio and Lemonade hit /models
Anthropic, Groq, and Gemini fetch live model lists from their remote APIs

description: Adds a configured model provider when the user connects a new backend.
heuristics: POST /api/providers requires type, name, and config
ModelRegistry.addProvider persists config first, then fetches the provider model list
The response returns stored config plus discovered chat and embedding models

description: Updates a provider config and refreshes its resolved model list.
heuristics: PATCH /api/providers/[id] requires id, name, and config
The registry rebuilds the provider instance after saving
Updated provider responses include refreshed chat and embedding models

description: Removes a connected provider when the user deletes that backend.
heuristics: DELETE /api/providers/[id] removes the provider from config and activeProviders
Missing ids are rejected with 400 before deletion

description: Adds a custom chat or embedding model key to an existing provider.
heuristics: POST /api/providers/[id]/models requires key, name, and type
Custom models are appended to provider.chatModels or provider.embeddingModels
The API strips any incoming model.type before persisting

description: Removes a custom chat or embedding model from an existing provider.
heuristics: DELETE /api/providers/[id]/models requires key and type
Removal filters provider models by model.key

description: Loads concrete chat and embedding model instances for a request.
heuristics: Chat and embedding models are loaded in parallel with Promise.all
The registry refuses keys not present in the provider's current model list
Providers return concrete BaseLLM or BaseEmbedding wrappers like OpenAILLM

description: Validates chat request bodies before any model or search work begins.
heuristics: Chat POST bodies are validated with Zod before model loading
optimizationMode must be speed, balanced, or quality
messageId, chatId, and message.content all require non-empty strings

description: Normalizes stored tuple history into chat-role messages for model calls.
heuristics: Tuple history ['human', text] becomes {role:'user', content:text}
Any other tuple role becomes {role:'assistant', content:text}
loadMessages rebuilds history from saved queries and persisted text blocks

description: Creates a chat row the first time a conversation id is used.
heuristics: ensureChatExists inserts only when the chat id is missing
New chats store title=query, selected sources, and attached file metadata

description: Persists message lifecycle state so answers can be resumed or replayed.
heuristics: SearchAgent inserts new messages with status='answering' and empty responseBlocks
Rewrites delete later rows in the same chat before reusing the message id
On completion, the final block list is stored and status becomes completed

description: Streams new blocks and patches so answers update live in the client.
heuristics: SessionManager emits 'block' for new blocks and 'updateBlock' for RFC6902 patches
Chat and reconnect routes stream newline-delimited JSON over text/event-stream
Text answers append to one text block and patch /data as chunks arrive

description: Reconnects the client to an in-flight answer stream after reload or navigation.
heuristics: If the last saved message is still answering, the client POSTs /api/reconnect/{backendId}
The reconnect route attaches to the in-memory session and resumes live events

description: Replays prior session events so late subscribers can catch up instantly.
heuristics: SessionManager stores every emitted event in an in-memory events array
subscribe() replays all past events before attaching live listeners
Sessions expire after 30 minutes

description: Loads a saved chat and reconstructs its searchable conversation history.
heuristics: GET /api/chats/[id] returns the chat row plus all message rows
The client rebuilds assistant history by joining persisted text blocks
Attached files are rehydrated from chat.files into fileIds and file metadata

description: Deletes a stored chat and all of its persisted messages.
heuristics: DELETE /api/chats/[id] removes both the chat row and its messages
Unknown chat ids return 404

description: Replays a chosen turn from its original question while trimming later context.
heuristics: rewrite() trims visible messages and chat history back to the selected turn
The resent request reuses the original messageId and only earlier history
Server-side rewrite deletes later persisted rows in the same chat

description: Turns inline citation markers into clickable source links in rendered answers.
heuristics: The client replaces [1] or [1, 2] with <citation href=...> tags
Citation numbers map to source block order using source[number-1]
Speech text strips citation markers before text-to-speech

description: Renders live research reasoning and retrieval steps while an answer is forming.
heuristics: Research substeps render as Thinking, Searching, Reading, or upload scan states
The panel auto-expands for the last answering message and collapses after researchComplete
Only the first 4 result chips or cards are shown per step

description: Shows cited sources as cards with favicons, labels, and overflow dialog support.
heuristics: The UI shows the first 3 sources inline and opens a dialog for the rest
Web sources use Google favicon lookups while uploaded files use a file icon
Cards are numbered from source order for citation matching

description: Dispatches structured widget blocks into weather, stock, or calculation UI.
heuristics: Renderer handles weather, calculation_result, and stock widget types
Widget blocks are inserted before the answer markdown in MessageBox

description: Generates follow-up prompts after a sourced answer finishes streaming.
heuristics: Suggestions are generated only after messageEnd and only when sources exist
The prompt asks for 4-5 medium-length related suggestions
A suggestion block is added only if one is not already present

description: Automatically launches image and video searches after an answer when enabled.
heuristics: After messageEnd, the client auto-clicks the image and video search buttons when autoMediaSearch=true
The setting is read from localStorage and defaults to true

description: Rewrites a follow-up into a focused standalone query for image search.
heuristics: The image agent asks the LLM for a standalone image query
Few-shot examples shorten broad questions like 'What is a car?' to 'Car working'

description: Runs image retrieval through SearXNG once the query has been rewritten.
heuristics: Image search uses SearXNG with bing images and google images
Only results with img_src, url, and title are kept
The UI keeps the first 10 images

description: Rewrites a follow-up into a focused standalone query for video search.
heuristics: The video agent asks the LLM for a standalone YouTube-friendly query
Few-shots keep educational phrasing like 'How does a car work?' intact

description: Runs video retrieval through SearXNG once the query has been rewritten.
heuristics: Video search uses SearXNG with engines=['youtube']
Only results with thumbnail, url, title, and iframe_src are kept
The UI keeps the first 10 videos

description: Builds a topical discovery feed from fixed site lists and news queries.
heuristics: Discover topics map to fixed site lists and query seeds
Normal mode searches every site/query pair with Bing News, dedupes URLs, then shuffles
Preview mode samples one random site/query pair instead of the full matrix

description: Lists saved chats so the library view can show past searches and uploads.
heuristics: GET /api/chats returns all chats reversed so newest chats appear first
Library cards show relative time, selected source labels, and attached file counts

description: Defines the persisted config shape and the UI fields used to edit it.
heuristics: ConfigManager defines version, setupComplete, preferences, personalization, modelProviders, and search
UI config fields also declare scope, required, defaults, and optional env bindings

description: Loads config from disk and repairs missing or broken config files on startup.
heuristics: Config lives at DATA_DIR or cwd + /data/config.json
Missing config files are created from defaults on startup
Invalid JSON falls back to defaults and overwrites the broken file

description: Updates a specific config key when the user changes a setting.
heuristics: POST /api/config requires both key and value
updateConfig walks dotted paths and creates missing nested objects

description: Restores client-side preferences like theme, units, and auto media search.
heuristics: Client settings are read from localStorage for theme, units, autoMediaSearch, and widget toggles
Measurement unit falls back from measureUnit to measurementUnit and lowercases the result

description: Marks setup complete once the user has connected models and finished onboarding.
heuristics: POST /api/config/setup-complete flips setupComplete=true and saves config
SetupConfig reloads the page after the API call succeeds

description: Shows onboarding until setup is complete, then mounts the full app shell.
heuristics: RootLayout checks configManager.isSetupComplete() on each render
Incomplete installs show SetupWizard instead of ChatProvider and Sidebar
Completed installs mount the full app shell
